title: Dragon Age: The Veilguard
critic_response:
다양한 평가를 받은 Dragon Age: The Veilguard는 매력적인 세계와 핵심 메커니즘을 갖추고 있어 밤새도록 플레이어를 끌어들입니다. 한 리뷰는 게임이 RPG의 뿌리를 버리고 액션 측면에 집중하여 잘 만들어진 엔트리를 제공한다고 평가했습니다. 그러나 다른 리뷰어는 게임이 전작에 미치지 못하며, 장르와 프랜차이즈 내에서 최악의 게임 중 하나라고 비판했습니다.
장점: 매력적인 세계와 핵심 메커니즘을 갖추고 있어 밤새도록 플레이어를 끌어들입니다. / 즐거운 파티 멤버와 로맨스 옵션을 제공합니다.
단점: 게임이 RPG의 뿌리를 버리고 액션 측면에 집중하여 일관성이 떨어집니다. / 전작에 미치지 못하며, 장르와 프랜차이즈 내에서 최악의 게임 중 하나입니다. / 일부 아이디어, 장면 또는 개념이 우수하지만 전반적인 경험이 분열됩니다.
critic_contexts: In this “fantasy California” no one dares to hurt someone’s feelings – not even a necromancer or a hired killer. It’s a shame that dialogues are this bad, because the rest of the developer team was actually working hard: combat is fun (if a bit too simple), and some levels are outright gorgeous. [Issue#266]
Ultimately, Dragon Age: The Veilguard delivers on the promise of every Dragon Age with its strong characters, engaging combat, and a classic BioWare role-playing experience.

Overall, the core mechanics were engaging and the world was enchanting enough to keep me coming back night after night. Even when it suffers from subpar writing or when the boss fights are a little too breezy, the dynamic, well-balanced combat, effective worldbuilding and ability to immerse the player in their role as the leader of a diverse group of adventurers who make choices that matter will appeal to a wide variety of gamers. There is much to do, much to say and much to discover around ever
Dragon Age: The Veilguard is a true tour de force from a company that knew it had to give its all to stay relevant thirty years after its founding—and it has undoubtedly succeeded.

Is this that amazing Dragon Age game we've been waiting years for? No, it's not. Is it a good game? Debatable. But it does so many right things at a few vital points that it managed to win my heart with those moments alone.

Regardless of the developers' goals, Veilguard feels like a game designed and assembled in parts. However good any idea, scene or concept is - and there are some excellent ones - it isn't bolstered by those beside it. Instead, each feels like a dazzling distraction from where it falls short in depth, consistency and trust in players to engage with a complex world. [Issue#405, p.102]

The party members are a delight to spend time with and woo. The combat is some of the most fun I’ve had in a while with an action RPG. It is just a shame everything else falls short. The exploration is far too uninteresting, the story is haphazardly put together, and the mission structure is too linear. There is a good enough RPG to enjoy in Dragon Age: The Veilguard for diehard fans like myself, but this is certainly no second coming of BioWare. I have disliked each new Dragon Age more than the
BioWare's worst dialog, trivial puzzles, annoying level design, and tedious combat. My diagnosis: complete apathy.
Dragon Age: The Veilguard is an exceptional-looking game with initially exciting combat, but it’s let down by uneven storytelling that makes it duller than it should be.

It’s goofy, and earnest, and consciously inclusive in a way that makes me, a queer person, scared to talk about it on the internet (even after all these years). It’s bold, and comfortable in itself, and even in its weaker moments, It does feel different to the past games, but for me, it’s a good kind of different.

Dragon Age: The Veilguard fails to live up to its predecessors and ranks among the worst in its genre and the franchise. Despite its potential and success with gender diversity, its attempt to modernize feels poorly executed. Visually, it’s pleasant, but it requires adjusting settings for optimal performance. It’s playable but it is best suited for people who aren't particularly interested in the plot or story.

The Veilguard has some fun combat and exploration mechanics, but in no way do they redeem a sterilized, safe, inoffensive, fully "Netflix" and badly written version of a dark fantasy world that has little to do with the series' past.

All told, Veilguard is an enjoyable RPG with rich character customization and striking visuals, especially within the cyberpunk-inspired Minrathous. The freedom to shape Rook's backstory and outlook is a welcome touch, yet it is offset by the reduction in meaningful choices and the series signature moral complexity. While Veilguard might not capture the epic scope of Origins or the tightly woven, character driven narrative of Dragon Age II, it is a solid entry that hints at a new direction. If f

While The Veilguard takes me back to the familiar world of Dragon Age, it also dares to experiment. Because that works out well (apart from some cringy writing), here is a satisfied fan. The heart of Dragon Age still beats, and with that mine too.
Dragon Age: The Veilguard is a colorful but absolutely passable game that lacks any depth. It will not only disappoint the fans of the previous parts, but also will not give joy to the new ones.

Dragon Age: The Veilguard misses the essence of earlier Dragon Age games, turning the dark fantasy world into a childish fairytale with shallow dialogue and limited, impactless choices. The game is linear and fails to honor the rich lore, intriguing characters, and complex choices the franchise is known for. Although the combat is smooth and visually impressive, it lacks depth and doesn’t fit the world.

This game generally falls within the category of a decent ARPG. The combat feels satisfying, and the progression system provides a rewarding sense of growth. The main quest and especially the final chapters deliver plenty of excitement. the characters’ narratives lack intrigue, and there are aspects that may disappoint fans of the series.

Dragon Age: The Veilguard is an action-filled game that is fluid, crisp, and exciting. The missions are well-written and enjoyable, and the dialogue between companions is delightful. There’s a lot to like about The Veilguard, but there are a few bumps in the road. For older Dragon Age fans, it might feel odd to be planted in this mostly dungeon-based mission corridor, but the experience never feels like a grind and is mostly BioWare playing it safe.

Dragon Age: The Veilguard is a worthwhile return for the RPG franchise. Despite some oversimplification of mechanics and uneven writing, the new combat style and interesting characters and narrative choices make for an engaging fantasy adventure.

Dragon Age: The Veilguard is undoubtedly a competent ARPG, with some commendable elements in its combat and exploration. However, it's disappointing that, aside from the impressive art design and nostalgic appearances of certain characters from previous entries, it feels like BioWare has strayed from the series' original essence. The familiarity is there, but it lacks the deeper connection that longtime fans might have hoped for.
When the long-awaited game comes out clichéd, we have no words to describe our disappointment. Unfortunately, Veilguard fell far short of our expectations.

Dragon Age The Veilguard is a massive new world full of thoughtful stories, epic battles, and beautiful visuals to accompany them. This round of companions is among the most interesting, thoughtful, and downright charismatic, and adventuring with them made for an unforgettable journey.

The writing can be overwrought, written by committee, and occasionally forced, but it’s also a major step forward for a team that needs the win. Dragon Age: The Veilguard brings us compelling characters, excellent combat, and a world worth saving.

Dragon Age: The Veilguard clearly wants to put epic action above all and sprinkles it with deeply emotional moments. Instead of choices and slow, tactical combat, the focus is clearly on a pre-composed story and powerful, action-packed battles. But if you engage with it, BioWare delivers one of the most thrilling role-playing games of recent years.

With Dragon Age: The Veilguard, I basically got exactly what I had hoped for from a sequel to Inquisition. An epic story about elven gods, the end of the world and the previously little-explored nations in the north of Thedas. Plus a wonderful cast of companions who all grew on me in their own way. All of this, embedded in the same attention to detail that made Inquisition one of my favorite games. Rounded off with very satisfying gameplay that works wonderfully in its mechanics. I simply had a

With Dragon Age: The Veilguard, BioWare has largely returned to its roots, casting aside the temptations of open world and/or live service games. Instead, Veilguard is a great mission-based RPGs with a memorable story that will leave Dragon Age fans enthralled by the revelations, an awesome combat system that perfectly blends action and tactics, and lots of loot and secrets to uncover through its 80-hour playthrough.

The combat is wildly different and the tone varies, but the characters here are the same vital, meaningful core that BioWare is known to deliver. While far removed in many ways from the fantasy franchise I grew up loving, Dragon Age: The Veilguard takes its place by prior adventures in Thedas with pride. Like the Hero of Ferelden, Hawke, and the Inquisitor before them, Rook shows that Dragon Age isn't out of tricks just yet.
Dragon Age: The Veilguard refreshes and reinvigorates this storied series with enjoyable action combat, a fantastic cast of companions, and moving character writing.
There is lots to do in this huge and beautiful fantasy world, but inconsistent writing and muted combat dull its blade.

Dragon Age: The Veilguard is full of heart and soul. It’s also got some great ideas. Conversely, many of those ideas feel like they struggle to get out of first gear - and those that do find it harder still to make it to third. Sometimes the cleverest ideas are undermined by other systems or decisions. Simultaneously feeling polished to within an inch of its life in places and utterly half-baked in others, it’s as baffling as it is engaging; as frustrating as it is fascinating.

There is so much else that is impressive and charming about Veilguard. The absurdly elaborate and expensive finales that cap off companion questlines; lavish, unique areas rolled out for a visit or two then never again. How story moments of real threat and menace stopped me in my tracks, because it turned out that Bioware wasn’t disinterested in this stuff, just saving it for when it really counted. The fantastic prose and worldbuilding in the huge glossary, filled as you find notes and items.

This isn’t the end of Dragon Age that I was expecting - in this respect, the game must be rated very low. However, as an action RPG with flair and a beautiful fairy-tale world, it turns out to be decent, and sometimes even more than that.

Dragon Age The Veilguard is the quintessential mass-market AAA, with all the qualities and shortcomings that implies. It's a great adventure with accessible, polished and well-thought-out content, even if the freedom isn't there. This is obviously a major flaw for an RPG, and one that is likely to anger many players. Ironically, in seeking to be open-ended at all costs, while avoiding taking risks, the game commits some major clumsiness in terms of writing. Veilguard is much better than the stud

The game may stumble occasionally in its technical aspects, but its core—compelling storytelling, rich character relationships, and engaging combat—makes it an easy recommendation for not just BioWare fans, but any RPG fan.
A genuinely enjoyable, gorgeous action-RPG that lacks the storytelling nuance of previous Dragon Age games.
Superb visuals and art for a well crafted entry that focus on the action side, abandoning the RPG roots.
Dragon Age: The Veilguard excels on a number of counts; let's see if they're the ones you've been waiting for.

Dragon Age: The Veilguard is a return to form for BioWare. Assuming itself to be more of an action game, some RPG elements have been pushed into the background as a result. The history of the world of Thedas mixes with the history of Solas and our companions, in a plot that seems unappealing at first, but blossoms in the middle of the game. With breathtaking scenery and a very competent combat system, Dragon Age: The Veilguard is yet another fantastic RPG in a big year for fans of the genre.

The latest chapter in the Dragon Age saga successfully combines the best of semi-open-world gameplay with a balanced and engaging combat system. While Dragon Age: The Veilguard falls short of previous installments in areas like side quests, story choices, and dialogue depth, it excels in combat quality, world design, and audiovisual presentation, delivering some of the most epic battles in the series. This game is a roller-coaster experience; at its peak, it entertained and amazed me, yet at tim

Dragon Age: The Veilguard is a work that comes to the proverbial test after ten years. It is immediately clear how complex the project was and how the videogame world has changed over time. With a good combat system, a convincing narrative and a good development of the relationships with the other characters, on some points it does not fully succeed in reaching the objective. I admit that so many words are actually too many, and I actually hope that someone has gotten to the end. but there reall

Dragon Age: The Veilguard is, therefore, a good game that is adapted to the new public but that also preserves enough on a narrative level to please the most classic fan who accepts the approach to action-adventure and who will find, once again, a beautiful, well-constructed universe and a new episode that will give much to talk about in the coming months.
A completely renewed Dragon Age that struggles with some ifs and buts, but above all plays on a very high narrative level.

With Dragon Age: The Veilguard, Bioware is returning to its more successful past. The fantasy role-playing game captivates with exciting characters and a crisp combat system, but is not quite able to revive the heights of its previous successful years.

Though it's ultimately held back its shortcomings, the other highs of Dragon Age: The Veilguard - its characters, its storytelling, and its sense of personality - make the game easy to like despite the flaws. The issues may be harder to look past for some, but others will find joy in the things it does do well.
user_response:
다양한 이용자들은 Dragon Age: The Veilguard의 콘텐츠와 전투에 대해 구체적인 호평을 남겼습니다. 그러나 일부 이용자들은 게임의 최적화와 그래픽에 대해 부정적인 의견을 제시했습니다. 2시간은 잘되다가 시간이 지나니까 계속 튕긴다는 문제를 겪은 이용자도 있었습니다. 성능이 떨어지는 현상이 발생하여 재시작하면 다시 괜찮아진다는 보고도 있었습니다. 전투와 그래픽에 대한 구체적인 호평도 많습니다. 최적화가 잘 되어 있어 플레이에 문제가 없다는 평가도 있었습니다. 그러나 일부 이용자들은 스토리와 캐릭터에 대해 부정적인 의견을 제시했습니다. 무기 방어구 외형이 재탕되고, 디자인 수준이 낮다는 비판도 있었습니다. 이용자들은 Dragon Age: The Veilguard의 가격 대비 가치에 대해 논의했습니다. 일부 이용자들은 EA-Play 구독을 통해 6달러에 게임을 즐기는 것을 추천했습니다. 또한, 게임의 스토리가 전작들에 비해 가벼워졌다는 의견도 있었습니다. 그러나 팬들이 스토리를 하도 욕하길래 걱정했는데 생각보다 재밌게 플레이했다는 평가도 있었습니다.
장점: 쉬운 조작감과 한번에 쓸어버리는 기술로 인한 액션 쾌감이 있습니다. / 그래픽이 준수하여 눈이 즐겁습니다. / 최적화가 잘 되어 있어 플레이에 문제가 없습니다.
단점: 무기 방어구 외형이 재탕되고, 디자인 수준이 낮다는 비판도 있었습니다. / 성능이 떨어지는 현상이 발생하여 재시작하면 다시 괜찮아진다는 보고도 있었습니다.
user_contexts: I will never consider The Veilguard as a Dragon Age game, and for many reasons… They had ten years of irregular planning and fragmented ideas, yet still managed to deliver this unfinished, inaccurate and overpriced product. From all the content and lore available, they reduced or removed so many elements that almost nothing meaningful remains, doing whatever they wanted with what was left. Apparently, some people didn’t even know who Zevran Aranai was, which shows a shameful lack of knowledge ab

Mhhmm, where do I start. Dragon Age: Inquisition is one of my favourite games of all time, so I was very excited for the conclusion of the Fen'Harel storyline, but the inital negative reception of this sequel made me hold off on playing it. Now that I have finally played and 100% completed it I can say, that I'm very conflicted about this game. On one side you do finally get a conclusion to a story that ended on a cliffhanger about 10 years ago and it's not horrible. Then on the other hand it ve

I'd been waiting a decade for this game. I *loved* dragon age. I can't even bring myself to beat it, I just do not have the will. I really tried to give it the benefit of the doubt, but every minute I spend playing this game just gives me psychological damage and makes me so sad for what we could have had. Instead, we get huge plot reveals and cliffhangers we'd been building up to over 3 games delivered through corny dialogue and throwaway background quests (or worse, a NOTE). Companions that ha

Honestly the game feels like a chore. Its just one level above the ubisoft tier games. This game had so much potential because the environments, lighting, textures, clutter(yes i spent most time breaking it) of this game are so good it feels regretable that they couldn't put some soul into the quest design of the game. The combat is somewhat satisfying, but after playing for some time it feels like GODFALL combat. I will eventually complete the game but i will not even touch the side quests of t

Veilguard is less like a sequel and more like a sanitized parody made by people who no longer understand why Dragon Age mattered in the first place. Dragon Age world used to be cruel, political, oppressive, dangerous, companions distrusted each other. The world did not care about your comfort. Veilguard strips away that identity and replaces it with overexplained dialogue, emotionally curated companions, and scenes that constantly soften their own tension before anything meaningful can settle. 1회차 80시간정도 구석구석 핥으며 템은 거의 다 최종강화, 세력치도 1개 2별 나머지 3별. 탐험도도 95%정도 했음. 뜨는 모든 퀘스트 진행함. 그럼 2회차를 할꺼냐고 하면 절대 안함. 추천하냐고 하면 역시 추천 안한다. - 그래픽, 최적화 매우 만족 (팁 : 설정에서 블룸 OFF, 시야 범위 적절히 조정. 난 120%) - 구석구석 탐험하는걸 좋아한다면 괜찮은편. 단, 퀘스트나 아이템이 맵에 뜨기 때문에 그런부분은 호불호 요소. 난 퀘스트는 불호. 다만, 메트로배니아처럼 '현재는' 못가는 길이나 못먹는 아이템들이 존재하는데 이런부분은 취향껏. 난 불호 - 전투 난이도가 쉬운편이긴 하지만 그럭저럭 손맛 있는편. - 충실히 넣어둔 패드 진동. - 대명사는 매우 불호. 자료집에 용어정리가보면 있긴 하지만, 대화창에서 바로 볼 수 있는 편의정도는 넣어줬어야 한다고 봄. - 스토리 자체는 특정 캐릭터들 서사가 꽤 훌륭한편. 스토리 자체도 후반부로 갈수록 괜찮은편이라 느낌. - 전반적인

아오.. 진짜 남들이 뭐라하든 그냥 게임자체로써 보고 즐기려고 했는데 하면 할수록 자꾸만 인퀴지션만큼만 하면될걸 왜 그보다 못하게 했는지에 대한 의문이 끊임없이 듭니다 대체 무슨 생각으로 이따위로 만든건지. 긴장감따윈 하나도 없는 무슨 디즈니동화를 만들어놓은 느낌에 시스템적으로 발전한건 하나도 없고 동료캐릭터들도 하나같이 뚜렷한 매력이 없어요 인퀴지션은 진짜 탐험하는 느낌이 들었는데 이건 그냥 어린이용 캐쥬얼게임하는 느낌이 듭니다 특히 이 시리즈의 중요한 숙적인 다크스폰이며 드래곤이며 그냥 애들 장난감같은 디자인으로 바뀌어서 딱히 잡아야할 마음도 안 생깁니다 그냥 이 시리즈를 말아먹겠다고 작정했거나 정신나간 스태프들이 정신나간 기획을 한거라고 밖에 안 보입니다. -엔딩 본 후 추가평- 단점: 최악인건 첫 서장 이후에는 스토리에 몰입도가 너무 떨어지고 긴장감도 없음.. 매번 넓고 텅빈듯한 맵에서 세상과 별개로 혼자 돌아다니면서 몇 종류 안되는 몹만 반복해서 잡는 느낌. 동기부여가 안됨
타쉬는 앰릭을 이름이 아닌 시체남, 데스메이지라고 부르며, '살아있는 시체는 끔찍하다', '냄새가 이상하다' 등 상대방이 듣고싶어하는 말이 아닌 본인이 하고싶은 말을 한다. 그러나 정작 본인은 '그녀'가 아닌 '그들'로 불리기를 바란다. 바이오웨어의 현재를 관통하는 캐릭터이다.
PC논란 같은거 크게 신경 안쓰는편임 전투만 재밌으면 장땡 (어크 섀도우 존나 재밌게함) 근데 시발 여캐들이 앵간치 못생겼어야지 캐릭 커마부터 ㅈ같이 생겼는데 초반에 동료라고 따라오는 여캐들마저 ㅈ같이 생겼길래 이건 너무 몰입도 안될거같아서 환불엔딩

I don't care about any agenda, I'm woke as heck myself. But believe it or not, this game is even worse than most people say it is. The combat is ADHD button mash rubbish with damage sponge enemies, the design is mobile game bs and the writing straight up blasphemous. Dragon Age used to be mature dark fantasy. This is, in every aspect, the anti-thesis to what Dragon Age should be about.
While it's not like the previous entries of the series, I personally don't understand the hate. I've enjoyed this and it's definitely worth picking up on sale.

드디어 대망의 엔딩을 보았습니다 !! ^___^ 모~~든 퀘스트 다하고 올 클리어 하고 왔습니다! 기록상 62 시간 써있네용!! 그럼 그 누구 보다 정확한 평가를 해보도록 하겠습니다 ! -- 우선 많은 분들이 말했던 *외모* 에 대한 부분은 그냥 평범합니다. 여러분 world of warcraft 아시죠? wow(와우) 그냥 이거랑 외모가 비슷해요 . 그리고 다양한 종족이 나옵니다. 편파적이지 않아요. 근데 여러분이 꼭 *주의* 하실 점 을 당부 드리자면 ,. 본 캐릭터는 ''반드시" 공을 들여서 엄청 예쁘게 만드세요! Customizing 이 꽤 잘되어 있어요. 컷 씬 마다 본캐릭터가 자주 나와서 표정연기를 합니다 , 반드시 예쁘게 만드셔야 몰입하는데 즐거우실 거에요 ^___^ 대충 만드시면 게임하는 내내 후회 하실겁니다 ^.^; 본캐라도 예뻐야.ㅋ - 자 그럼 게임 *액션* 에 대해서 평가 해봅니다 달리기,구르기,대쉬,막기,점프,패링 있어요. 그리고 논 타겟팅이라서 원거리는 에임

액션만큼은 훌륭하다고 생각하는데, 몰입도 떨어지는 스토리 텔링, 개성 없고 지루한 케릭터들, 이해하기 어려울 정도의 선형적 플레이가 이게 드래곤에이지 시리즈가 맞나 싶은 정도이고, 하다보면 내가 RPG를 하는 건지, 핵앤슬래시를 하는 건지 알 수가 없음. 일단 스토리가 너무 노잼에 몰입도가 떨어져서 유튜브 틀어놓고 그냥 전투만 함. 그래도 전투가 그나마 재밌어서 다행인듯.

그냥 '베일가드'로 나왔다면 괜찮은 게임이다. 살짝 부족한 A급정도? 하지만 슬프게도 이 게임은 '드래곤 에이지™:베일가드'로 나왔다. 그리고 이것이 '복합적'인 심정의 비추천의 원인이다. 평가를 쓸려면 어쨌든 한 번은 엔딩을 봐야된다고 생각해서 세일가에 구매하여 1회차를 돌렸다. 어느정도 성실하게 돌아서 '진엔딩'이라고 부르는 결말을 봤다. 그리고 나도 복합적인 심정이 되었다. 어디서 읽었는데 제대로 플레이 해보지도 않고 몇몇 트레일러와 스크린샷 및 기사를 보고 냅다 욕부터 박는다고 하던데 이것은 잘 모르고 하는 소리다. 플레이하는 내내 이게 내가 플레이하던 오리진, 2, 인퀴지션을 잇는 드래곤 에이지가 맞는지 의문이 들었다. 매번 시리즈가 발매될때마다 게임형태가 확확 바뀐건 맞았지만 이건 스타일정도가 바뀐게 아니라 뭐라 말을해야될까 어. 시발 친자검사를 해봐야하지 않을까? 어디서 혼종새끼하나 델구와서 니새끼라고 빡빡우기는데 이 애새끼가 좋은애인지 나쁜애인지 잘생긴애인지 못생긴애인

Dragon Age Veilguard is a third person action RPG where player character Rook must assemble a team of heroes to stop a pair of Elven gods from destroying the world. Dragon Age Origins is my second favourite game of all time (after Witcher 3), however I thought DA2 and Inquisition were disappointing in various ways. I also replayed Inquisition recently to prepare for Veilguard. [h1]Gameplay (medium quality)[/h1] • Character creator is incredibly detailed, and I think I spent about an hour adjusti
게임은 잘 만들었는데 캐릭터 디자인을 잘못해서 몰입도 안되고 진행과정 흐름이 끊기는 구간이 많이 생깁니다. 그래도 돈주고 산거니 엔딩은 봐야죠 ㅎㅎ 저랑 같이 하던 동생은 하딩 이쁜척하는거 못봐주겠다면서 버티면서 하다가 결국엔 끝을 못보고 접었습니다.

팬들이 스토리를 하도 욕하길래 걱정했는데 생각보다 재밌게 플레이했음 근데 확실히 전작들에 비해 굉장히 가벼워진 스토리이긴 해서 전작의 다크하고 딥한 스토리. 이런 걸 기대하면 실망할 것 같긴 함 동료들 개인 스토리도 그렇고 그냥 전체적으로 되게 가벼워짐 정말로 ㅠ 전작은 설정 엄청 촘촘했는데 여기서는 설정 구멍이 보임. 가장 치명적인 건 역시 전작 연동을 싹 없애버린 거 이게 진짜 ㅁㅊㅅㄲ들 같음 그리고 자잘한 버그들도 좀 있었음 물론 나는 해피엔딩무새이고, 그리고 전작들 코덱스를 잘 핥아먹지 않았기 때문에 생각보다 잘 즐겼다 그리고 후반에는 좀 드에 시리즈 느낌이 남 뽕도 차고 웅장하고 나한테 개막중한 선택을 강요하고. 또 나는 수집 요소도 좋아해서 좋았고 전투 난이도도 엄청 쉬워졌고 도전과제도 그리 어렵지 않았다는 점이 좋았음 아 그리고 그래픽은 좀 감동적임. 오리진의 흙감자 그래픽에서 여기까지 왔다는 게 안 믿긴다 그냥 크게 기대하지 않고 가볍게 플레이하면 즐겁게 플레이 할 수
전투라도 재밌다고 해서 했는데 개병맛풍겜

i played through veilguard twice on console and am now picking it up on steam after almost two years. i've been a huge dragon age fan since origins, i waited 10 years for veilguard and i just have to say i really enjoyed the conclusion to the series! of course theres pros and cons to the gameplay loop, writing and characters, but overall, for me, this is the perfect ending for such a long winded story. i feel like you can't really make everyone happy with such a huge fanbase and that's okay. it'

진짜 오랜 팬으로써.. 그리고 바이오웨어사를 정말 좋아하는 사람으로써. 적음. [h1] 반PC주의에게 일갈. [/h1] 얼마전에 이 겜을 하면서 캐릭터 얼굴만 보고 쌍욕박는 사람들 영상을 봤음. 적잖은 충격이었음. 나도 PC주의를 굉장히 싫어하고 이 놈들이 다 된 프랜차이즈에 모래뿌리는 걸 많이 봐서 상당한 원한이 있는데. 미안하지만 타깃을 잘못 설정했다고 말하고 싶음. 바이오웨어는 한 번도 PC하지 않은 적이 없음. 그냥 PC한 회사 그 자체임. 난 반PC주의에 상당부분을 동의하지만, 이렇게 앞뒤 안가리고 두들겨패는 건 동의하지 않음. 원래 매운맛 떡볶이 만들던 식당 가서 왜이렇게 맵냐고 하고 앉아있는 꼴. 오히려 이런 사람들 때문에 진짜 비판해야되는 곳이 부각되지 않고. EA라는 개같은 마수가 내가 제일 좋아하던 게임사를 지난 10년동안 어떻게 개박살냈는지. 그로인해서 작품성 높게 나올 수 있었던 게임이 계속 망가져서 나오는 게 너무 짜증남. [h1] 시리즈 최고의 액션성이었음.

이야, 전투 방식 플레이가 많아졌다고 해서 이전 시리즈 보다 재밌으려나 하고 즐겁게 시작했는데. 아니 이거 로그라이크였나요. 스토리 게임이 아닌것이야? ;_; 저만 걸리는 버그인건가 싶은데 게임한지 하루가 지나거나 3시간 이상하면 로드 오류라고 뜨면서 세이브 된 파일을 불러올 수 없어요. 이전 파일들을 불러오기 눌러도 여전히 로드 오류로 뜹니다. 3일째 처음부터 다시 시작해서 지겹도록 솔라스 의식 방해만 했네요. 이제는 못참겠어서 그냥 제 폴더에서 이녀석은 지워버리려구요. 재미가 없는건 아닌데, 세이브가 안되는 스토리게임이면 강제로라도 켠왕해야 한단거잖아요? . 하하. 그러고 싶진 않네요.

저마다 각자의 취향이 있겠지만, 저는 게임에 애정을 붙이려면 먼저 캐릭터에 정이 가야 한다고 생각합니다. 그런데 이 게임에는 멋지거나 매력적인 캐릭터가 전혀 보이지 않네요. 첫인상이 중요한데, 시작부터 마음에 들지 않았습니다. 평가가 곤두박질친 이후에도 꾸준한 사후 지원이 있었던 것도 아니고, 게임을 회생시키려는 노력조차 보이지 않습니다. 제가 알고 있던 갓겜 '드래곤 에이지'는 대체 어디로 사라진 걸까요?

상당히 잘만든 게임임.. pc도 글쎄다.. 그렇게 강한 느낌을 못받았았고.. 일부 사람들 말처럼 pc사상을 주입한다는 말은 터무니없는 헛소리임.. 논바이너리에 대해서도 단지 그런 가치관을 갖고 있는 소수자가 있고 그런사람들의 가치관을 소개해주는 정도였지.. 그들의 사상을 주입한다는 그런 느낌은 전혀 없었다.. 일부 유투버의 선동에 놀아나지 말고 직접 플레이해보는것을 추천함.. 절대 못만든 게임은 아니고 드래곤에이지 시리즈 쭈욱 다 해봤지만.. 난 베일가드가 가장 재밌었음.. 오리진은 서사의 깊이는 있었지만.. 전투는 진짜 형편없는 수준이여서 몇차례나 초반에 중도포기했었음.. 베일가드 스토리 깊이가 없다라는 말도 있는데.. 내가 봤을때 이정도면 액션 rpg게임으로서 무난한 수준임.. 분량도 많고 상당히 공들인 느낌이 많이 들었음.. 갠적으로 최소 85점이상의 게임이라고 생각하며 정가로 사도 전혀 아깝지 않은 게임이였음..

게임은 진짜 재밌긴함 게임성 중요시하면 무조건 하세요 재밌긴해요 게임자체가 정교하게 잘 짜여져있음.근데 왜 게임을 해야되는지 스토리적으로 공감과 동기부여를 못해줌. 1. 스토리가 복잡하고 이해가 안되고 (참고로 드래곤에이지전시리즈 다함) 2. 갈등이 모두 사라지고 (엘프가 소수민족같은 느낌인데 뭔 논바이너리가 갑툭튀해서 짜증나게) 3. 1,2의 문제로 선택지 선택을 재미있게 해야할 이유가 없어짐 그냥 대충 스킵하고 겜만하게됨 내가 10대 20대때면 끝까지 했을 게임인데 나는 늙고 지친 30대라서 결국 하다가 말았음. 저는 중립인데 너무 저평가되어있어서 추천으로 박음.

현세대 AAA급 자본으로 보여줄 수 있는 최고의 퍼포먼스였다고 생각 됨. 아쉬운점이야 많지만, 여기서 자본과 시간을 더 투자할 수 있는 회사는 많지 않다고 본다. 장점 높은 수준의 그래픽이 와이드리니어 형식과 잘 결합되어 훌륭한 연출과 내러티브를 보여 줌. 단점 파밍이나 스킬트리 깊이가 너무 얕아서 여러가지 빌드를 짜보는 재미같은건 없음. 차라리 파밍요소를 버리고 스킬트리를 세분화 시키는게 더 좋은 선택이었을 듯. PC주의 뭐 그런걸로 욕 개쳐먹던데, 막상 해보니 그렇게 뜬금없다는 생각은 들지 않았고, 해당 캐릭터의 출신과 상황에 잘 들어맞는 적절한 설정이었던것 같다. 회사 상황이 좋지않아서 이 시리즈가 어케 될지 알수는 없지만 그래도 재밌었고 다음에 또 보자~

LGBTQ 빼고 게임만 놓고 평가하면 - 스토리는 엄청 심오하지도, 대단하지도 않고 무난함 - 동료 케릭터 간의 연계 콤보와 그럭저럭 볼만한 이펙트와 타격감 - 이럴거면 굳이 퍼즐을 넣었어야 했나 싶을 정도로 단순하고 반복적인 퍼즐 - 남녀를 떠나 그럭저럭 매력적인 동료와 NPC들 LGBTQ 를 빼 놓고 평가할 수 있었던 이유 동료 NPC "타쉬" 가 게임 내 LGBTQ 대표 케릭인데, 플레이 전 이에 대한 사전 정보는 모르는 상태였음 - 게임 진행 중 필수 동료로 처음 만나게 되는데, 첫 만남부터 개싸가지 없어서 호감 1차 스택 - 어찌저찌 반드시 같이 진행해야 하는 스토리 구간이 있어서 진행 중인데 무슨 호르몬 폭발한 틴에이지 마냥 말 한마디 한마디가 졸라 띠겁고 개씹잼민이 마인드라서 호감 2차 풀스텍 - 풀스택 쌓은 이후 타쉬 동료 퀘스트가 생기건 말건 말을 걸던 말던 걍 쌩깜, 돈필요할땐 장비 벗겨서 팔아넘김 - 엔딩 본 후 이 게임이 LGBTQ로 불탔던 게임이라는데 생각보다

드래곤에이지 팬이라 100% 완료했다. 대중성을 잡겠다는 논리로 동료들을 단순 주인공의 콤보를 위한 스킬셔틀로 활용하게 함으로써 시리즈 근본이었던 포지션 설정, 스킬 활용 등의 요소를 제거하여 전략적 깊이를 거세했다. 고리타분한 과거의 틀을 깨겠다며 PC적 요소는 아주 적나라하게 주입했지만 정작 CRPG 장르의 본인들의 고유한 색깔은 매니악하다는 이유로 스스로 지워버린 지독할 정도로 모순적인 게임이라는 인상이 강하게 남는다. 부디 다음 작품에서는 대중성이니 PC주의니 주접들 떨지 말고 본인들이 잘하는 CRPG 근본으로 회귀하기를 바란다. (2.0 / 5.0)
명작이었던 시리즈를 종결낸 바이오웨어 역대 최악의 게임 시리즈의 팬에대한 그리고 게이머들 과거 시리즈를 만들었던 모든이들에 대한 모욕 무료로 준다해도 손도 대서는 안되는 게임

전투가 확실히 재미있다! 손맛도 있고! 그치만 주인공 커마를 열심히 하면 모하냐? 동료들 면상보면 울컥하는데! 무기 방어구 외형은 재탕에.디자인 수준이. 동료들 면상급으로 울컥하게 하네! 돈을 쳐받고 게임을 파는거면. 그냥 재미와 볼거리를 있게 만들어서 쳐팔면 되는건데. 왜 특정 사상을 강제로 주입 하려고 하지? 드래곤에이지 시리즈와 매스이펙트 시리즈는. 이제 추억으로 묻어둬야겠다! ㅂㅂ2

형성된 여론만큼 엉망인 게임은 아닌거 같습니다. 기본기에 충실한 게임인데 특출나게 강점이 있는건 잘 모르겠습니다. 그나마 드래곤에이지 전작들 캐릭이 나온다는데 강점인거 같습니다. 추천, 비추천, 중용이 있다면 중용을 누루고 싶은데 그래도 Bad는 아니라 추천 누릅니다. 다 무난하게 좋은데 프로스트 바이트 엔진 특유의 느낌과 움직일때 다소 무게감이 떨어지는게 약간 아쉽습니다.

순수하게 재미가 없음
오히려 전작을 안했다면 재미있게 했을 것 같다. 파티원 둘을 데리고 다니면서 적절한 보조로, 주인공이 메인으로 전투를 이끌어 갈 수 있는 것이 강점. 맵은 비교적 일직선이지만 자잘한 퍼즐이 있다. 자주 등대로 돌아가 동료퀘를 할 것! 메인 스토리 진행되면 더 이상 진행될 수 없는 것들이 있다!

대사가 존나게 많은데 스킵 기능 있어도 중간에 선택지를 넣어서 스킵 조차 힘들게 해놨다 그 선택지라는 게 스토리에 있어 중요한 것만 선택하게 했으면 좋으련만, 하등 쓸 데 없는 대사까지 선택하게 해놔서 흐름이 뚝뚝 끊기는 건 물론, 인퀴지션 사라처럼 재밌는 대사만 하면 상관 없는데 별 쓰잘떼기 없는 걸로 떠든다 그나마 나은 게 전투인데 그것조차 초반에만 재밌고 레벨 올라갈 수록 체력돼지들만 등장하고 미친 듯이 지루한 전투만 반복한다 인퀴지션은 파트너 교환은 물론 다른 캐릭터까지 컨트롤할 수 있어서 전투의 다양함이 느껴졌는데 이건 후속작임에도 불구, 그 장점을 빼버리고 단순하게 만들어서 유일한 장점마져 사라졌다 누군가는 드래곤에이지만 빼면 괜찮은 작품이라고 하던데 이건 완전 별개의 타이틀이라고 해도 절대 좋은 점수 줄 수 있는 작품이 아니다 액션 RPG라면 똥이라도 괜찮다는 유져라 할지라도 시간 낭비하지 말고 다른 걸 찾아 보시길

- 장점 최적화가 잘 되어 있다. 그래픽이 준수하여 눈이 즐겁다. 쉬운 조작감 한번에 쓸어버리는 기술이 있어 액션 쾌감이 있다. 맵 순간이동 기능이 잘되어 있어서 편리하다. - 단점 법사 기준 특정 빌드가 너무 쌔서 한쪽 테크만 타게 됐다. 다른 테크 여러 개 해봤는데 작성자 능지 이슈 때문인지 특정 빌드만 너무 쌨다. 스킬 구성이 단조로와 전투 스타일이 거의 변화가 없다. 패시브 트리는 있지만 트리를 찍더라도 드라마틱하게 변화 하지 않는다. 전작들과는 다르게 동료들은 그냥 스킬싸개일 뿐이다. 전략적인 움직임이 필요 없어졌다. 스토리가 단조롭고 뻔한 영웅 서사라 흥미가 떨어진다. 동료들이 안 예쁘고 매력적인 서사도 없다. - 총평 드래곤에이지 1.2.3 모두 엔딩을 본 팬으로써 매우 매우 아쉬운 후속작이다. 의리 때문에 겨우 엔딩을 봤다. 지인이 하겠다고 하면 비추천한다. 스토리라도 흥미롭게 짜주었다면 혹은 스킬트리라도 좀더 세분화하여 성장하는 재미라도 조금 더 있었더라면 혹은 매력

전투스타일이 취향이라, 못생긴동료는 뭐 스킾하면 되니까.. 생각을 했지만, 컷씬 나올때마다 그 얼굴들 참을수가없다.. 환불함 게임 만큼은 좀 판타지로 냅두면 안되냐 개발자들아?

처음엔 재밌게 했는데. 원래부터 대대로 PC요소가 없지 않았지만 자연스러운 서사여서 거슬리지 않았는데 이번 작은 PC주의를 교육하려 하고, 그래서 유치해지는 점이 최고 단점이라고 생각된다. 이전의 드래곤에이지 작품들은 인종이나 계층에 대한 차별이라던지 부조리함 등이 적나라하게 표현되는 성인들의 다크 판타지였는데 전투나 퍼즐요소, 숨겨진 것들을 발견하는 재미는 굉장히 좋았지만 스토리적으로 몰입할 요소가 너무 유치하고 PC요소를 강제로 교육 하려 드는 느낌이어서 실망하고 흥미가 떨어지게 되었다. 캐릭터 디자인도 전작까진 비율이 8등신이었던거 같은데 묘하게 작은거 같다. 그림체가 달라져서 그런건지 비율 자체가 변한건지 좀 어색한거 같다. 드디어 디즈x 도 PC사상을 손절치기 시작한 요즘 더더욱이나 이런 요소가 참 아쉽게 느껴진다. 드래곤 에이지를 처음 접하는 사람은 1편이나 3편을. 특히 1편을 플레이 하길 바란다. 이 작품은 아니다.

Dragon Age: The Veilguard — A Masterpiece That Trips Over Itself - Probably the best game in the series mechanically. Possibly the most frustrating to recommend. Some classes need rebalancing, but for a single-player game that's a minor footnote, not a dealbreaker. RPG and Story - Your decisions carry weight — genuinely Game of Thrones-level weight in places. Overview: I cannot recommend this if you are a fan of Dragon Age Inquisition and want to continue the story from where it left off. For no valid reason, the devs got rid of all the characters in inquisition, including the one you play as, in favor of a completely new cast that has nothing to do with the current plot. All the characters from inquisition were deeply involved with the current story and now they cease to exist, except Varric who is relegated to an invalid in the first 10 minutes of

Beautiful visuals, an engaging combat system with party combos and interdependent builds, and a party and faction system that takes you across the breadth of Thedas' worldbuilding. The narrative did a great job of brining together storylines that have been building across the Dragon Age franchise for years, making a great coda for the series.

Every iteration of a Dragon Age game is different. My young 13 year old heart was broken when DA 2 was released. It was not the sequel I had dreamed of and it taught me a valuable life lesson about expectations. Since then, I have learned to check out trailers and reviews before purchasing a game. Veilguard was no different. I knew what I was getting into. It's an action adventure game that drank the Marvel Kool-aid and added rpg elements. And with those expectations, I enjoyed my experience. 60퍼 세일로 구매한 사람의 환불기록 갓 오브 워와 스파이더맨 같은 연출에 집중한 서양 AAA급 게임 특유의 전투 감각이 너무 취향에 안 맞아서 결국 환불합니다. 만화적으로 과장된 붕붕거리는 모션으로 체력달린 나무통 투닥거리는 그 감각이요. 몰입도를 갖기엔 전작을 안 한 관점에서 무슨 이야기인지 무슨 상황인지 이해하기엔 조금 붕 뜨는 감이 있었고 주절주절 다들 말만 많게 느껴졌구요. 스킵 버튼을 누르면 꼭 자막이 공백 0.5초 뒤에 띄워져서 그 많은 텍스트량을 속독 불가하게 느릿느릿 읽어야했구요. 모든 설정 인터페이스는 버튼 누를 때가 아니라 뗄 때 입력되는 방식이라 체감 지연속도가 200ms가까이 느껴지고 퀘스트 표시, 아이템 획득 표시, 튜토리얼 등 '거의 모든' 인터페이스는 불필요한 수준으로 반짝거리고, 출렁거리는 과장된 표현으로 시선을 분산시키고 눈을 아프게 만드는 등 짜증을 유발하구요. 게임 구석구석 만듦새가 떨어지는 구석이 시작부터 많이 보여서 환불을 결정했습니다. 솔직히
플레이하는 재미는 엄청나긴 한데.좀 앙상하긴 하다. 묘하게 파판16이랑 진행방식이 비슷한거 같기도 한데. 16이 전체 시리즈 안에서 어떤 평가를 받는지를 생각하면, 기존팬들이 반발하는 것도 충분히 이해가는 상황. 사실 전작을 아주 조금만 해본 상황에서는 그냥 저냥 재밌는 게임이라 생각되지만.
이런 장르는 처음 먹어봐서 아직 맛을 잘 모르겠습니다 그러나 다시 먹을 일도 없을거 같아요

[h1] 아쉽지만 그래도 좋긴하네 [/h1] 아 참 재미는 있는데 말이야. 이야기는 참 좋아. 근데 이게임이 나름 액션 RPG 게임이라는게 참. 디테일들이 너무 아쉬워요. 그냥 이야기는 되게 좋았는데 말이야. 차라리 무비게임을 만들지 그랬냐 바이오웨어야. 전투들을 단순한 조합으로 만드는건 알겠는데 너무 단순하고 가벼워 그렇다보니 금세 전투가 지겨워진다. 게임이 액션 RPG 인데 전투가 지루해진다? ㅅㅂ;; 컷신같은거나 힘 많이주고 만들지 아유 답답하다 답답해~ 이게 정녕 최신게임이 맞냐. 디테일들이 너무 없어 허접하다 허접해~ 그와중에 성우들과 작가들은 열일했다 ㄹㅇ 아무튼 그래도 재밌게 했다. 시리즈 나올때마다 어설프게 이상한 도전정신으로 전작의 장점들좀 갖다 버리지좀 말고 좋았던거는 좀 재탕좀 했으면 좋겠네 입문작으로 하기에는 쓰레기 같긴하지만, 그래도 시리즈 팬들한테는 나름 괜찮을 것 같다고 생각함. 뭐 여러가지 내부사정으로 전작들의 내 선택지들을 홀라랑 다 날려먹는건 ㅈ같았지

If you like BioWare storytelling and epic fantasy, definitely give it a go. Would play again.

Absolute work of art. Might be a hot take but it got way more hate than it deserved. There are certain gameplay changes that i didn't really enjoy but nothing that took away my love for the game. I've loved this series since it came out pretty much and after years of waiting to be able to play this installment of the franchise myself I can safely say it's reignited my passion for the series. Well done to those who took part in it's creation. I'm eagerly awaiting whatever comes next in the story.

Every iteration of a Dragon Age game is different. My young 13 year old heart was broken when DA 2 was released. It was not the sequel I had dreamed of and it taught me a valuable life lesson about expectations. Since then, I have learned to check out trailers and reviews before purchasing a game. Veilguard was no different. I knew what I was getting into. It's an action adventure game that drank the Marvel Kool-aid and added rpg elements. And with those expectations, I enjoyed my experience. Overall the game is fantastic. A VERY solid combat system that makes use of every button of the controller with well designed intent. Nothing ever feels out of place, and although you have to periodically keep an eye on your cooldowns, it's never more than a fraction of a second. Nor is the GUI invasive into what you need to be paying attention to while you're fighting. It has a solid arcadey hack and slash feel to it. Nothing too deep, but developed enough to be engaging throughout the battles.

Veilguard has a great story, some pretty good characters, and easily the best combat and visuals in the series. It's Mass Effect in the Dragon Age universe. And yes, that's a bit more action-focused than the first two entries in the series, but I never really felt like those games nailed their more tactical combat anyway so I'm VERY pleased that they overhauled it and just focused on excitement. I can legitimately understand someone being upset that the combat style was changed, even if I was ve
The hate is undeserved. This is a really great game. I hope more people figure that out and give it a try.

와씨~ PC 묻었다고 이 겜 안했으면 어쩔뻔했냐! 전투, 스토리, 음악, 맵 설계, 온갖 디테일들.. 정말 갓겜 수준이다. 남들은 마지막이 별로라고 했지만 난 마지막 전투하러 가는 길부터 감동을 도가니로 먹어버렸다. 말 그대로 PC는 묻은거니 이건 그냥 묻었다 생각하고 무시하면 되는거다 진짜 2025 올 해 내가 한 게임 중 젤 재밌게 한 게임이다

겜 자체는 그냥저냥인데 팬으로서는 완전 별로 때깔만 좋지 게임성 그닥, 주인공 및 동료 캐릭터들 매력 너무 없음. 도중에 한번 엎어졌었다더니 겨우 꿰맨느낌. 게임성은 오히려 오리진이 최근 기준으로도 발더스3에 견줄만큼 정말 재밌었는데 이렇게 반복전투로 퇴보할거면 그쪽으로 걍 틀면 좋겠다 요소 하나하나씩은 정말 괜찮은데, 너무 따로노니까 조화가 안되서 집중이 안됨. 그리고 주인공을 포함한 모든 캐릭터들이 매력이 없음. 외적으로도 개성적으로도 매력이 없으니까 정이 안감. 심지어는 전작캐릭터들 매력까지 다 뺏어버렸다고 느낌. 이럴거면 그냥 인퀴지터를 다시 주인공으로 세우던가ㅜ 그래서 스토리도 그냥저냥 아직까진 재미 못느끼고 오히려 이전 시리즈들 더 하고싶어져서 도중에 이탈하게 됨. 무려 10년이나 기다린 후속작이고, 오리진부터 쭉 쌓아온 떡밥들이나 설정들도 풀어줌에도 불구하고 재미를 못느끼니까 그런 것도 다 연결되어있다고 느껴지지가 않음.

물론 실망할 법하지만 액션 RPG 로써는 할만합니다. 어드벤쳐 요소는 가끔 킹 받지만 쉬운 편.. 맵 디자인은 같은 장소 여러번 오게 하는데 나름 나쁘진 않았습니다. 특히 PC주의적 요소는 불합격 연애 요소가 있는데 왜 해야 되는지 모르겠음.. 심지어 하트 나올 때마다 불쾌하네요.. 스토리도 너무 지루하고 재미없어서 바로 스킵하게 됩니다. 그냥 그래픽 괜찮은 액션 RPG (긴머리 여캐를 만들어서 머리 휘날리는 거 보세요~) 드래곤에이지 다음 작품을 재미있게 하기 위한 발판 같은 느낌으로 본다면 하세요~!

드래곤에이지 시리즈를 하나도 안해본 상태에서 플레이함. 로맨스 루트는 타지 않음. 액션, 타격감 좋고 전투시스템도 아주 만족스러웠음. 스토리선택은 큰 줄기에선 분기점이 있고 자잘한 대화는 큰 영향력이 없는데 그래서 부담없어서 괜찮았음. 개인적으로 뒤로 갈수록 스토리 몰입도도 아주 높았음. 그래픽도 내 취향이고 캐릭터 커스터마이징이 좀 고정된 느낌이고 목소리타입이 적은게 아쉬웠지만 여성 루크 목소리가 아주 좋아서 그럭저럭 만족.
조금 해봤는데 재밌네여
다른 사람은 몰라도 나는 너무 재미있겠했다.
It wasn't my favorite of the Dragon Age games, but I still really enjoyed this game, for the most part. The graphics and scenery in the game were beautiful.

Great game with amazing gameplay, a compelling story and fun companions. I don't know how it holds up to the rest of the series since I only played inquisition and Veilguard, but I'm loving it. I especially love the new inclusion of trans and nonbinary experience and dialogues specific to these. I also really like that your identity and background overall (specie and faction) have an impact in the dialogues.
Awesome game

My starting point to this series was Dragon Age Inquisition, I did not play 1 and 2. Therefore, the first time I played this game with an EA store subscription, I lacked a lot of context for the history of the game. The first time I played the game, I played it donning figurative eyewear made of extreme criticism as I expected a lot from this game, fair to say I did not like it and had to stop halfway into it. Then I bought it on steam for a discounted price and watched some history into the wor

One of the few games that didn’t irritate me at all: the interface is intuitive and comfortable to use, I encountered no bugs, and the game runs very smoothly. The story felt confusing at first, and the companies seemed a bit generic, but it improved over time. Overall, I really enjoyed playing the game.

Overall, an amazing game that any Dragon Age fan should play. The positives: - The graphics were top notch! - The level design was beautiful and extensive. - The sound effects were perfect and the music very good. - The gameplay (at least the warrior side - didn't play the other classes) was both fun and challenging. - The main storyline writing was engaging. The negatives: - The companion storylines felt cheesy and predictable (save Taash's. Theirs connected even if controversial to some). Also
Honestly, my favorite dragon age. 10/10 just wished it was longer.

This is a great capstone for the Dragon Age series, and brings the ending of Inquisition to a satisfying finale. The gameplay is stellar: you can tune the difficulty up to higher settings if you like action RPGs, because it can get pretty intense and feel very rewarding when you go through entire fights without getting hit, setting up combos, and making the most of directing your teammates and optimizing your build and gear. Or you can tune the difficulty down and breeze through the game for the
♥ love complaining
It's not what I want from DA, ofc, but it's not a big game. Mediocre DA game, but ok'ish - solid game overall.

I really enjoyed this game. The combat was solid, story engaging and graphics excellent. I think this one is a big win for Bioware despite the low sales. really hope they continue this trend because this game is fantastic.

I came into this game knowing how much everyone seemed to hate it, so my expectations were pretty low. It's definitely not as expansive and choice-driven as the previous games. The choices you do get seem to get pretty streamlined, and one or two of them have pretty atrocious consequences that you really cannot avoid one way or another. The game seems to hold your hand for the entire run, where you pretty much know at all times what missions are available and where, which is great, really, but i

Crazy fun game. A good narrative driven game where the dialogue gets a bit goofy at times and it is slow to start, but once you get into it you can run around for hours making fun in Thedas. I would play it again

If you really enjoy story and fast paced fighting compared to the strategy the other dragon ages are i think its a good play. just wish your choices had more affects on the world around you especially 1-3 all had links to eachother.
I like this game
You can't have a Mabari hound in this one. almost voted no from that.

This has been one of the most fun single player experences I have played through in a bit. This game was recommended to me over their representation of trans/ nonbinary storylines. But this game is more than the "woke" representation. The story is great and each charcater is well written with lovely personalities.
'Veilguard' is easy to pick up, even for someone previously unfamiliar with the series, is full of endearing characters, has an intriguing plot, and has very satisfying combat mechanics.

It's pretty enjoyable IF you don't play it as a direct Dragon Age game. More like.. something set in the same world. Definitely not on the same level as the previous games. Kinda sad that you can FEEL the potential this game had and the little bits of it that remain as mere ghosts of what could have been. It's certainly pretty tho

Over all maybe 5,5/10 experience, I suspect that none of the people who made the original games work here anymore and it shows. I did finish it, but the whole story resolution was a letdown. Definitely a heavy discount purchase option. Great music, probably the best part of the game Encounters with Solas were some of the best scenes in the game I dont mind the big lore expansion and changes they made Landscapes were pretty, but character design looks super cartoony?? much worse than the previous

not every game is for everyone and this game certainly has some things that I hate (Taash, obvious ideology pandering) But overall this game is super fun, combat is fun and the story and characters aren't terrible. Overall a gorgeous romp through thedas :D

Ever since a dear friend gifted me the Ultimate Edition of Dragon Age: Origins, I've been riding that high all throughout the series. While DA2 didn't resonate as much with me as others, I still relished the love and attention put into it as much as its predecessor. Dragon Age: Inquisition remains one of three games I've put 1000+ hours into; now that I've gotten back into modding, I'm gonna play it [b][i] again [/i][/b] just to see what all I can do. But this.this game I've waited so long for,

game is good. surprisingly, i seem to find the new companions easier to level up compared to dai. also, in the character creation menu for my inquisitor, i couldn't find any option to "romance her with solas" what a bummer :/ also the romance scenes are so-so, just few scenes. never liked the idea of "forced death" regarding davrin or harding.

The game is good. The companions may not be as compelling as previous games, but they have their charm. The story itself is pretty alright too, maybe not the direction most people were hoping for, though. It can be really corny at times and lacks the full grit of a "Dragon Age" game. I think the strong points definitely lie in the graphics, I could watch those cutscenes over and over.

Overall the game isn’t actually that bad, but I wouldn’t call it good either. It doesn’t quite feel like a dragon age game, the characters lack depth, but are interesting in concept, the choices are meaningless, and have little impact on the story, and it lacks the distinct dark fantasy feeling of the previous games.

I have mixed feelings about this game. Inquisition will always be my favourite game I've ever played due to the worldbuilding, the characters, the politics, the gorgeous settings, the incredible music, the writing and everything else. I remember the Trespasser DLC moving me like no other game has (yes, I romanced Solas). So Veilguard had a lot to live up to. I think the best way to play Veilguard is to approach it as a completely different game that happens to be set in the Dragon Age universe a
Game is alright by itself but the story includes some forced woke-ness that you can't 100% avoid, At least I can kill the woke NPC's in BG3.
Not really difference with inquisitor but story and new looks
i really like the fighting mechanics they gave it a much better upgrade, i like the stories do wish they had a little more romance with some characters
It's awesome.

pc충이 난장을 피워놓은 불쾌한 모델링과 스토리 등등은 다른사람들이 충분히 말해줄테니 각설하고, 애초에 게임자체를 못만들었음. 별것도아닌 달리기 키마저 제대로 못만들어서 정지,방향전환,점프,언덕넘기 등등 아무 동작이나 하면 토글이 풀려 게임내내 달리기 키를 손가락 부서져라 눌러야하며 인게임에서 획득할 수 있는 장비의 종류도 갯수가 매우 적고, 상대할 적도 색놀이도 없이 그대로 수치만 바꿔 복붙만 주구장창 해서 잡몹부터 보스까지 다합쳐도 종류가 열가지도 안됨. 스토리는 누구나 말하지만 동료끼리 갈등1도없고 스토리 서사도 부실하며 전개 과정도 미흡한 엉망진창 왜 이 게임이 혹평을 받고 실적이 망했으며 그 결과로 개발진이 전부 정리해고당하고 스튜디오가 사실상 폐업의 수준에 이르게 만들었는지 알수있게됨
아 뭔가 아쉽다. 나름 재미는 있는데. 전작이랑 이어지는 요소도 적고 더 해봐야 알듯

For what Bioware had to go through to push to release this game? I'd say it was a good attempt. Is it the best game ever made? No, not really. Is it even the best dragon age game? Seriously doubt anything can live up to what people think the BEST dragon age game is. I'm a "new fan" (I played through all the games, somehow without spoilers, from origins to now veilguard for the past year or so) so I can't say anything about "waiting for 10 years only to be let down". This game isn't nearly as ♥ a

It's not perfect by any means and it left a lot more loose ends from previous games than I would have liked. But it's fun for what it is and scratched a lot of my lore itches. Don't go in expecting it to be at the same level as previous games and you're more likely to have a good time.
Its good but not as a dragon age game

I love the Dragon Age Franchise and wish they would make more games in the series, I love that I can have romance options and that I can even settle down with whomever I choose. I enjoy every minute of my time in the world of Dragon Age.
Different than the previous iterations, but a good game nonetheless.
Underrated af !

Dragon Age: The Veilguard isn't Dragon Age: Origins. And once I stopped expecting it to be, I had a great time. The combat is faster, more action-focused, and far more cinematic than previous entries. Building your character, combining abilities with companions, and taking on larger encounters feels satisfying throughout the adventure. While longtime fans may miss some of the older tactical systems, the combat remains engaging and fun in its own right. The real highlight is the companions. As al

Despite the criticism I did have fun with this game, though the fetch quests and story telling can be frustrating. If you enjoy this kind of game, get it on sale. Worth a try, but probably not something for those looking for a game that feels like Dragon Age.

title: Star Wars Jedi: Survivor
critic_response:
스타 워즈 제다이: 생존자는 비평가들로부터 구체적인 호평을 받았습니다.
장점: 강력한 서사적 순간들과 탄탄한 게임성이 돋보입니다. / 게임은 전작의 규모와 범위를 확장했습니다.
단점: 일부 비평가들은 게임이 전작의 성과를 따라가지 못했다고 평가했습니다.
critic_contexts: Respawn Entertainment still looks up to Dark Souls, but at the same time they try to make a hardcore game with the most softcore experience. For example, there’s always plenty of shortcuts, so Cal never loses much progress after his inevitable demise. [Issue#262, p.58]

Deep and tantalizing combat, enticing exploration, smooth platforming, and strong narrative moments lead the way in Star Wars Jedi: Survivor. This sequel improves nearly everything and only the suboptimal framerate leaves a sour taste.
The best Star Wars game of recent years is unfortunately plagued by serious technical problems that dampen the enjoyment.

The story works but it has some notable flaws, and if you’re familiar with the property lots of it feels like familiar ground. The gameplay is solid enough but it’s very much about following a very strict path rather than going off in new directions. The visuals are afforded far too much weight and don’t actually work all that well beyond seeing quick snippets in trailers.

Jedi: Survivor is a strong entry in the modern Star Wars canon, part of a new subsect of adventures in this universe finding ways to be sci-fi fantasy without ignoring the innate horror and banal evils inherent in the premise. The story paints an impressively dire picture of the new status quo in the galaxy, and it weaves in elegantly with the interactivity of the game, tying it directly into the fact that Cal is still powerful but only one Jedi in a galaxy that fell even when there was an entir

This is a solid Star Wars game with excellent combat, great immersion for fans, and an engaging story. It looks great at most times, but runs poorly overall and needs patches for PC before it gets a solid buy recommendation.

Star Wars Jedi: Survivor is an amazing game. I had a lot of fun with the new stances and the story is enthralling. If not for the performance issues, this game could have been easily among the best released this year.

Ultimately, whether Star Wars Jedi: Survivor is for you will depend on how much you liked the first game. As much as it builds on the first game in terms of size and scope, it doesn’t feel like it’s going in any new direction. It’s more of what was good about the last game, a second helping of a meal you already know you like. That’s not to damn it with faint praise, it’s an enjoyable game with a lot of content, but there’s no new angle that the previous game didn’t cover already. Add to that th

Star Wars Jedi: Survivor shows that Star Wars games are far from dead. Somehow, Electronic Arts has released one of the deepest AAA single-player action games in years. Judging by the quality of the second part, the next game in the series has every chance of becoming the best game in the history of a galaxy far, far away.
A wonderful sequel with a gripping Star Wars story, excellent combat and fatal performance.

Star Wars Jedi: Survivor is almost the perfect sequel. Sadly, improvements in gameplay, story, visuals and more is mired in an undercooked package, with horrible performance and fatal crashes plaguing, at the very least, the game's launch week.
The PC version of Jedi Survivor is a disaster for optimization. You have to wait for the updates to solve the problems because the PC game offers very nice things. The game is sublime.

Star Wars Jedi: Survivor is an enjoyable adventure game, but it has some flaws in the combat system. The gameplay and system design are based on mature industry standards, with platforming, exploration, combat, and puzzle-solving all present and tightly interconnected. However, the game's performance issues result in a significant gap between what players expect and what they experience during the game.

This is a sequel that miserably fails to fill its predecessor's shoes. It has the best features of the original but they didn't know how to take advantage of them. The current lack of ambition in animations, scripts, graphical execution, optimization and overall satisfaction levels leave a lot to be desired. This 2023 is not the best year for the Star Wars franchise.
Star Wars Jedi Survivor is the best game in the franchise since Kotor, offering an immersive gaming experience with captivating exploration, exciting combat and extensive customization.

Jedi: Survivor is an excellent sequel for Fallen Order. Not only has it gotten rid of the weaknesses of its predecessor, but it has also turned them into strengths. If only I could say the same thing for its technical problems.

No surprises here. Survivor doubles down on Fallen Order, deepening the Jedi power fantasy and expanding on its predecessor in every way, though all its choice and freedom does limit both the story and, to a degree, the combat.

Star Wars Jedi: Survivor is a marked improvement upon the first game, despite suffering from some of the woes I had with the 2019 title. Cal Kestis’ journey across the galaxy is fraught with danger and the supporting cast are a treat to chat with whether in the midst of a mission or as you explore Koboh. While the combat and movement is still quintessentially Star Wars, it feels like the game has yet to reach its full potential. The good news is that, if you’re a Star Wars fan, you’re going to h
Whether you're a hardcore fan, or loved Fallen Order before it, Star Wars Jedi Survivor is The Empire Strikes Back to Fallen Order’s A New Hope.

I was on a mission-critical infiltration to save someone's life with nice giant Bode, and I kept running off like "I know man, just, yeah, no it is, it's very important. yeah, no, one second, I saw something shiny over here and I'm not going to replay this level for ages". That doesn't feel very Jedi Knight. My repeated calls for every game to be at least 40% smaller go unheeded. Still, it's very fun rolling a Stormtrooper over your back and pinning him to the floor with a lasersword, isn't it?

It’s hard to assess an excellent and very engaging game that's also fundamentally flawed with technical and performance issues. Then again, these are easier to eliminate, and probably they will be soon. Or not. Star Wars Jedi: Survivor is a 9/10 game for SW fans, but for everyone else, as an experience that's not very comfortable, the score would be considerably lower.

A massive sequel with a more fun combat system, a great protagonist and a beautiful galaxy to explore. The story could be better, and the technical issues freeze as well, but it looks beautiful, plays well, and you can build the lightsaber of your dreams.
Star Wars Jedi: Survivor is an excellent sequel and a fun Star Wars story, but maybe wait for better performance.

With Jedi Survivor, Respawn-Electronic Arts takes us on a real Star Wars adventure. The atmosphere and the grandiosity of the sets go into light speed, thanks to the use of ray tracing and the power of the PS5 / Xbox Series generation. The open areas are really fun to ride and never veer into easy filling. The staging is top notch from start to finish, delivering both epic and touching scenes. The writing and the scenario even gain in thickness. Finally, we would have just liked the gameplay, ce

The best way to describe Star Wars Jedi: Survivor is “safe.” Much like with Jedi: Fallen Order, it is clear that Respawn has a lot of good ideas for this franchise and wants to draw from a variety of influences to make something special, but it ultimately ends up making a pastiche of AAA game mechanics and fixings that are serviceable but not very refined. Jedi: Survivor doesn't have the same novelty and luster that Fallen Order had behind it, so when it rests on its laurels like this, the whole

It's hard to gauge how much of these technical hurdles are down to a rushed launch, poor optimization or simply the results of a game that hasn't been designed to run on last generation's hardware - but it takes what is undeniably a game of the year contender for me, and makes it difficult to fully recommend to anyone without either the hardware to back it up. Star Wars Jedi: Survivor is an excellent game, and an especially fantastic sequel; it's just a shame that most players will ultimately ha

Did not expect to enjoy Survivor as much as I did. However, I have some complaints. The game has decent character progression, story progressed in an enjoyable and interesting way than its prequel, more combat variety with the introduction of multiple stances that can be switched mid fight and much more. Survivor included everything its prequel was missing while also improving everything I enjoyed about it. This game actually felt like an open world with a drastic improvement in map design. Noti

This game is truly incredible, and I mean it; It's better than its predeccesor in almost every way. The gameplay is expanded and improved, the visuals have improved*, the story is phenomenal, you name it. But I absolutely cannot recommend this game simply because It has been 3 YEARS since it released and it is one of the buggiest and worst performing games I have played in the last several years, Comparable to Stalker 2 and Cyberpunk 2077's launch states. Genuinely just terrible. Even after usin

Store page saying: Needed EA app, after long thinking i was like "Yeah, sure, game is only 10€, fine" but i didn't realize it needed EA account (It was also told in store page but got blinded by cheap price) too to play SINGLEPLAYER game. F EA. Only way to play this game is using yarr tactics in future. Refunded.

I saw a lot of people who said they get over 120 fps with DLSS and frame generation, but somehow the frame generation is broken when I enable it. The latency is almost 150ms. 플스 5에서 사 제다이: 서바이버를 재밌게 해서 스팀에서 제다이 오더의 몰락이랑 묶어서 샀습니다. 오더의 몰락은 원활히 잘 실행되어서 오더의 몰락 끝낸 후, 서바이버 다운 받고 한껏 기대하며 게임을 켰지만, 실행한 후부터 계속되는 프라임 드랍에 불편함을 느끼고 유튜브/구글에서 프레임 드랍 해결법도 찾아 해보았지만, 프레임 드랍을 멈출 수는 없었습니다. 그 후 조금 플레이하다 계속되는 프라임 드랍에 안되겠다 싶어 게임을 껐습니다. 출시 된 지 3년이나 지났으면 고칠 만도 한데.3년 동안 욕 꾸준히 먹고 있는거 보면 한숨이 절로 나오네요. 아껴뒀다가 프레임 드랍 고쳐지면 그때 다시 하겠습니다. 제발 고쳐주세요.
난 광선검들고 다 썰어버리고 싶은데 길찾기만 존나게 시키는 게임
★★☆ (플레이) 본인은 전작인 오더의 몰락을 재밌게 플레이 했음 그래서 약간의 기대감을 가지고 플레이 했지만 새롭지 않음 차라리 전작을 추천하겠음
편의 사항은 전작에 비해 좀 개선된거 같다. 최적화 이야기는 워낙 많이 들어서 감안하고 하는데, 맵을 짜증나게 만들었다는게 가장 큰 문제인듯. 매달렸다 공중에서 이곳 저곳 점프로 이동하며 추락사만 수차례 진행하고 도착해보면, 출발 지점과 도착 지점이 너무 근접한데 먼 길을 빙빙 돌아야 하는 구조로 만들었다.

게임 초반엔 좀 재밌게하다가 중반부터는 버그가 너무나 많음. 그냥 작은버그라면 게임에 지장은 안주지만, 아예 진행이 안될정도 그래서 30분전 파일을 로드해야함(이마저도 체크포인트 불러오기도 없음, 타이틀로나가기해서 와야함) 버그걸리면 일단 그자리에서 못움직임 그리고 ui는 너무 불편하고 미니맵도 없고 개인적으로 한손검을 쓰는데 적보다 느려터졌고 공격하고있을땐 가드가 안되고 여러모로 전작보다 퇴보한 느낌을 많이받음. 왜 사람들이 욕하는지 알 것 같음. 일단 전투중 버그도 너무많음 피격판정이라던지, 갑자기 적이 순간이동 한다던지 고쳐진게 이정도라면 예전엔 얼마나 심했을지. 조작감도 솔직히 좋은편은 아님. 스킬도 쓸데없는 것 많고. 아무틍 중간에 버그걸려서 빡쳐서 그냥 안하네요ㅐ
사운드나 비쥬얼이 좋긴 한데 그냥 재미가 없음 1편은 끝까지 했는데 2편은.. 분명 계속하면 뭔가 더 나오긴 할텐데, 그때까지 굳이 계속 해야 되나? 음.. 전혀.. EA 앱 까는 것도 불호고, 게임 용량 ㅈㄴ 큰 것도 불호고..
메인퀘만 쭉쭉 밀면서 하니 재밌네요. 특히 막바지에 몰아치는 상황과 전투는 정말 박진감 넘쳤습니다. 탐험과 서브퀘, 수집은 개인적으로 매력이 떨어졌습니다. 출시 초기 최적화 문제가 많았던 게임이라고 하는데 제가 할 때는 2번 정도 뻗었고 대체로 쾌적하게 플레이할 수 있었습니다.

최적화 문제는 있지만 스토리, 액션, 비주얼 점수는 만점을 줘도 부족하지 않다고 생각합니다. 스타워즈 원작에선 보지 못한 행성의 분위기와 배경을 보여줘서 좋습니다. 칼의 여정이 앞으로 어떻게 진행될지, 그리고 원작과 연계는 어떻게 될지 기대가 됩니다. 다만 컴퓨터 사양이 좋지 않다면 최적화 문제때문에 원활한 게임 진행이 되지 않을 수도 있습니다. 그리고 중간에 버그로 인해서 다시 시작해야 하는 문제도 있었습니다. 발매된지 3년이 다 되어 가는데도 해결되지 않은 문제가 있어서 아쉽지만 그래도 추천합니다. 스타워즈 팬이라면 더더욱 강추입니다.
퍼즐요소 길찾기 초중반 버티다 후반가서 진짜 지친다. 난 액션겜을 원하지 길찾는거 유트브 보면서 까지 해야하나.
1편도 재미있게 해서 질렀는데 역시나 만족. 파쿠르와 퍼즐을 할때는 내가 이걸 왜 샀을까 싶기도 하지만 그래도 길찾기도 쉬워지고 특히 다양한 스탠스를 통한 전투, 디테일 살아있는 스토리 좋았다. 크흐.
후속작도 나쁘지 않게 게임 했음 그리고 화질 개선도 나아짐 그리고 다스베이더에 특별출현 낫 배드
칼이랑 메린이랑 뽀뽀했대요 얼레리꼴레리

전작의 맵은 탐험하다 보면 여기가 어디지, 하는 와중에 맨티스로 돌아가라고 해서 멘붕 오고 그랬는데, 이번 작품은 맵 구조도 그렇고 미니맵도 그렇고 편의성이 대폭 좋아졌습니다. 다만 쥰내 열심히 돌아다녔는데, 지름길 해제되고 보니 시작점 근처이고 그런 뺑뺑이 구조가 좀 많아서 살짝 허무한 감이 있습니다. 맵을 복잡하고 알뜰하게 쓴 거죠. 저는 전작의 맵도 좋았던 입장이라 뭐가 더 낫다고 하긴 어렵네요. 스토리 좋고 캐릭터성 좋고 눈도 즐겁습니다. 라세 뽕도 제대로군요. 스워 팬 아니어도 충분히 재밌게 하실 수 있습니다. 팬이면 더 좋을 거고요. 게임 내, 칼 커스텀에 제다이 옷 어두운 색 입히고 바람머리 헤어 하니까, 아나킨 같아져서 아주 설렙니다. 아직 다 깨기 전입니다. 아껴 하는 중.

An amazing follow up to Jedi: Fallen Order! Pros: -The action is great and improved upon in every way from the last game. -The graphics are stunning. (4K OLED really shines) -The soundtrack HITS. -Level design is great, serious improvement in post-story navigation from Fallen Order. Cons: -Performance is not good. It's worse than Fallen Order. UE4 title, lots of stutters, random frame drops - I am on a 9070xt system. There are mods to mostly fix the stuttering though! -The story is not nearly as

Took me a couple of tries to get through this game. The intro and later sections are pretty solid but some chunks in the middle felt more sluggish and I lost interest. The gameplay has some solid souls-lite with some fun boss fights. I was having some framerate issues and entering loading spots sometimes tanked performance. Overall, Jedi Survivor is solid, and if you're really into Star Wars, will scratch that itch.

Performance issues aside, I had a great time with this game, I enjoyed the story much more than the first game, loved the characters and the locations, traversal tech and combat felt better. Here's hoping we've got one more Cal Kestis adventure on the way to end this story.

엔딩 보고 적는다. 1. NVIDIA앱에서 해상도 모니터에 맞게 해라. 내 모니터가 QHD인데 4K로 자동 설정해버리는 바람에 프레임드랍 엄청났었는데 QHD로 내리자마자 쾌적해졌음. 최적화가 안좋은 만큼 옵션 타협은 좀 하는거 추천 2. 일단 무조건 메인부터 다 밀어라. 사이드 수집품 이것저것 모으려면 이동기가 필요한데 메인을 밀어야 이동기가 생긴다. 그 부분을 미리 알려줬으면 좋았을텐데 절대로 갈 수 없는 곳으로 가려고 낭비하는 시간이 너무 많았음. 그 점 제외하면 재밌는 게임임. 스타워즈 팬이 아니라도 꽤 재밌게 할 수 있고 스타워즈 팬이라면 미치지 않을까 싶다.

오더의 몰락을 몇년 전에 엔딩 보고 이번에 서바이버 엔딩을 봤다 전작보다 많은 면에서 좋아졌지만 3D 맵의 시인성은 여전히 거지 같아서 길찾기에 헤매기 쉽상이다 그래서 서브는 포기하고 메인 스토리로만 쭉 달렸지만 길찾기가 짜증날뿐 액션이나 성장 요소, 스토리는 좋은 편이다 다만 최적화가 좋지 않은게 느껴질만큼 컷씬에서 프레임 하락이나 팅기는 단점이 있는데 팅기는 부분은 화면을 전체화면이 아닌 전체화면-창모드로 하면 해결된다 그래도 스타워즈 팬이고 전작을 해봤다면 서바이버를 해보는걸 추천!
스타워즈 덕후로서 이 정도면 잘 했다. 최적화 이슈도 예전에 비하면 많이 고친듯.

1편에 이어 2편도 엔딩보고 소감을 적음. 전체적으로 전작보다 많이 발전하였고 재밌다. 이겜은 먼가 엄청난 스토리가 있는것은 아닌데, 뭐랄까 연출을 잘한듯하다. 1편때도 그랬지만 100%달성을 하지말고 메인 스토리 위주로 밀고 나가면 재밌다. 아쉬운 점이라면 분량이 좀 아쉽고 서브퀘스트가 크게 와닿지 않는 것 같다. 부디 3편은 최적화 문제등 아무탈 없이 깔끔하게 출시되어 유종의 미를 거두길 바라며 3편을 기다린다. 마무리가 어떻게 될지 기대된다. 추천 쾅!

title: Dave the Diver
critic_response:
다이브 인 데이브는 아름다운 픽셀 아트 그래픽과 다양한 활동을 제공하여 플레이어에게 쾌적한 경험을 제공합니다. 게임의 다양한 장르 조합은 플레이어에게 새로운 경험을 제공합니다. 플레이어들은 도전적인 다이빙을 완료하고 '쿡스타'에서 구체적인 호평을 받는 것을 즐깁니다. 다만, 한 리뷰는 마우스와 키보드로의 조작이 익숙하지 않을 수 있다고 언급합니다. 그러나 한 리뷰는 조작의 어려움과 같은 문제를 언급하기도 합니다.
장점: 다양한 장르 조합으로 새로운 경험을 제공합니다. / 도전적인 다이빙을 완료하고 '쿡스타'에서 구체적인 호평을 받는 것을 즐길 수 있습니다.
단점: 마우스와 키보드로의 조작이 익숙하지 않을 수 있습니다. / 한 리뷰는 게임이 혁신적이지 않다고 언급합니다.
critic_contexts: Dave the Diver is an excellent, enormous title that is best left as vague as possible. Go in without looking too deeply into it and be ready for dozens of hours of some of the best that video gaming has to offer.
Dave the Diver is an eventful experience where every new dive is an adventure of its own that always come hand in hand with an unexpected plot developments.

In a world full of fear and uncertainty, Dave the Diver is here to offer a lighthearted story and engaging gameplay. It doesn’t try to follow trends or emulate big AAA titles. It doesn’t have an in-game shop to try to get you to spend more money. It’s simply a silly, fun game about a diver named Dave, and I can’t put it down.

Dave the Diver is an absolute must-play if you are in the mood for some sushi and relaxation. It has its tense moments, but with its beautiful surroundings, an absurd amount of activities, and just pure hearted and wholesome nature, you will be sucked into this magical lagoon of heavenly food and fun.

Dave the Diver is the right indie game at the right time. In a year full of great releases, Mintrocket shows us the worth of a simple and relaxed experience, that truly feels like a pleasure to sit down and play.

This is demanding work, but the game’s distinct but complementary loops of playful labour are highly compelling. The satisfaction of completing a challenging dive without needing to be rescued, then watching the rave reviews on “Cooksta” pour in, is profound. Stylish, witty and exquisitely designed, Dave the Diver uses several hooks to achieve its goal, while establishing the relationship between the food we eat and the world from which its harvested with useful urgency.

Dave The Diver is a true indie gem. The story acts as the glue that holds things together while most of the weight of the game is on the shoulders of gameplay mechanics that are gradually added, new areas that are unlocked in each chapter of the story, and new weapons and equipment that can be found in boxes scattered in the Blue Hole. This game is a simple delight like a cup of coffee in the morning or an ice cream in the summer heat; its true value is felt only when you taste it yourself.
Dave the Diver is a great adventure with many elements from other genres, so it never gets boring. Only the control with mouse and keyboard takes some getting used to.

Dave the Diver is a whacky, 2D/3D game that mixes several genres to form a fun adventure with silly characters and never a shortage of things to do. Each trip into the mysterious Blue Hole is different, and you’ll have plenty of quests and upgrades to work on. Spend your days diving, and your nights running a sushi bar. If you are looking for a great balance of not too hard, not too easy, Dave the Diver is it. While some of the missions can get kind of hairy, managing your resources is what it’s

Dave the Diver is a surprisingly excellent game. Its beautiful pixel art is joined by plenty of activities, not very deep, but fun all the same. The weird underwater world is charming and invites discovery. The characters are interesting, with memorable quests. The tempo is a bit unequal sometimes, but the game offers plenty of content nonetheless.

2023 didn't really need a game that good. It was already a banger of a year for video games. But here we are, with Dave the Diver being one of the most fun, fulfilling, wholesome, and sometime downright beautiful games that came out this year.

Dave the Diver is the best thing I’ve played this year and I wish I had more such stellar games in my life to help me escape the grey reality. Its gameplay loop is terrifyingly addictive and the “one more dive syndrome” lies in wait for anyone who decides to check out this dark horse of the GOTY race.
Wholesome, harmonious and completely unwilling to settle, this is one of the most generous games in years.

Dave the Diver’s unusual genre blend creates an infinitely satisfying and addictive gameplay loop, which, thanks to the constant influx of new mechanics and game content, feels fresh and rewarding until the very end.

Dave the Diver deserves to be a hit of summer 2023. He does not innovate and does not offer new gameplay mechanics, he is "only" concerned with giving ten hours of superfine quality and succeeds with a style and lightness from which one immediately becomes addicted. It is a much more accurate work than it seems, an experience in magnificent balance between its two souls, characterized by an immense heart and continually able to amaze you as it drags you deeper and deeper into the abyss of the "a

Dave the Diver is one of the most interesting games of this year. From such a simple concept – bring some fish to your restaurant! – the game keeps building on its gameplay and deepening the experience, captivating the player in an enjoyable and magnetic loop that you will find hard to jump off from.

Dave the Diver delves into the hauntingly beautiful and multi-genre masterpiece that captivates players with its engaging game play and ever-changing dynamics. The game seamlessly combines various elements, from fishing and selling sushi to battling sea creatures, making it an enjoyable and unique experience. The story is light, humorous, and entertaining, keeping players engaged throughout. The game's flexibility allows players to choose their preferred game play style, whether it's diving for
Dave the Diver is an extremely generous game that hooks you with all its gifts you can't refuse.

Games are often referred to as mixing the right ingredients and mixing the best recipe. But in the case of Dave the diver, the ingredients and recipes on the plate are the most important thing, not metaphorically, but completely practically. And the resulting food tastes so good that you will lick your fingers.

It is one of these games that is the full package. It has engaging mechanics that really make you want to play, it has a visual style that really highlights everything the game is trying to do, and it has some of the best and most varied music I've heard in a while.

Dave the Diver is a game with a distinctive style that demonstrates the potential of the medium with its comprehensive and varied gameplay. While the late-game mechanics may feel repetitive in the full version, the enjoyable fishing experience for the first ten hours makes it worth the price of admission.

DAVE THE DIVER is a fantastic adventure RPG. Can you take to the water and solve the mystery of the "Blue Hole"? Explore new locations, complete each quest, and help in the restaurant. I loved the aesthetics, the evolving gameplay, and never-ending content. Furthermore, it is great on Steam Deck.

Dave The Diver very much deserves the enormous success it’s received in its first month, selling over a million copies, and hopefully making developers Mintrocket enormously rich. They’ve created something really special, an RPG-meets-Diner Dash-meets gentle SCUBA sim, that manages to feel utterly crammed to the gills with things to do, yet joyfully relaxing to play.

With one of the most addictive gameplay loops of the year, Dave the Diver combines diving roguelike and sushi bar management with the skill of a master sushi chef. Its plucky irreverent tone, fun writing, constant minigames, and well-balanced drip-feed of mechanics made for an experience I was happy to sink many hours into.

Dave the Diver combines two excellent gameplay elements, but still feels like more than the sum of its parts. Deep sea diving and sushi restaurant management are equally enjoyable and complex, making for a supremely addicting gameplay loop. Not only is it the surprise hit of the summer, but it’s one we’ll be having a discussion about when it’s time to look back on 2023’s best games.

There’s basically nothing to find fault with in Dave the Diver. Did I have trouble mastering a few of the cooking minigames, and an easy mode for the rhythm games would have been appreciated – but that’s a drop in the ocean compared to what it does right. Filling out the fish index, sneaking through a secret base, gambling in a merfolk casino – everywhere I turned there was something new to try, and all of it was built with care and wit. There’s a shocking amount to do here, all of it is worth s
Charming, deep, and constantly surprising, Dave the Diver is packed with activities and full of heart.

You might question whether a sushi restaurant management sim crossed with underwater diving is your cup of tea, but that would be missing the point. Dave the Diver is a unique and memorable vacation away from expectations, and it’s the very fact that you don’t usually play games like this that makes it so satisfying.
Quotation forthcoming.

A triumph in just about every way, Dave the Diver is undoubtedly going to be one of 2023’s biggest success stories. This gorgeous adventure keeps you hooked no matter what you’re doing. Whether you’re serving up sushi, catching fish or exploring the depths of the oceans for long-lost secrets, you’ll struggle to pull yourself away. We just love it. Even if it does leave us craving sushi – but it’s a small price to pay.

Dave the Diver is an absolute delight to play. Its core gameplay loop is wonderfully relaxing, but before too long players will be finding all the different options in its nooks and crannies. It’s surely one of the best games of the year so far, and players will love taking on the role of our goatee'd hero.

Dave the Diver is much more than the sum of its individual components. It lacks originality and is never particularly deep in its various mechanics, but the end result is a game with excellent pacing, fun, dense with content and continues to come up with new ideas even after hours and hours.
Fun, funny and really well designed, Dave the Diver is an addictive adventure that combines many elements that shouldn’t work together, but somehow, they do.
A wonderfully strange and imaginative indie adventure that delights in terms of its visuals, storytelling, and enjoyably unpredictable gameplay.
know I’m already hooked in, and it’s quickly become one of the best titles I’ve played this year.

Dave the Diver from Mint Rocket is something to behold. The game features exploration, collection, side quests, an RPG backbone, and a restaurant simulator, all of which are beautifully balanced and connected. This is by far one of the better games of 2023 and shouldn’t be overlooked.

And the prize for the 'Most Game 2023' goes to South Korea: Dave the Diver tirelessly surprises with new ideas and keeps its catchy basic elements as fresh as good sushi. One of the year's most addictive games.

What makes Dave the Diver work so well is wanting to see what comes next. In another game that just tacked on mechanics and plot devices with no plan, it could feel like the padding around a threadbare premise. But Dave’s kitchen sink approach somehow feels like a perfectly logical, if absurd, escalation — like a Tim Robinson sketch in game form. It’s a teetering pile of mechanics and minigames that never gets around to collapsing because the balancing act is just too much fun.

Dave the Diver is captivating. It throws a lot of ideas at players, most of which are great. The dominant mechanics, diving and restaurant work, have depth and plenty of surprises. Even after many hours of harpoon fishing, it’s still thrilling to evaluate a target, aim, fire, struggle with it, and repeat until the result is extra sushi material. The many narrative threads don’t always connect with each other meaningfully, and there are some side activities that don’t impress. But Dave the Diver

Every moment in Dave the Diver feels unique the first time through. The constant changing of side-missions, the cutscenes when upgrading weapons or fish dishes, and the eccentric cast of characters, all blend together so well into a game that, when viewed just from the surface, might seem shallow. But players willing to dive into the meat of the game will find even more enjoyment than they bargained for, especially with its official 1.0 release. It’s a single-player adventure with heart, good mu
Whether you're diving into the depths or managing a sushi restaurant, Dave the Diver is full of ideas and engaging from start to finish.

Dave the Diver was crafted by video game visionaries with a pure love of fun. I have never seen a game jampack so much into one seemingly tiny title and somehow make it all work with the clarity of something smaller in scope. Dave the Diver doesn’t just deserve your attention, it demands it, and if you’ve been waiting for your sign to dive into this addicting game, I’m telling you now it’s time to take the plunge.

Dave the Diver knows from the very first scene what it wants to be, and from the get-go it's firing on all its available cylinders. You get a fantastically complex gaming experience here, with a self-awareness that would suit many major studios.

Dave the Diver shows a perfect connection between two genres, Adventure and Tycoon. While adventure element is deep, Tycoon part gives a speedy, hectic fun. On top of that, well handled B-rated vibe, attractive characters, and various mini-games are creating a synergy throughout the game play.

Dave The Diver is a tremendous and unforgettable RPG adventure that I can’t put down. The story and characters are full of heart and charm, the underwater exploration and restaurant management are easy to lose time to, and the sheer number of weird minigames and other surprises that are packed in the 30-hour adventure ensure things never grow stale. Even with wimpy bosses, simple puzzles, and a fair number of bugs, Dave The Diver has so many other interesting and intriguing things going on that
Dave The Diver will end up being one of those fundamental titles that people talk about in the same breath as Stardew Valley, where it achieves on nearly every front.

Great game, never gets old. The gameplay loop is also very satisfying.

The creators clearly had fun making this game, which shows from the humor, all the unique characters, and all the different mechanics and plotlines thrown in, which is why I really wanted to like it. 압긍 기대에 못 미침. 미니게임과 추가 컨텐츠가 계속 나오는데 정작 메인 컨텐츠가 약한 느낌. 너무 많은 컨텐츠를 넣으려다 모든 컨텐츠의 깊이가 얕아짐. 어인족부터 하품 나오고, 귀찮음. 세일하더라도 추천하기 애매.

초밥집 운영을 하고 싶어하는 플레이어도, 탐험을 하고 싶어하는 플레이어도, 물고기와 전투하고 싶은 플레이어 그 어느쪽도 만족시킬수 없는 콘텐츠 구성이다. 전반적으로 질이 높지 않은 구성요소들이 유기성 없이 나열돼있어서 하나의 게임이 아니라 차라리 미니게임 모음집이라고 생각하면 된다. 할인할때 13000원으로 사서 개꿀이다 싶었는데 하면 할수록 13000원에 이 게임보다 플레이 타임은 짧아도 훨씬 더 집중해서 재밌게 할 수 있는 게임이 더 많다는 생각이 든다. 압긍인 이유를 모르겠음.
사운드가 진짜 잘 뽑힙. 도트랑 그래픽도 너무 좋음. 처음에는 진짜 감탄하면서 플레이 했었음. 하지만 경영 요소가 부족해서 그런지 가면 갈 수록 좀 물림. 스토리 자체는 잘 짜여져 있지만 퀘스트들이 너무 반복적인 느낌..? 그래서 생각 날 때마다 한 두시간 씩 가볍게 즐기는 중. 몰아서 하기보다는 여유를 두고 힐링한다는 느낌으로 플레이하면 재밌음 !!
데이브는 낮에 목숨 걸고 바다 들어가고 분점 관리에 농사, 어업까지 하다가 밤에는 장사까지.초인임?
너무 재밌게 했구요, 초반부터 중반까지는 정말 빠져들면서 했지만 이후 종업원, 가게 업그레이드 싹다 밀어버리니깐 흥미가 떨어져서 게임 켜는게 너무 힘들게 다가왔어요 이젠 안켜게되네요. 살지말지는 본인판단이겠지만 저는 이 정도 가격대면 사도 괜찮다 생각해요 세일 자주하니깐 반값할인할때 구매해보세요^00^
유튜브로 먼저 접하고 내 취향인 걸 알았지만, 공부하면서 해야되는 게임류인 것 같아서 1년 넘게 망설임. 스팀 할인을 참을 수 없어서 결제하고 플레이했는데 뭐 하나 거를게 없음. 사실 싸우는 거 별로 안좋아해서, 보스 사냥할 때마다 스트레스 수치가 올라가긴 했지만.. 메이플스토리처럼 끊이지 않는 퀘스트와 식당 운영이 중독성 있어서 계속 하게 됨.

Peak game. I love the big variety of game mechanics here, some characters, beautiful graphics and the atmosphere that this game has. Plus, I've never been so invested in getting all of the achievements like I was in "Dave the diver" :D Sure, some parts were frustrating and confusing, but it's forgivable as there are only a few of such moments in the entire game, which is very long. I would recommend playing "Dave the diver" to people who like casual fishing games with quirky characters and fun s

So before I leave my review let me just say, I am not the biggest gamer, sometimes I can play for a hour or two and other times I will turn my PC on, look at my games, and then turn it of again. This was not the case with Dave the Diver, from the moment of installation I was harpooned and reeled in, from the various stunning ocean backdrops, vast amount of fish you can catch, boss battles, the sushi restaurant, story line, and the list goes on, Dave the Diver definitely ticked all my b
this game is really fun :D there's a fun story and lots of stuff to do. i recommend ! !
Fun game, I like that there's so many things to collect and that it switches between diving in the ocean and managing the restaurant. The story is interesting with a bit of humour.

I really love how immersive the game feels, and it’s easily one of the best RPGs I’ve played. The lore was surprisingly really good, and the cutscenes are so funny and entertaining. Bancho’s cutscenes are honestly the best hahaha. The gameplay is also very engaging — it can get a bit repetitive sometimes, but it’s still really enjoyable and worth playing. ⸜(｡˃ ᵕ ˂)⸝♡

Went into Dave the Diver expecting a Dredge-type game and quickly realised how different it is. Hard to describe a game like this because there's so much to do and so many games within this game that you could spend countless hours on. Dave does most things well, story is surprisingly good, characters are interesting, music is great (LET THE SONGS PLAY WHEN I EXIT THE APP), and the art style is solid. I do wish combat was more enjoyable (outside of some of the boss fights) and there were more op

Dave The Diver was a great game to sit down and rewind after a tough day with. There's tons to do and tons to collect, the game can never truly be found boring in my opinion. If you've found yourself ever considering to playing, I'd suggest you give it a try, its more fun then you'll ever know.
THE ARTSTYLE IS SO DAMN BEAUTIFUL and the gameplay loop is fun too, would recommend to anyone

Heard it was surprisingly good, and heard right! The graphics are charming - a mix of 3D models and 16-bit (maybe voxels too) - with some dynamic music and intriguing characters. The only cons I've encountered are a lot of screen tear (no V-sync option‽) and that there needs to be more player agency when it comes to cutscenes/plot. By this I mean that it's not always possible to pause, safely exit or go AFK because there's an onslaught of tutorials and events which require constant player input

probably one of the best games ive ever played tbh. you wouldnt expect such a simple looking game to have THAT much content but theres so much to do and the story is actually pretty cool. i love everything about the game but the BEST thing about it is def the little cut scenes you get with the characters, the art style is very light hearted and intriguing (coming from a serial cut scene skipper)
Incredibly made, extremely polished, funny, and the story was super fun.
This is a very addictive game. The objective and the wonderful underwater world will keep you hooked. My favourite are the cut scenes of Bancho

A very shallow game about deep-sea diving. It's not a horrible time, the writing is semi humorous but the same jokes get tiring after a while. The gameplay is a collection of minigames half of which you'd rather just ignore. And it's a "roguelike" where the level is 95% the same every run so all they achieve by being "roguelike" is artificially lengthening the game by getting you to re-tread the same waters at the start of each session. All in all I finished the game and didn't suffer doing it,

An enormously fun little game about . scuba diving. Diving, hunting fish, and running a Sushi restaurant. It uses a beautiful retro pixilated art style. There is a story to progress, but most of all, I just love the days of quietly slipping into the sea and exploring the depths. At night, the restaurant is open, providing an entirely different kind of game play that can be at times, frustrating, but at times surprisingly rewarding. How best to describe the game? You are an obese Athlete, a so called diver. You need to annhilate opposing enemy fish with a device that they call harpoon. Annihilated fish is then repurposed by some kind of a non-asian ninja into food that you need to offer to other humans. Your whale-like body attracts dolphins. they are your friends. you do not shoot friends with a harpoon. only the other fish. I am about to sort of understand the game, I think. It is a slow process for sure.

The visuals are beautiful, and the gameplay loop is simple but surprisingly addictive. It can get repetitive after a while, yet it had me playing for 10 hours straight when I first downloaded it. My interest faded quickly afterward, but I still had a great time with it.
