Dave the Diver is a simple yet engaging game. Gameplay is split into two parts, the diving and restaurant management halves, and both are well done. The diving part is the more interesting part, essentially you explore the depths, catching fish and having adventures. There's things you need to find in order to fulfill certain missions, and other things you need to retrieve to participate in research, that helps fund your activities. Money is used to upgrade your equipment and weapons, the latter

Dave the Diver sure is a unique game. The characters are lovable, the storyline is engaging - despite being very cheesy at times -, and the gameplay is friendly to new and experienced gamers. It's a longer game than I'm used to, but it really progresses at the player's pace. Could I have completed it quicker.likely, but I enjoyed the freedom of not being rushed from task to task. The game's pacing felt very organic and player-driven. I was surprised by how many mechanics were present in the game

**Dave the Diver — A Surprisingly Deep hidden pearl* Let me get this out of the way immediately: Dave is not your typical video game hero. He's a stocky, barrel-chested everyman who looks more at home at an all-you-can-eat sushi bar than plunging into the ocean depths — and I absolutely love him for it. In a medium obsessed with chiselled protagonists, Dave's portly frame is refreshing, charming, and oddly endearing. He waddles, he sweats, he huffs. He's *us*, and that's the point. But don't let

A very fun, beautiful, casual, and addictive game.. I can't wait to dive again to hunt other types of fish after helping out at the sushi restaurant the night before. The comedy style is also funny. If only there were more diving locations and players could dive more than 800 m.. Playing this game also makes the desire to eat sushi or other seafood very high..

To be honest, if you don't control yourself and let your body get carried away by the fun of this game, your health will suffer. Unbelievably cozy and chill game. No wonder it's so well-received. As soon as you understand the flow of the game, it'll hook you in like a damn fish. 처음엔 이 게임이 솔직히 그 정돈가 싶다가, 재미는 있네 싶다가, 결국 엔딩까지 보고 노가다 하다보면 이 단순한 방식의 게임을 엔딩까지 즐기게 하기 위해 제작진들이 얼마나 고민했는지 느껴지는 게임. 잘 만든 게임이지만 다 즐기고 나서 생각해보니 데이브 중심으로 전개되는 게임의 흐름이 재밌는 거였고, 게임 시스템은 재미의 보조 역할이란 생각이 많이 든다. 좀 직설적으로 이야기하면 엔딩을 딱 보고나서, 다시 인게임 보트 안 화면을 마주치는 순간, 갑자기 게임이 정나미가 확 떨어진다. 성장할 여지는 아직 있고 진짜 모든 컨텐츠 종결까진 먼 거 알겠는데, 너무 똑같은 일의 반복만이 남았다는 걸 바로 느낄 수 밖에 없다. 물고기를 잡고, 식재료를 구하고, 음식을 팔고, 더 크고 더 비싼 것들을 팔면 되는데 이 안에서 변수란 게 사실상 없다. 짧게 설명하자면 이 게임의 성장 방식은 거의 대부분이 정해진 가격이 있고, 재료도 하루에 얻을 최선이 사실상 정해져 있다. 무슨 소리냐. 엔딩 이후론 말

title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
critic_response:
클레어 옵스큐어: 익스페디션 33은 높은 수준의 완성도를 갖춘 게임으로 평가된다. 스토리 측면에서도 감동적인 경험을 제공하며, 개인적이고 세심한 주제로 깊은 울림을 준다. 전투 시스템은 전략과 도전이 완벽하게 결합된 형태로, 플레이어에게 만족감을 준다. 하지만 일부 이용자들은 전투가 어려울 수 있으며, UI가 혼란스럽거나 레벨 디자인이 약하다는 의견을 제시하기도 했다. 종합적으로 이 게임은 높은 완성도와 다양한 장점으로 인해 올해의 게임 후보 중 하나로 꼽힌다.
단점: UI가 혼란스럽거나 레벨 디자인이 약하다는 의견이 있다.
critic_contexts: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a graphically beautiful game, but despite its glowing reputation, it does little to innovate within the RPG genre. The lack of navigation tools makes exploring its world incredibly frustrating, and the story only starts to get interesting near the very end.
Clair Obscur: Expedition 33's Souls-inspired, turn-based combat system can ruin your mood. If you can handle it, you'll discover a rich, surreal world with an interesting story full of emotion.

Expedition 33's story is a touching and highly emotional ride that, even as the stakes increase, always feels personal in the way that it thoughtfully depicts its themes. It's a stunning game, and stands alongside the best in the genre.

Clair Obscur Expedition 33 is a game that checks all the boxes for me in a game of its style and is currently my game of the year, it has an engaging battle system that I enjoy, its world setting is interesting, the cast of characters and the story are interesting, and I don't wish for it to even end.

This is a game we can confidently recommend to all RPG fans — and without a doubt, it's one of the strongest contenders for Game of the Year. But beyond that, it's important to recognize the significance of this project and what its success means for the modern gaming industry as a whole. This is the kind of healthy, grounded approach to game development that we desperately need right now. It’s not just about mechanics or genre — it’s about the philosophy behind the work: a small team of former
Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a masterpiece from start to finish, a beautiful and deeply moving journey.

From thrilling turn-based combat to incredible artistic direction and a truly engrossing story, this is a hard game not to recommend to players of all preferences, and it has easily cemented itself as a contender for my game of the year.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a magical, gripping, and technically impressive RPG that delivers a rich story, clever gameplay, and enchanting atmosphere—despite a few rough edges from its small-studio origins. Fans of complex fantasy and mystery-laden role-playing games will find much to love.
Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is nothing short of a masterpiece. Words can’t fully capture just how remarkable this game truly is. Play it ASAP and let the experience speak for itself.
Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a phenomenal game in almost every aspect. It is engaging, exciting, dramatic, emotional, thought-provoking, and incredibly memorable.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a masterpiece of the turn-based RPG genre, clear indicator of the knowledge and capacity of modern French game-dev scene, and a title that will surely be placed among the best games of 2025. Play it as soon as possible.

With a gripping story, a beautifully written cast of characters, and engaging gameplay that breathes new life into familiar mechanics, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a journey that leaves a lasting mark on both the mind and the heart.
The best JRPG in years - despite coming from France. Hard-hitting, well thought-out and utterly rewarding, Clair Obscur is a beautiful reminder of what truly matters.
Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a mesmerizing RPG masterpiece, combining stunning art, innovative combat, and heart-wrenching storytelling into an unforgettable adventure.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a masterpiece of emotional storytelling, rich in atmosphere in which history and magic intertwine. The game is not afraid to ask hard questions and point out harsh truths, all the while featuring a gripping narrative, believable characters, and an impressive turn-based combat with some novel mechanics.

Just a while ago, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 could have been called a dark horse — a hidden gem with untapped potential. But not anymore. With its official release, it’s proven itself worthy of being a Game of the Year contender.

Clair Obscur Expedition 33 is a game in which every element—strategic depth, the joy of progression, emotional intensity, and audiovisual artistry—is not only solid but also organically interconnected. While it's not perfect, it stands out as a truly exceptional experience.

While it’s not quite perfect, this is a really stunning showing from developer Sandfall Interactive. Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 had me feeling my own mortality (as someone in their early 30s, perhaps it hit a little close to home) and man, I love when a game has the narrative gumption to do that.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is one of those experiences that redefine your concept of what an RPG can be. What Sandfall Interactive has achieved in their debut is simply astonishing: a narrative that captivates you from the first second with adult characters facing complex moral dilemmas; a revolutionary turn-based combat system that transforms each encounter into a strategic dance full of tension; and a visually stunning world that masterfully blends Belle Époque with dark fantasy. The conversa

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a modern RPG masterpiece. Despite coming from a new studio, it combines strong storytelling, captivating ambiance, engaging combat, and a creative progression system. Sandfall Studio surprises by delivering such an ambitious, deep, and artistically consistent project. With a spectacular soundtrack, memorable performances, charming visuals, and refined mechanics, the game easily makes it onto the list of the best of the year. If you enjoy RPGs and are open to experi

The turn-based RPG and debut title from French studio Sandfall Interactive delivers on all of its promises in refreshing the decades-old genre, taking inspiration from games of the past and the Belle Époque era of French history to craft a beautiful, tragic, and memorable experience that should be considered one of the best games of 2025 and a tremendous first entry for a very talented studio.
Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 feels truly unique and artistic, and reminds us how moving, exhilarating, and provocative big-budget adventure games can be.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a tremendous game that has set a new standard for RPGs, the ambitions of smaller teams, and AAA experiences in general. It's an easy game of the year candidate if I've ever seen one. A masterful story born from an exceptional concept.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is one of the best surprises of recent years. An XXL production from a small, passionate team, immersive, polished, addictive gameplay and told to perfection. A real lesson and a new benchmark for all JRPG enthusiasts, it should satisfy all fans of the genre, and particularly the generation that grew up with FF7, 8, 9 and 10, and that has less and less faith in Square Enix to make a really good new JRPG. A slap in the face we won't soon forget, and what's more, it's F

Despite its occasionally confounding UI and weak level design, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is an essential turn-based RPG that is both a love letter to its inspirations and a potential vision of the genre's future.

Clair Obscur is a genuine standout RPG, driven by courage, sacrifice, and the powerful bonds formed in the midst of tragedy. It pushes you to confront loss and hope in equal measure, resonating long after the credits roll. This is a beautifully tragic expedition that demands your emotional investment, repaying it with a story and characters that will reward the time it takes to develop the skills of not taking any damage in combat. This may be a divisive game within the community due to its skil

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a confident new IP, bold in its execution and not afraid to mix old and new ideas. It’s a stunning achievement, born from passion and raised within technical excellence equal to the best in the business. Go in unspoiled and be ready for a truly rewarding experience.

An exciting turn-based RPG that offers a fresh take on the genre from a small French development team with a big heart and a courage to change that we welcome. This is a stunning adventure that you don't want to miss.
Clair Obscur is an all-you-can-eat buffet for RPG lovers.

Sandfall Interactive's debut RPG perfectly fuses the deep strategy of turn-based combat with the satisfying challenge and mastery of real-time action games, resulting in a wholly unique experience that sets itself apart. Together with the game's stellar storytelling, jaw-dropping presentation, and good overall performance, that makes it one of 2025's must-play titles — and a huge release for Xbox Game Pass.

When you factor in the voice talent involved, the visual splendor on offer, and the sheer scale of the whole game, it really feels like Clair Obscur is punching above its weight, considering the comparatively small size of the team at Sandfall. This is a supremely confident game that reinterprets familiar mechanics with an imaginative eye. Its combat is a consistent delight, delivering thrills with thoughtful strategy and reactive action in equal measure, complementing a heartfelt story of share

There are words we don't dare use too often: due to their overused nature, the very essence of their meaning seems to have lost its substance over the years. "Masterpiece" is one of them. And yet, we feel like breaking the rule for Clair Obscur: Expeditions 33. We have here a lesson in scriptwriting mastery, artistic vision, and gameplay complexity rarely achieved in a game of this genre. The few small technical flaws (potentially corrected with a day one patch) cannot overcome the excellence an

I know I'm going out on a limb, but Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 may not be a masterpiece for everyone, but it comes close without fear. A superfine narrative, a galvanized and well-optimized combat system, a dedicated soundtrack that hits all the most tense moments. I entered the sacrificial tunnel of Expedition 33 and came out with tears of joy for how much I savored throughout the adventure. About thirty hours of gameplay that have been a continuous joy and I am fully aware that I will play ju

Driven by their passion for role-playing games, the young team at Sandfall Interactive has transformed this first attempt into a stroke of genius. With the tip of their brush, dipped in paint materialized thanks to the talent of their teams, the Montpellier studio has proven that a video game with a modest budget can overshadow the greatest RPGs. I had high expectations for Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 and the adventure has met all my expectations, managing to constantly amaze me thanks to its un

Clair Obscur Expedition 33 is a monumental work, perfectly balanced in length and pace of play, with an engaging combat system and a progression system among the best in its category. Equipped with an exciting story, full of twists and a profound final message, it will bewitch anyone who decides to embark on Expedition 33. We wholeheartedly recommend it to any fan of "Japanese-style" RPGs.

If I were a teenager still, this game would ingrain itself into my brain the same way Final Fantasy VII did in 1997. Right now, when there are numerous predictions about the death of single player games, when creativity is shackled by the sales department, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a true gem worth every praise.

Sandfall Interactive’s debut is a triumph. Everything about Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is either a fresh reboot to soothe the traditionalist’s soul or an exciting, bold leap into new territory, and the result is a piece of art that pulled me in and refused to let go.

Very occasionally, I'll play an RPG that makes me feel ten again. Rebirth. Cris Tales. Revisiting Suikoden. Years come, the big number ticks down, and comfortable appreciation replaces the spellbound enchantment of being told a story, of being swept off to a new world. Of playing Final Fantasy 8 in that special edition shirt that Ben Starr likes to wear that I wish I'd kept because I bet it's worth a bloody fortune now. You wait for a game to bring you back there, mostly certain you've moved pas

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has exceeded every one of my expectations. Sandfall Interactive's debut title is one of the greatest accomplishments in gaming, delivering a well-paced, polished, and unforgettable experience. From the incredibly impactful and deeply emotional narrative to the enjoyable, challenging, and rewarding combat, Expedition 33 delivers on all fronts. I wanted an old-school RPG that sticks true to its roots while taking advantage of modern game design. I got that and so much m

What makes Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 worthy of recognition isn't just how well everything works, but the fact that it all sticks with you. The characters, the music, the world design, the emotional throughline that runs underneath all the strategy and spectacle — none of it ever fades into the background. Clair Obscur is the kind of game that lingers after the credits roll, not because of how much it offers, but because of how much it gets right. It’s rare to see a game so mechanically confide

Blasting the door down and becoming an instant masterpiece classic, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 defies all expectations and ambitions and then some. Looking at decades of genre refinement and somehow still managing to innovate, creating immensely tactical, thought-provoking and rich combat is nothing short of a miracle achieved at the hands of Sandfall Interactive. Accompanying it is an entirely fresh and magnificent world full of wonders and beauty, even despite its intricate and often harrowin

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a modern creation deeply infused with the soul of classic JRPGs, carving out a bold new possibility for the genre's future. Its QTE-infused combat system brings a rare sense of impact and fluidity to turn-based battles, while boss encounters inspired by Souls-like design offer room for skill-based progression.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is one of those double A titles that blurs the lines between it and the industry's biggest hitters. It isn't without issues, but as a debut title for Sandfall Interactive, it's an incredibly impressive showing that's well worth checking out.

But it’s also a story of perseverance. Of pushing forward even when it feels impossible. Of carrying the weight of sorrow without letting it define you. And daring to believe in a future no one has ever seen – simply because someone has to. And that’s what makes Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 so quietly powerful, and such a stroke of brilliance in this medium.

The game delivers an intense, innovative turn-based combat experience with a rich atmosphere and gorgeous visuals, although the repetitive level design and a story that loses focus halfway through diminish its overall impact.

What a surprise Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 was! Expectations around Sandfall Interactive's RPG were high, but the end result exceeded all expectations. Clair Obscur: Expedition 33! a solid game, which on the technical side is no match for much richer productions. Gustave's adventure amazes through a mature and adult use of storytelling and thanks to a combat system that mixes turns and real time, then opening up a huge range of strategic possibilities with the introduction of Pictos. From the m

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 took me by surprise and I'm glad it did. Sandfall Interactive has managed to create a delightful RPG that combines modern influences with an irresistible old school sensibility. The story is another of its great assets, enhanced by an excellent cast who, with a fantastic performance, help paint this canvas that I won't soon forget.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a competent new JRPG with which genre fans will likely be pleased. However, it does very little to iterate on the foundation laid by those that came before it. Instead, it’s content to offer its flavor of what has worked before.

Expedition 33 is magnificent in every possible way. I was in love with it whole time and while playing I’ve became so engaged with characters and the story surround them I forgot the flow of time entirely. This is clearly the future for JRPG’s and it’s sense of scale is outstanding. Definitely a classic for years to come.

Clair Obscur : Expedition 33 is a wonderful game, an absolute gem. Its universe of rare elegance and its deep gameplay are not its only qualities because it also offers a fantastic emotional experience that will make you laugh as much as cry, with characters so well written and so "real" that you would think you knew them, absolutely sublime music and a masterful, Dantesque staging. A lesson in video games!

The magic of Expedition 33 is that it never gets old. You’re always discovering something, meeting new characters, learning skills, finding secret areas, battling hidden bosses, or discovering remarkable places. Through it all, Expedition 33 tells an incredible story of defiance in the face of impossible odds, discovery, loss, love, death, and belief. It’s the kind of game that reminds you what games can be, the kind with characters you’ll remember long after the credits roll. Sometimes art come
Clair Obscur is emotional, challenging and intense - a role-playing game that stands out in terms of atmosphere and story.
The way Clair Obscur’s various mechanical ideas fit together is exhilarating, to the point where you’ll find yourself running around looking for fights not very long into the game.

An audiovisual delight and a surprisingly deep role-playing game: With its story, style, and excellent combat system, Clair Obscur breathes a breath of fresh, croissant-scented air into the JRPG genre.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 isn’t just a great RPG, it’s a generational one — and picture proof of the power of how a team’s clear passion, when used just right, can be used to create something transcendent.

Clair Obscure: Expedition 33 is not necessarily an innovative game. However, it is a great value proposition because its excellence lies in the flawless execution of its ideas and their implementation.
Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a beautiful RPG with exciting combat, excellent voice work, and a fantastic score, but design constraints may turn off genre fans seeking a grand adventure.
Clair Obscur: Expedition 33's gorgeously realized world is let down by its emphasis on combat, marred by a few fatal stumbles.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a strong debut title for Sandfall Interactive, with compelling characters and an original, unsettling story that’s part allegory, part surreal fantasy. Its turn-based/real-time hybrid combat is well thought out and grows more interesting as the game progresses. Some narrative pacing issues and repetitive enemy encounters pull Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 from the brink of greatness. Still, it remains a unique and engaging take on both fantasy storytelling and JRPGs.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a cinematic RPG experience that showcases both visual and audio prowess. The tone is depressing, but is countered by the beauty of this world and the hope from its characters to succeed on their mission. The combat is engaging enough as it relies on Quicktime Events and a parry system to keep players engaged. This is a solid leap in this turn-based RPG genre that normally would get stale due to the repetition of combat. The overall experience is a slow burn, but co

환상적인 게임이지만 일직선상으로 엔딩으로만 달려가는 게임이라 , 다른것을 해볼까? 전투를 또 해볼까? 하는 마음은 들지 않아서 아쉬운 게임.. 스팀 평가에 예 아니요 밖에 없어서 어쩔수 없이 아니요를 줬지만 예 아니요 중간 사이가 있었다면 중간을 줬을 게임 .. 엔딩 역시 호불호가 갈릴듯 한데 환상적인 비쥬얼과 분위기로 만족은 줬지만.. 여운이 남는다던지 정말 좋았다 라는 마음은 들지 않는 , 그냥 좋았네 멋지네 정도라 아쉽다. 게임 내내 엔딩을 향하고 싶고 .."와 환상적인 비쥬얼" "와 전투가 재밌네" 라는 생각은 들지만 전투를 또 해야하나? 라는 생각이 내내 들면서 전투를 하고 있는 게임

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a game about being stuck but unaware of it. It is a hollow spectacle, mechanically satisfying and with striking music and visuals, but it's let down by weak writing, by a disconnected mess of a third act, and by its strong points' repetitiveness. But what's worst is how [i]frustratingly close[/i] it keeps coming to being coherent and meaningful, to having [i]interesting things to say[/i]. The game's themes are creativity and grief and trauma, and losing oneself in

"귓가를 맴도는 황홀한 선율 속에서, 턴제는 지루하다는 편견을 완벽하게 패링해 냅니다." 평소 넓은 오픈월드를 누비며 다채로운 빌드를 깎아가는 재미에 푹 빠져 살던 제게, '턴제'라는 장르는 전혀 어울리지 않는 옷 같았습니다. 하지만 이 게임은 제가 알던 고루한 턴제와는 완전히 달랐습니다. 턴제임에도 패링과 반격이라는 짜릿한 액션 요소가 더해져 매 턴마다 긴장감을 놓을 수 없었고, 무엇보다 제가 사랑하는 오픈월드 기반의 자유로운 탐험과 수많은 빌드 조합이 완벽한 시너지를 이뤘습니다. 미니맵이 없다는 소식에 처음엔 조금 걱정했지만, 직관적으로 뻗은 길과 발견하기 쉽게 배치된 아이템들 덕분에 오히려 맵을 구석구석 맨몸으로 개척하는 순수한 탐험의 재미를 흠뻑 느낄 수 있었습니다. 출시된 지 벌써 1년이나 지났는데, '왜 이제야 이 명작을 켰을까' 하는 짙은 아쉬움마저 밀려올 정도로 압도적인 재미를 선사합니다. 물론 훌륭한 서사도 빼놓을 수 없습니다. 게임 내내 펼쳐지는 스토리가 깊은 여운
정말 영화 한편 보는것 같군요 게임성도 우수하고 인생의 교훈을 주는것 같네요 정말 괴롭고 슬픈일이 있어도 캔버스(중독되는 모든것)속에 숨지말고, 현실을 마주하며 살아가라고 하는거 같네요. 추천합니다 꼭 해보세요! 2025 G.O.T.Y 4관왕 받은 이유가 있었네요.

독창적인 세계관과 예술적인 비주얼이 강점인 RPG다. 프랑스 벨에포크 시대에서 영감을 받은 몽환적이고 아름다운 그래픽이 뛰어난 몰입감을 제공하며, 턴제 전투에 실시간 액션 요소를 결합해 전략성과 긴장감을 동시에 살렸다. 또한 개성 있는 캐릭터와 깊이 있는 스토리가 어우러져 플레이어가 자연스럽게 세계관에 빠져들게 하며, 음악과 연출 역시 영화처럼 완성도가 높아 감성적인 RPG 경험을 선사한다.
아는 사람한테 빌려서 했는데 분위기 ost 게임성 모든게 좋았음.. 괜히 goat받은게 아닌듯.. 그래서 나도 스팀사서 열나게 플레이중 ㅎㅎㅎ

이건 예술이다. 군대 휴가 때 이 게임을 했습니다, 아직도 그 느낌이 잊혀지지 않네요. 발더스 게이트3를 하고 난 뒤 다른 게임을 해도 뭔가 허전했는데 이 게임이 저의 공허한 느낌을 해소해줬어요. 일단 OST가 진짜 작곡가가 미쳤나 싶을 정도로 진짜 엄청 좋습니다. 제 음악 취향이 팝, 힙합쪽인데 제 플레이리스트에 이 게임 노래가 있어요; 너무 좋아요 작곡가 스펙트럼이 되게 넓은 거 같아요. 솔직히 스토리보단 전투가 더 재밌게 느껴진 게임입니다. 보통 제가 스토리를 음미하면서 게임을 즐기는데 이 게임은 걍 빨리 스토리 넘어가고 전투하고 싶다는 생각이 계속 들었어요. 패링 할 때의 타격감과 매우 좋은 음악이 전투에 있으니 당연히 빨리 하고 싶다는 생각이 날 수 밖에요, 전투 난이도도 그렇게 어렵지 않아서 2,3 트만에는 다 깨요. 위에 스토리 건너뛰고 전투하고 싶다고 말했지만 스토리가 부족하다는 말이 아닙니다. 스토리도 정말 좋고 극초반에 이게 왜 이렇게 되는 거지 하면서 궁금증 유발

In the early stages, the story felt somewhat unclear and didn’t fully draw me in. The pacing and narrative weren’t especially engaging at first, but things begin to shift in the later chapters. As the game progresses, the tone and direction become much more compelling, leading to a strong and memorable ending. By the time I reached the conclusion, my overall impression had completely changed, with the finale leaving a lasting impact. 초반 진행 과정에서는 스토리가 다소 애매하게 느껴졌습니다. 전개 자체의 몰입도도 높지는 않았지만, 후반부로 갈수
게임이 아니라 영화다.. 게임 하는내내 영화를 플레이하는 느낌이였다. 게임플레이하면서 나오는 노래들이 너무 좋았다 제일 어려움 난이도로 플레이했는데 소울같은 스트레스로 더 오기생기게 만들었다. 다만.. 후반갈수록 원펀맨이 되어서 아쉽다.. 나는 턴제를 좋아했었을지도..
Overall good, the gameplay is solid but the story is not on par. Best described as "wide as an ocean, deep as a puddle"

It's not a bad game, but it's far from one of the best games ever made, I seriously doubt this will be remebered in a couple of years. Soundtrack is awesome, the visual design is cool, all the rest is just "meh", it's just a linear JRPG with baguette add to it, not a joke or anything, it really is an average JRPG with a french skin, nothing else. 2025 was a really average year in gaming, that's the only reason I can see for this game winning so many awards.

Great everything except gameplay, if your reaction time sucks in any way you are just gonna be frustrated because they decided that QTE's are a good combat mechanic. I'ma just watch a ♥ Let's Play at this point.

Simply one of the best single player games I have ever played! The story had me hooked to the point that I put down all other games to keep playing this. I am actually sad that I finished it now because of how good the overall game was. I have a hard time sticking to games due to my ADHD brain just wandering off but this game had its hooks in me!

We need this level of artistry from games again. The story has so much depth, the more I play it I'm still connecting the dots from things I already got the answers to. There's so many thing you can do with each characters skill sets and synergies. There's so much GREY area in terms of decisions and peoples moral dilemmas instead of black and white good or bad and I absolutely love that. I would recommend this game to absolutely anyone, I loved every second of it.

one of the best games ever. like we did, I recommend ignoring initial thoughts about turn based combat. This game will surpass your expectations and not only beg more attention but emotion from you. what a game.

If this game is already dead after just 33 years, I’m not sure I’ll ever understand why that happened—because this game is a masterpiece in every respect. The graphics are amazing, the combat system and animations are insane, and the story is simply mind-blowing.

Your like me. your in your early 30s and you feel youve lost your love of gaming. where did it go? Nothing piques our interest anymore. Buy a game only to turn it off 20 minutes later. It's an excellent game, and it's replayability is great too. I played it last year on launch and I picked it up again a year later. It's just as captivating the second time.
Honestly one of the best games I've ever played. Do your best to not look up too many things about it. Just play and get lost in the story.

This isn't a souls-like that'll have you yeeting your controller through a concrete pillar because you were stomped yet again by the tutorial boss and can't seem to "get gud". Beautiful and tragic. Easily a top-10 story from an RPG, while also having some of the best turn-based and action-response combat. Loved it from start to end.

This game. just playing the prologue i knew i would love it , and i do and i am enjoying The emotional rollercoaster its sending me on, It is an absolute Masterpiece from the story to the music every single part of it shows you exactly why it won so many awards. really awesome game if you don't fall in love with it well i don't know what to tell you . everyone needs to play this game.

Not a fan of the genre, but all the positive reviews are absolutely spot-on. Great story, characters, visuals, music. Gameplay can also be pretty fun once you get used to it. Just don't google any builds until you get a fair bit through the story because some guides contain heavy spoilers.

I got this game very skeptical of the absolute glaze fest the game was met with and what seemed like from an out of nowhere release. There are a few nitpicks here and there such as the very poor facial animations and what feels like mis-timed parry frames but aside from that the game is an amazing journey through an original story with a strong performance by the VAs and writing team. I'd suggest this to anyone who likes RPGs.

10/10 story, 9/10 gameplay Listen I've played a LOT of long RPGs throughout the years (of all genres, easily over 100) and I can confidently say that out of all of them this game has the BEST story. It's not just the writing - the delivery, the atmosphere, settings, consistency - everything is just *le chef's kiss*. It’s one of those games that, after you finish it, makes you wish you could erase your memory just so you could experience it for the first time again.

Took me on an emotionally rollercoaster first turn based game I've actually really got into and first game in a while that really made me want to do all the content it had to offer 10/10 would erase my memory to play this masterpiece again

I just finished this game. I'll give it a thumbs up because it had an interesting story, the voice acting was good, the environment was interesting, it had one of the better starts from a story perspective to the game compared to many others. [h1] GOTY급은 절대로 아님 [/h1] 할만하냐 아니냐 하면 할만은 한데, 몇몇 단점이 너무 크게 다가와서 솔직히 남한테 추천하고 싶은 게임은 아님. 이게 왜 GOTY? 첫째로 시인성 가시성이 최악임. 어느 맵구간이고 가릴 거 없이 그림자 지거나 어두운 부분으로 들어가면 밑바닥이 전혀 안 보여서 그냥 길 걷는 게 너무 귀찮고 짜증남. 밝기를 일부러 2.5로 올리고 했는데도 광원처리가 최악임. 맵이 복잡하지도 않은데 그냥 보이지가 않아서 길 돌아다니기가 싫은 경험은 색다르긴 한데 정말 빡침. 둘째로 패링 회피 개념을 턴제에 넣은 건 좋은데 활용법이 화나게 만듬. 후반부로 갈수록 엇박 쓰는 얘들이 늘어나는데 이게 시각적 효과+청각적 효과가 실제 내 패리 타이밍이랑 안 맞음. 아니면 너무 빨라서 패턴 외워서 예측 안 하면 그냥 처맞아야 함. 개인적으로 페인트리스 전에서 그 물감판 4연타였나 그거 정말 화나더라. 이게 와 이거 어렵네 좀 더 해봐야지가 아니라 그냥 화만남. 셋째는 레벨

게임 볼륨과 세계관에 비해 전개되는 얘기가 너무나 작아진다. 떡밥은 깔려있었다고 하긴 해도, 떡밥 분석해서 스토리 예측에 성공한 입장에서 봐도 별로였다. 세계의 비밀에 대한 얘기를 흥미롭게 풀만한 생각을 못 내서 이런 스토리가 나온 것 같다. 설정을 설정으로 덮어씌우는 느낌이 들었다. 가지고 있는 능력에 비해 너무 인간적이게 느껴지기고 했고, 등장인물들의 반응도 와닿지 않았다. 이야기가 진행될 수록 어느 캐릭터에게도 몰입할 수 없게 되어갔다. 지극히 서양스러운 스토리라는 느낌. 전문가 모드로 1회차 다 밀었는데 게임은 재밌다. 브금은 좋긴한데 다 분위기가 비슷해서 특색있게 기억 남는건 없는 듯.
정말 재밌었습니다. 게임에 몰입이 확되게하는 세계관, 그걸 받쳐주는 아트, 음악 턴제혐오자인 나한테 조차도 매력적인 전투방식 뭐하나 빠지는거없이 재밌었습니다.

A gorgeously presented, stylishly composed, and mostly very fun game (provided you don't mind parry mechanics) with a story that is excellent for the first act, and still pretty good from the second act until the ending. You may or may not enjoy the big twist, but regardless of narrative nitpicks, it is a fantastic game and a love letter to classic JRPGs, so if you are a fan of the genre, I'd say give it a shot!

I came for the beautiful art direction. I stayed because the game emotionally parried me into depression. Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is one of those rare RPGs that grabs your attention with its visuals and keeps you hooked with its characters, combat, and story. Every area looks like it belongs in a museum, every boss fight feels meaningful, and somehow the game manages to make "waiting for your turn" more exciting than some action games. The combat system deserves special praise. It takes trad

One of the best stories I've experienced in gaming, I was very hooked from beginning to end, the twists and conclusions all felt satisfying, and all the characters were likeable and had amazing dialogue. The gameplay was fun and introduces a parry and dodge mechanic to a turn-based combat which I thought was very engaging, it gets a little grindy near the end but that's fine. I also loved the soundtrack which made the story cutscenes and bossfights that much better.

Absolute masterpiece, Expedition 33 is truly the greatest game I've ever played, blending a powerful, emotional, story with incredible music and engaging turn based combat, it deserves its praise. 게임불감증이 아니라 게임이 문제였던 것

이 게임은 분명 초중반 까지는 정말로 "재밌게 했었다." RPG , JRPG , 턴제게임의 골자에서 조금 색다른 게임. 독특한 게임 시스템 , 독특한 스토리 등등 흥미롭고 색다른 감명을 줘 재밌게 했다. 하지만 게임 후반부에 접어들거나 게임 자체를 이해한 시점에서의 감상은 그 색다르고 , 독특한 게임 시스템 , 독특한 스토리 모두다 "단점"으로 다가왔다. 턴제 게임 에서 "패링"은 초반에만 신선하고 독특 했을뿐 어째서 "턴제"에서 "패링"을 해야 하는거지? 라는 생각을 시작으로 "패링"을 못 하는 "똥손 , 리듬게임 못 하는 음치,박치" 들은 "게임 플레이에 지장이 있을 정도"로 잘못된 설계 , 문제있는 설계" 로 다가왔다. 이런 문제를 타고 올라가다보면 "능력치-스텟 투자 시스템" 이나 방어 관련 능력치,아이템 등등 요소들은 똥손 이나 음치들은 별 도움이 되지 못 하고. 금손 과 칼박자 유저에겐 당연히 필요 없는 시스템 이며 모든 시스템을 공격에 몰빵하여 플레이 하는게 더 이롭다.

ui 디자인은 성의없고 초반부터 미친듯이 질주하는 npc인데 옆에 붙어서 대화하는 거 마냥 사운드가 들리고 모션은 뻑뻑하고 전투 ui는 페르소나와 비슷하고 환경사운드는 빈약한 편이고 게임 좀 했다고 하면 이거저거 생각날만만 겉모습이에요. 스토리나 분위기는 중반까지 몰입감있고 컷신도 공 많이 들인 느낌이 나서 좋았어요. 하지만 이후 전개에서 다소 호불호가 생길 수 있어요. 전투는 패링/qte가 아니였더라도 다듬으면 충분히 재밌었을 게임인 거 같아요. 패링/qte가 턴제전투만 보고 기겁하며 하지 않았을 사람에게도 먹게 해주는 시스템이라 평이 더 좋았을거라고 생각해요. 또한 개발자들이 대체로 jrpg를 좋아하는 사람들이라 그런지 자신들이 재미있었다는 부분을 충실히 구현하려고 노력한 느낌도 많아요. 거석파트는 진짜 지루했어요. 엔드 콘텐츠는 경우에 따라서 빌드를 통한 죽창싸움이 되는데 이 부분은 선택의 여지를 준다고 생각해서 긍정적으로 생각하는 편이에요. 결론은 재밌는 게임이에요. 이게 한

게임 구매 망설이는 분들 위해서 장단점 3줄요약 장점 1. 전투 몰입감이 압도적임 2. 타격감, 패링, 회피 할때마다 짜릿함 그냥 도파민 덩어리임 3. 알게 모르게 깔리는 OST가 진짜 가슴이 떨리게함 단점 1. 솔직히 그냥 중국 양산형 게임같음 공격모션 같은거 기적의검 같음 2. 개 쓸데없는 컷신이 많음 3. 스토리가 클리셰라 그냥 예측이 감 솔직히 초반에 너무 몰입해서 스토리 하나 하나 보는데 컷신이 중요해서 보면 개 병신내용이고 안중요할거 같아서 스킵하니까 존나 중요한 장면 놓침 심지어 컷신이 매우매우 긺 중반부터 스킵해서 모르는데 내가봤을 땐 돌잔치 얘기도 했을거같음 한줄평 소울류 입문용 정가주고 사도 안아까움

정말 오랜만에 인생에 남을 게임을 즐기게 되어서 매우 기쁨. 신생 게임 개발사 33명이 만든 작품이 맞나 싶을 정도로 매우 높은 완성도를 가졌으며, 단 한 순간도 게임이 재미없다고 생각된 적이 없을 정도로 재미있었음. 턴제 RPG라는 역사가 깊으면서도 단순하고 지루할 수 있는 이 장르를 새롭게 개척한 점도 대단하다고 생각함. 여러 가지 부분에 대한 나의 의견을 정리해 보았음 1. 스토리/서사 - 취향을 떠나서 서사 자체가 가진 완결성이 매우 높음. 잘 짜여진 몇 권으로 연재된 소설책을 읽는 듯한 느낌이었음. 현실과 동떨어진 SF라고도 할 수 있는 장르임에도 불구하고 초반부터 몰입할 수 있도록 등장인물간의 개연성, 동기를 촘촘하게 잘 만들었다고 생각함. - 특히, 초반 플레이 시점에서 세계관의 새로운 요소들을 이해할 수 있도록 적절한 설명을 다양한 등장인물들로 풀어주었고, 게임 내 환경 곳곳에 배치함으로서 너무 설명적이지 않으면서도 충분히 관심을 가지고 이해를 할 수 있어서 유치하다는

title: TEKKEN 8
critic_response:
TEKKEN 8은 전반적으로 구체적인 호평을 받았습니다. 또 다른 리뷰어는 'TEKKEN 8은 전작의 장점을 계승하고 개선하여, 정말로 재미있는 게임 플레이를 제공한다'고 했습니다. 하지만, PC 버전의 온라인 기능이 콘솔 버전보다 뒤처진다는 의견도 있었습니다. 전반적으로 TEKKEN 8은 훌륭한 게임으로 평가되며, 탐험 콘텐츠와 개선된 게임 플레이를 제공합니다.
장점: TEKKEN 8은 전작의 장점을 계승하고 개선하여, 정말로 재미있는 게임 플레이를 제공합니다. / TEKKEN 8은 탐험 콘텐츠를 제공하며, 모든 플레이어에게 적합합니다.
단점: PC 버전의 온라인 기능이 콘솔 버전보다 뒤처집니다. / 한 리뷰는 게임의 진행 장벽이 높다고 느낄 수 있습니다. / 온라인 모드에서 지연 문제가 발생할 수 있습니다.
critic_contexts: Even if you’re not a fan of the online competitive scene, Tekken 8 has a lot of content to offer: assisted mode that is great for parties, a story campaign, and an absolutely fantastic Arcade mode that features its own story and unobtrusively helps you become a better player. [Issue#263, p.36]

Tekken 8 is another impressive addition to Bandai Namco's highly successful line-up of famous fighting games, combining abundant content and a very entertaining "easy-to-learn/hard-to-master" gameplay.

Tekken 8 blew us away with its excellent, engaging features that are fine-tuned to fit every fighting game fan's preference. It also offers a battle system that new and veteran players can enjoy.as long as you can take the Heat.
A masterclass in fighting game design, perfect for both new and old players alike.

TEKKEN 8 hits hard with what could quite possibly be the best TEKKEN entry to date. Fresher than newcomer Azucena’s signature coffee blend, this title does everything in its power to make this King of Iron Fist tournament worthy of all players.

With Tekken 8, Bandai Namco takes everything that was good about its predecessor and ups the ante on most things. While it’s not a revolutionary sequel, all the improvements make Tekken 8 a must-have for fighting fans – whether you play to be the best or just for fun.

Good story mode, fun gameplay, great training modes, near perfect online mode, plenty of fighters to choose and detailed customization options. Tekken 8 is an ideal package with everything I could want from a fighting game.

Tekken 8 is an example of a game living up to it's own hype. With a fantastic roster of warriors, amazing single and multi-player modes, great online connectivity and incredible graphics and sound, Tekken 8 solidifies itself as one of the best in the business and an excellence of execution.
The most accessible, most extensive and currently simply the best fighting game on consoles - the PC version noticeably lags behind online.

Having now spent enough time online and offline with Tekken 8 across Steam Deck, PS5, and Xbox Series X, I can safely say that Bandai Namco Studios hit it out of the park with this one. It is a universal improvement over the last game and delivers a superb package right from day one. While it has a few things I’d like to see fixed in updates, Tekken 8 gets my highest possible recommendation right now. I can’t wait to play more. It feels great to not only enjoy playing a new Tekken game, but also

Tekken 8 brings everything you desire from a new game in the franchise. A fantastic story mode, lots of options, fighters and possibilities. Fine, refreshing gameplay and good online functionalities. The few minor downsides the game has are that the character customization isn't that captivating and the rollback netcode could be just a little bit better for the perfect experience. That being said, Tekken 8 is a must have.

Tekken 8 is a stellar entry to the series with something for everyone. Whether you're a grizzled veteran or just want to play as the guy who looks like Bruce Lee, there's never been a better time to enjoy the King of Iron Fist tournament.

Tekken 8 is excellent. The fighting is just as crisp as ever, and the addition of the Heat mechanic and an increased focus on aggression create a game that feels both exciting to play and watch. The bevy of new modes was what the game needed at launch, and there's more than enough content to keep players busy without feeling like every facet of the title had been explored. The fighting game scene has been blessed with banger after banger over the last few years, and Tekken 8 keeps that trend goi

Tekken 8 is the ultimate Tekken game. A glossy veneer of modernity encases a strong foundation that builds upon its predecessors to great success. In every regard, Tekken 8 matches and surpasses the very best that the series, and the fighting game genre as a whole, has to offer.

Featuring cutting-edge visuals for fighting games and exceptional learning tools, Tekken 8 stands out as one of the best releases in the fighting game genre over the last few years. While the netcode is generally acceptable, regrettably, it falls short of replicating the seamless offline experience. This drawback significantly affects the overall experience, way more than the limited amount of single player content, and somewhat diminishes the effectiveness of the game's outstanding learning too

Tekken 8 improves on its predecessor with a complete graphics overhaul, drastically improved netcode, and a robust base game package featuring 32 characters and 16 stages. With DLC fighters already in the pipeline, there’s more than enough content to keep fighting game fans packing punches for the foreseeable future.

My point is: YOUR favourites are probably still here. And if they are, you'll be content to kick and/or punch. There isn't anything revolutionary happening aside from the Heat meter and all that it entails. But a lot of the quality of life improvements and subtle design tweaks stack up. Practice mode alone has become an excellently robust training zone that clearly displays a lot of handy info about frame advantage and move properties. For pros, streamers and rank-chasers, the transfer to this s

If this review appears glowing, frankly that's how I feel about the game. It's without a doubt my favourite fighting game out right now, and I've been fiending to get back online ever since they took the pre-release servers down. The one scratch on the game's spotless form is online infrastructure that doesn't quite live up to other games on the market right now, which makes it hard to recommend to those with seperated friend groups or subpar online connections. If neither of those apply to you,
The best Tekken game in years, let down only by its struggling netcode and aged customisation.

Tekken 8 is a more than generous game, and a wonderful love letter to lovers of the license. It's a shame that its story mode is too redundant in terms of gameplay, and that its solo modes are a little less interesting than those of some of its rivals, because it would otherwise have been the best fighting game released recently without any possible dispute. The heart of the game will really be to fight with other players, the other modes being rather limited and relatively short. If the balanci

Tekken 8 is full of excitement and we loved it! Technically, in both substance and form, the Bandai Namco fighting game is a gem of the genre. If it sometimes lacks a little originality in terms of content, but there is still a lot of it, it all makes it one of the best 3D fighting games of recent years. Its new features in terms of gameplay are enough to stand out from previous opuses and hold its own against its competitors, all with vast content that is still substantial enough to play for do
Tekken 8 is a marked improvement over Tekken 7 and a perfectly executed balancing act, keeping older players happy while revealing its trademark freedom to newcomers.

Street Fighter 6 just raised the bar for AAA fighting games midway through 2023, and its long-time 3D rival Tekken has already matched it. Tekken 8's story mode is a breath of fresh air after T7's, and its abundance of single-player modes will give casual fans plenty to do afterward. Veterans can enjoy Tekken 8's new characters and mechanics without the game’s fundamentals being compromised, and a top-notch presentation will impress without technical hiccups. No one could have expected Tekken 8
Tekken 8 takes everything that made its predecessors so popular and takes it to a whole new level.

Tekken 8 has progressed in every aspect compared to its predecessors, from the overall narrative and direction to the depth of the system and user convenience. It is a fighting game that can provide ample enjoyment not only for long-time fans but also for new users.

Fantastic gameplay, plenty of game modes, and a substantial roster of playable characters are the main strengths of Tekken 8. The evolution is more on the cautious side, and the story mode could use some refinement. However, in the competition with last year's giants, Bandai Namco has nothing to be ashamed of.

TEKKEN 8은 탐험 콘텐츠와 개선된 조작감으로 많은 유저들이 즐기고 있습니다. 뉴비들이 많아지고 있으며, 조작감이 편리해져 입문하기 좋은 게임이라는 평가도 있습니다. 하지만 일부 유저들은 게임의 진행 장벽과 밸런스 조절에 대해 불만을 제기하고 있습니다. 특히, 랭크 매치에서의 자동 콤보 사용과 캐릭터 밸런스 등에 문제가 있다고 지적하고 있습니다. 또한, 게임의 최적화와 가격 정책에 대한 비판도 있습니다. 키 씹힘 문제와 같은 고질적인 문제가 여전히 존재하며, 캐릭터 구매를 위한 추가 비용이 필요하다는 점도 지적받고 있습니다. 그럼에도 불구하고, TEKKEN 8은 현재 최고의 격투 게임 중 하나로 평가받고 있으며, 매력적인 그래픽과 음악, 그리고 대규모 캐릭터 라인업으로 많은 팬들을 보유하고 있습니다. 다만, 게임의 진행 장벽과 진입 장벽에 대한 우려도 있습니다. 기존 캐릭터들의 특징이 줄어들고 사기 기술이나 자세가 늘어나면서 입문이 어려워졌다는 평가도 있습니다.

철 7에서 했었던, 격투게임이 이게 맞나 싶은 생각이 많이 들었음. 보라 / 황금단에서 1~3계급만 올라가면 그래도 파괴신인데, 대부분의 상대가 딜캐 상관없이 기술 지르고 개기고 파크 쓰고 2타 상단 아무렇게나 뿌리고, 헛치고 별의 별짓을 하는데 게임이 잘 돌아가고 오히려 그렇게 하는 게 이득인 말도 안 되는 어그레시브적인 형태임. 대강 파랑단쯤에서 하는 짓을 그대로 해도 별문제 없이 이기고 황금단까지 올라가짐 내가 이겨도 이게 왜 이기지하고 잘했단 느낌도 안 들고, 반대로 졌을 때도 이게 왜 지지하는 느낌이 상당히 많이 듦. 불쾌한 어그레시브, 지나치게 긴 콤보 등등. 철 7 유저로서 불만인 부분이 많아도 게임이 흥했다면, 그냥 틀에 박힌 철 7 유저에 불과할지 몰라도 지금의 평가를 보면 게임 자체를 상당히 잘 못 만든 것 같음. 그래도 시즌 3 때는 베이직으로 돌아간다고 해서 참고 기다렸는데, 놀랍도록 크게 바뀐 것이 아무것도 없었음 전작, 전전작에서 없어졌던 모션 재탕하기 /
잘못된 방향성, 문제 파악을 전혀 못하는 개발진, 늘어지고 피곤한 인게임, 느려 터진 사후 대응, 불규칙적이며 너무나 긴 패치 주기, 소통 부족. 길었던 시리즈의 마지막이 될 게임.

격투게임을 좋아하는 사람으로서 철권을 군대에서 태그2로 입문한 이후로 꾸준히 즐겨왔고 이번 철권8도 나름 플레이하면서 느낀 결과 지금 철권에 필요한 건 개발진 교체, 사기 기술의 너프 이런 것들도 있겠지만 이 게임의 현 시점에서 가장 큰 문제는 2D격겜과 3D격겜 그 사이의 어중간한 게임성이라고 생각함 사실 격겜 유저라고 하면 모든 격겜을 좋아하고 잘할 것이라고 여겨지지만 2D격겜과 3D격겜은 완전히 다른 세계에 놓여져 있는 장르임 가령 2D격겜에선 장풍과 점프, 리버설과 대공기 등으로 대표되는 거리재기와 점프 심리전이 주를 이루고 인간의 반응속도로 구분하기 힘든 속도의 공격들로 이어지는 압박을 통해 굉장히 빠른 템포를 자아낸다면 3D격겜에선 상대의 공격 예측을 통한 횡이동 같은 무빙과 상단회피기로 대표되는 지르기 기술, 공격들의 프레임 계산을 통한 산술적인 플레이와 이를 비트는 역심리가 얽히고 섥히면서 만나는 상대마다, 매 세트의 템포가 달라지는 굉장히 변칙적인 공방이 이루어짐 때

기존에 있던 캐릭터들만의 특징은 줄이고 사기 기술이나 자세, 이지/삼지선다 강요가 늘어나는 등으로 입문이 매우 어렵습니다. 태그2 부터 했고 철권7도 1000시간이 넘게 했는데, 솔직히 양학성 플레이나 일부러 조롱하는 듯한 플레이어는 예전에도 많았지만 철권8의 단점들이 부각되면서 질때의 불쾌감이 이전작들에 비해 너무 커졌습니다. 게다가 신캐나 특히 신기술들의 개발에 있어 유저들의 의견은 별로 반영되지도 않고 모션 재탕 등 성의가 보이지도 않는 부분들이 많아요. 실제로 유저수나 격겜 대회에서 위상 등 실제로 보이는 수치에서만 봐도 스트리트 파이터랑은 비교도 안될 정도로 망겜에 가까워지고 있긴 합니다. 그래도 일단 스파나 킹오파 같은 격겜보다는 철권만의 조작을 더 좋아했고 계속 플레이 했지만 솔직히 유저들도 대부분 관성으로 철권을 하는거지 게임에 대한 애정은 다들 많이 식었을 겁니다. 2D 기반의 스파, 킹오파, 길티 등의 조작보다 철권같이 횡이동도 곁들인 격겜이 취향이시거나 하면 할만

철권이랑은 그다지 연이 없어서 제대로 플레이 해본 건 8편이 처음인데, 랭크매치 성적은 형편없지만 혼자서 스토리모드 보고 CPU 상대로 콤보 연습하는 건 상당히 재밌었습니다. 스토리모드가 거의 애니메이션 보는 느낌으로 영상을 잘 만들어놓았네요. 그리고 콤보 자동으로 나가는 시스템 덕에 기술 모르는 케릭터로도 투닥거리며 제법 할 만했습니다. 그런데, 이전 시리즈를 그다지 깊이 즐긴 적이 없어서 저한텐 훌륭한 그래픽과 다양한 기술들로 콤보 꾸리는 재미가 있는 게임이었지만, 스팀 평가랑 유튜브의 날선 비판들을 보니 분위기가 심상치 않네요. 견문이 얕아 근래 철권팬들의 불만이 폭발하고 제작사에 개선을 역설하는 이유에 대해 잘 이해하지는 못하고 있지만, 제작사에서 팬들 의견에 귀기울여 얼른 개선돼서 시리즈 명성을 이어갈 수 있었으면 좋겠습니다.

디렉터들은 캐릭터 성능을 고심한 생각으로정성것 생각한게 아니라 똥싸다가 영감받아서 만들었나 어떻게된게 딸깍으로 100딜을 쳐넘기냐 그래 내가 못하는것도 맞거든? 근데 그 아득바득 이길려는 노력을 개지랄 몇번이면 쳐이긴다는게 존나 꼴깝임 그리고 횡돌았는데 무슨 사람 옆에서 귀에 속삭이듯이 말할 수 있을법한 상황에 발등찍었냐? 히트 쳐맞는건 왜 쳐맞는거냐 내가 개발자가 아니여서 이해를 못하는거니? 할인도 아니라 정가에 8만원으로 산 그냥 나새끼가 개발자에 개돼지할게
유지로 나오는거 때문에 좋아요 준다 ㅋㅋㅋ

게임의 외형 측면에서는 그래픽 좋고, BGM좋고, 액션성 뛰어나고.. 사실 액션게임으로서는 그리 깔 곳이 없다. 아니 철권 역대 최고수준의 퀄리티이다. 스토리 모드는 뇌절에 뇌절이라 개인적으로는 매우 불호이지만, 격투게임 스토리 치고는 공을 많이 들인 느낌은 느껴진다. 운영과 밸런스 문제로 평가가 나락갔지만. 파이트패스나 업데이트가 계속 이루어지는 라이브서비스 중인데다.. 하위리그에서 즐겜하는 개인적 입장에선 크게 신경 쓰이진 않는다. 멋있는 캐릭터가 좋고, 화끈한 타격감을 느끼고 싶다면 안 할 이유가 없는 게임.
한 기술 계속 갈기는 초딩들 개 많음 그리고 자동콤보 이거 좀 랭크매치에서는 사용 못하게 해라 콤보는 유튜브가서 외우면 되는게 짜증나네

title: Forza Horizon 5
critic_response:
포르자 호라이즌 5는 오픈 월드 레이싱 게임으로, 멕시코를 배경으로 한 탐험 콘텐츠와 뛰어난 그래픽스로 호평을 받고 있습니다. 이 게임은 시리즈의 최신작으로, 이전 작품인 포르자 호라이즌 4와 비교하여 혁신적인 요소를 추가했습니다. 그러나 한 리뷰는 게임이 전작과 비교하여 큰 혁신을 이루지 못했다고 지적했습니다.
장점: 게임은 시리즈의 최신작으로, 이전 작품인 포르자 호라이즌 4와 비교하여 혁신적인 요소를 추가했습니다.
단점: 한 리뷰는 게임이 전작과 비교하여 큰 혁신을 이루지 못했다고 지적했습니다. / 게임은 여전히 훌륭한 레이싱 게임이지만, 시리즈가 후반부 핵심 요소 상태에 있다고 느낄 수 있습니다. / 한 리뷰는 게임이 더 많은 혁신을 추구하지 않은 것에 대해 아쉬움을 표현했습니다.
critic_contexts: The graphics, lighting and weather effects are some of the best we've seen in a racing game, but of course it's all about the cars. Although this is an arcade racer that does not focus on realism but rather on short and fast action, the cars behave truthfully and react differently whether you are driving on a track, on the street or off road. The controls have been kept simple, making the game accessible to just about anyone. Altogether, this makes Forza Horizon 5 the most fun racing game of the

Forza Horizon 5 still is a great racing game, but there’s no denying that the series is in a torpor-like state. It’s obvious that Playground is afraid not only to innovate, but to make any sort of significant change to a successful formula. [Issue#257, p.64]

I don't think there's a motoring enthusiast who won't be excited about Forza Horizon 5, with a slight caveat for those who have had their fill of Forza Horizon 4 and might want more of the same - though honestly, I can't imagine how the formula could be improved upon, which in the arcade driving department is bordering on perfection. It promises and delivers an incomparably customizable driving experience, with dream-cars, in breathtaking landscapes.
A masterful open-world racer in an unforgettable setting - would have benefited from a little more courage to innovate.

It’s no surprise that Forza Horizon puts itself right into the mix with 2021’s Game of the Year contenders. Playground Games has been getting it right for a decade now. This is the definitive experience in the series so far, one that excels both on PC and the Xbox Series X. Minimal issues, such as server connectivity, fail to spoil the party that is just crammed with happiness and adventure. A number of small improvements across the board elevates the racer to being best in class; a huge plus fo

While Burnout Paradise Remastered still offers more involved gameplay on a second-to-second basis while still offering a similarly gleeful atmosphere there isn’t another open world racing game so exquisitely polished as this. If you’ve played a Forza Horizon game before then you might feel a slight sense of deja vu, but you won’t care as the formula has been perfected at last. The best sound and visuals, the most variety of gameplay, the best editors, superb car handling… it’s sheer class. And s

Familiar, excellent, and polished to a degree that hardly seems possible. If you love taking vintage Porsches for joyrides through environments that’d have a poet bawling then you're going to be very happy here.

Forza Horizon 5 is the racing game for everyone, which takes outsiders from the racing genre and turns them into followers of the speed, and for those who are already fans, it offers an almost endless variety of activities and challenges. Also very remarkable is the representation of Mexico, which offers vibrant landscapes full of incredible details that only those who live in Mexico will recognize. It is an indispensable game and highly recommended.

Forza Horizon 5 is still an incredible racing game, one only overshadowed by the brilliance of its predecessor. Those with a lot of time behind the wheel in Forza Horizon 4 may feel this is one step forward for two steps back, but there's still a ton of fun to be had in this high-octane adventure.

With Forza Horizon 5, the racing series makes its first stop in Mexico. It delivers breathtaking vistas, fast action, and the hook: the new EventLab mode that lets the community create cool, custom challenges.

The detour into Mexico is a sight for sore eyes after a three-year stay in the rather dreary United Kingdom of Horizon 4. Innovation is about the last thing you’ll find on the Forza Horizon 5 buffet, but there is no denying that this is the best software available for Microsoft’s Series X|S consoles and, for people who have strong PCs, the best reason yet to pony up for a 4K 120Hz HDR display.

Forza Horizon 5 has carried the tradition of consuming my time inside a genre that normally doesn't interest me. Playground Games has displayed technical wizardry with this game and while it doesn’t change the formula to any significant extent, the experience found within Forza Horizon 5 is killer.

¡Ay, caramba! Forza Horizon 5 is maturing under the Mexican sun a recipe concocted by Playground Games that has already delighted millions of pilots. Like an antojito, Forza Horizon 5 can be enjoyed in a good mood with anyone, anytime. Rolling over everything that has already been technically done and taking advantage of the Xbox Series X springboard to stamp the skies, Microsoft's software climbs the volcano of fun and unleashes an exhilarating torrent to be experienced alone or with others. It

While Forza Horizon 5 launches with a score of "only" 9.0, it has potential for even more. All Playground Games have to do is to eliminate bugs and bring back some missing cars, which could be done in a few months, with several updates. What about a perfect 10/10? It would require a multitude of further improvements to AI and physics. But perhaps Forza Horizon 6 will achieve that. I surely hope so, as this would give us the perfect racing game.

Playground's singular series gets a lush new setting, but otherwise remains committed to tuning an already winning formula. But even a marginal improvement on the best racing series around is worth celebrating.

Forza Horizon 5 is yet another step up for the series and arguably the best racing game ever made. Offering a host of content across a fantastic representation of Mexico, its varied terrain and scenery, there's so much to explore and race. A massive roster of cars will perform all of these, each feeling different from the others, with a variety of upgrade and tuning options almost matching those of simulation titles. While there are a few minor niggles here and there, they are nearly so small as

Forza Horizon 5 is a masterclass example of how to do an open-world racing game, bar none. Thanks to its deeply rich map, wealth of content, and fantastic visuals, it's the best racing experience I've had yet.

You can whine about the lack of revolution, but when everything works perfectly fine, maybe there is no reason to change it. Forza Horizon 5 is definitely the best racing game out there and has no competition. This is, by far, the biggest car adventure you can have on your screen.
Forza Horizon 5 is undoubtedly a visual showcase and technical benchmark for PC and Xbox Series X owners. If you have the hardware to run this game on PC, this is the choice to make.

Forza Horizon 5 is a formidable game, with a spectacular graphics and an overwhelming amount of content. But above all, it is a fun title that dignifies the fun in video games with no greater pretension than that, to have the best time possible.

Forza Horizon 5 is built to deliver hour after hour of glossy, beautiful fun. Hit the race mechanics as deeply as you like. Zip around the amazingly beautiful map in a Corvette for no reason at all. Find secrets. Build out your car collection. Get rich. Have a good time the way you want. Go anywhere, do anything, just don’t skip this stellar game.

포르자 호라이즌 5는 역대 최고의 지역인 일본으로 떠나게 될 생각에 설레는 플레이어들이 많다. 게임의 조작감은 좋고, 패드 새로 사서 하면 몰입감이 너무 좋다는 평가가 있다. 게임 자체가 비싸다는 느낌이 있지만, 노트북으로 QHD+ 해상도에서 즐기는 것이 가능하다는 평가가 있다.
장점: 게임의 조작감은 좋고, 패드 새로 사서 하면 몰입감이 너무 좋다는 평가가 있다. / 캐주얼하게 즐기기 좋고 그래픽이 좋으며, 물리엔진이 우수하다. / 게임에서 타기 힘든 차들을 즐기는 재미가 있다.
단점: 퀘스트 및 안내가 복잡하다는 단점이 있다. / 마이크로계정 찾기 문제가 발생한다는 평가가 있다. / 윈도우 계정 로그인 버그가 있다는 보고가 있다.

Forza Horizon 5 is easily the best racing game I’ve played. I originally picked it up as something casual to play between bigger, story-driven games, but it quickly became one of the games I spent the most time with. What surprised me the most was the sheer amount of content. There’s always something to do, whether it’s racing, exploring, collecting cars, or just driving around. More importantly, the content feels varied and well-made rather than being there just for the sake of quantity. My onl

“Bigger map, brighter world, same incredible driving fun.” Forza Horizon 5 takes everything great about the series and pushes it into a more vibrant, more diverse, and more visually impressive direction. Set in a stunning recreation of Mexico, the game immediately stands out with its color, scale, and environmental variety. The world design is easily one of its biggest strengths. From dense jungles and sandy deserts to volcanic peaks and lively cities, every region feels distinct and worth explo

It may not be as good as FH4 or previous entries, but FH5 is still an amazing experience and still one of the best casual racing games still available. The graphics are really realistic for a nearly 5 year old game and even though activities like the festival playlist can be repetitive at times, gameplay is still really good nonetheless.

1. 주행 평가 솔직히 이 게임은 자동차를 수집하는 것을 좋아하는 사람들이 할만한 게임이지 이 게임으로 진정한 드라이빙(언더, 오버가 비현실적으로 느낌이 이상함) 을 즐길 생각은 하지 마세요. 그냥 캐쥬얼 하게 즐기는 게임이지 하드 하게 하실 거라면 아세토나 그란투리스모 하세요. 그렇다고 포르자 모터스포츠 하시지 말고요. (그것도 별로입니다) 2. 그래픽 및 최적화 평가 2021년에 나온 게임 치고는 사양이 좀 높습니다. 아무리 DLSS키고 하더라도 그렇게 만족할만한 프레임은 안 나올 겁니다. 그렇다고 그래픽이 충분히 좋은가? -> 그것도 아니다. 그냥 찰흙 모형에다 다듬어서 반사 효과 많이 넣은 느낌? 비유하자면 포카리 살 돈 없어서 맹물에다 소금 넣는 느낌임. 솔직히 이 게임은 컨텐츠도 별로 없어서 (스토리는 재미 없음) 10시간 내외로 쫑 가능하다.

title: Overwatch 2
critic_response:
오버워치 2는 블리자드의 슈팅 게임이 필요로 했던 변화를 가져왔다는 평가를 받는다. 새로운 5대5 경기 방식과 영웅들의 등장으로 게임이 혁신을 이룰 준비가 되었다. 오버워치 2는 엄청나게 좋으며, 5대5 방식이 게임에 새 생명을 불어넣었다. 새로운 영웅들, 특히 정크퀸, 소전, 키리코가 신선한 바람을 가져왔고, 게임의 새로운 높은 페이스가 경쟁 환경에 적합하다. 한 리뷰는 오버워치 2가 완벽하지는 않지만, 게임플레이의 통제된 혼돈이 자신에게 적합하다고 평가했다.
장점: 오버워치 2는 깊이 있는 슈팅 게임으로, 독특한 영웅들을 숙달하고, 맵을 학습하며, 많은 시간을 투자할 수 있는 게임이다.
단점: 한 리뷰는 게임이 이전 버전에서 일부 매력을 잃었다고 느꼈다. / 라이브 서비스 트래핑과 같은 문제들이 게임의 장기적인 재미를 방해할 수 있다.
critic_contexts: Overwatch 2 plays brilliantly. It’s a wonderful shooter full of depth, with unique heroes to master, maps to learn, and hours of your life to lose all over again.

Overwatch 2 doesn’t flip the formula the way you might expect a long-awaited, numbered sequel would. But through various clever tweaks, it’s a well-rounded evolution of the experience into which I’ve poured more than a thousand hours since 2016. I may never recreate the magic of those first few years in Overwatch, but Overwatch 2 is a big step towards restoring the faith in the franchise and has me thinking it’s time to pour a few more hundred hours into my favorite team-based shooter.

At launch, Overwatch 2 is a launchpad for something much, much grander. It’s rough around the edges, but the controlled chaos of the actual gameplay is right up my alley. Given the live service nature of the game, it’s something I’ll likely re-review every so often. Still, Overwatch 2 is in pretty great shape overall.

Overwatch 2 is so damn good. 5v5 has breathed life into the game, Junkerqueen, Sojourn, and Kiriko are a breath of fresh air, and the game’s new high pace suits its competitive nature perfectly. The new maps are insanely fun to play, albeit with some needing tweaking, and the new Push gamemode only enhances Overwatch 2’s core experience. The only downside comes from its cosmetic-locked, 9-week battle pass system, which will be costly to continually purchase.
A breath of fresh air for the Overwatch franchise, with the new game modes, characters, and mechanics all working together superbly well, for a genuinely exciting sequel.

Overwatch 2 represents the arrival of the foundational changes Blizzard's hero shooter needed in order to return to its former glory. While it's not perfect — few things ever are — the changes that the game makes to Overwatch's gameplay structure, progression systems, content release schedule, and presentation are absolutely stellar.

Overwatch 2 seeks a second chance from the community to be the "eSport of the decade". It has changes in the gameplay, in the competitive integrity, even aesthetic and sound changes but no matter what, it is one of the most exciting and adrenaline-filled shooting video games ever created.

Overwatch as a franchise has always felt like a child taking toys out of a box to hurriedly show you what’s next, breathless through excitement before switching to another. In that sense, Overwatch 2 is a bigger toy box, with a few more action figures and incredible new dioramas to pose them in. It’s definitely a variation on a theme rather than something wholly new, at least until the PvE component arrives in 2023, but what’s here has reignited my love for a world, its characters, and their sto

Overwatch 2 is a worthy successor to the original Overwatch that doesn’t so much reinvent the wheel as it does improve upon it. While it does have its issues, the core gameplay and improved UI make for a fun and fully realized sequel.

Overwatch 2 is the change that Blizzard's shooter was crying out for. This one is ready to revolutionize the community and completely transform the image we had of Overwatch. His move to 'free to play' is a key element to attract more players, and other changes such as 5v5, the Battle Pass or the new characters are the perfect elements to keep them all.

Overwatch 2 is one of the best shooters of the year for obvious reasons. Its gameplay, competitiveness, extensive lore and huge fan base have made this second installment one of the biggest games of the next decade for Blizzard.

In overall balance-wise, I was able to feel the change from Overwatch to Overwatch2. Especially, 5 vs 5 match locking one tank successfully returned the game to a fast-paced battle in the early days while suppressing the existing tank meta. Though it is hard to say the balance between characters is perfect, I believe the game is going to provide fresh, joyful gameplay in each season.
A really good and extensive free-to-play sequel to the legendary hero shooter, which loses some of its character, but has been carefully renewed and fortunately not become too greedy.
While not revolutionizing the genre as its predecessor did, Overwatch 2 works thanks to a more modern competitive structure and is fun with its smoother gameplay.

This sequel is not revolutionary as the first chapter: it is simply what who wanted a more up-to-date PvP structure needed. The 5 vs 5 gameplay works and is smoother, but in the new era of the franchise seems like there is less space than before for casual or not competitive players.

If you loved Overwatch, there’s really no reason why you won’t enjoy continuing this journey. If you weren’t a fan, this isn’t going to change your mind. That said, if the first batch of heroes, modes, and maps were any indication, it at least looks like Overwatch 2’s new free-to-play seasonal content rotation is going to keep things interesting for a long time to come.

Where it counts, Overwatch 2 feels like a fantastic update to a multiplayer game with mechanics that enable thrilling skirmishes between teams of heroes. However, as a separate entity and sequel, it stumbles. Changes to the core gameplay are steps in the right direction, but it is surrounded by new points of friction where there previously were none. Thankfully, as a live-service game, things will be added or altered once Blizzard has more player feedback, so at the very least the studio has a s
Overwatch 2’s switch to a 5v5 format breathes new life into what was once the sharpest shooter around. It just hasn’t quite recaptured all of that glory – yet.

Overwatch 2 restores the fantastic hero shooter by making it free for all to enjoy. Changes to multiplayer mechanics make gameplay even more enthralling and address annoyances with the original. Although, there are still valid concerns about hero balancing, UI overload, and the future of the battle pass model. While it may not feel like a true sequel without the promised PvE story mode, Overwatch 2 can potentially be the most popular shooter on the market.

A bold reinvention, Overwatch 2 is fun to play and has a variety of smart tweaks that reinvigorate the game and package it up for a new audience. While live service trappings like unlocking heroes through a battle pass are unwelcome distractions, once you’re bedded into this shooter, it’s hard to point to any other shooter that can deliver this much joy.

It’s difficult to look back on a game like Overwatch and wonder what it could’ve been if it had been delayed, and released as a complete, full-priced package with none of the extras: no feel-bad Battle Passes, with the story mode and Hero Missions available to all from the get-go. It’s not all doom and gloom of course; I’ve been in denial about the change in direction for PvP, but tens of hours of playtime have convinced me that the new 5v5 format will lead to more exciting experiences going for

Overwatch 2 is not a revolution of any kind, because it revolves around that identity that shook the market in 2016. However, the new composition of the teams must be assimilated, it is promising and rewards good players at the expense (perhaps) of novices. The free to play model seems perfectly in line with what the market offers (for better or for worse), but blocking the original heroes may not have been the best choice. Overwatch 2 is still the fun and captivating game it always was, and now

As you can see, Overwatch 2 isn't really Overwatch 2; it's more of a big content update or upgrade. The license will get a new game when the story mode is released in 2023 (which will require an update of this test by the way). However, despite the economic system that is very surprising and not looked upon favorably, the competitive games are still very exciting. The new heroines are excellent and the maps are just as good, and it's still fun to watch the talented designers at Blizzard. We hope

At the moment, Overwatch 2 cannot be considered a new game in its own right, but is positioned much more as a huge content update. The title in its full version will really see the light of day when the Story mode is released. However, it remains an addictive and very well thought-out shooter with heroes just as interesting and endearing as each other. It knows how to please experienced players, as well as occasional players thanks to a wide variety of mechanics to master. Even if the Battle Pas
Overwatch 2 does some things differently than its predecessor, but it doesn't pass as a sequel.
With a revitalised gameplay experience and tighter combat, the actual moment-to-moment gameplay of Overwatch has never been better. Let’s hope the monetisation strategy relaxes a little.

Overwatch 2 is a soft reboot of the hero shooter, enabling a quicker pace thanks to 5v5, well-designed maps, and extensive rebalancing. There are still some progression and monetization issues to sort out, but this update/sequel establishes a solid foundation for the series' future.
Overwatch 2 is more akin to a big update than a full fledged sequel: it still is the fun, dynamic and charismatic shooter it was but the new free to play model is lackluster and predatory.

Overwatch 2 has a lot of common ground with the original, but it's managed to take what we know and breathe a new life into a landmark hero shooter. The fact that it’s now free-to-play means more people can enjoy this truly wonderful shooter, though the monetisation feels heavy handed compared to other games.

Overwatch 2 simply exists in an awkward transition phase between the game it was and the game it wants to be. It's not a revolution by any stretch, at least not how the original game was, but it's still Overwatch and that means it's still a ton of fun.

Even if at first glance it doesn't seem like a new game, the new environments and small and larger adjustments immediately make Overwatch 2 one of the most fun shooters of the moment. The fact that the game is free - except for battle passes and cosmetic tweaks for the various characters that you can buy with real money - and that it doesn't matter what platform you play on, are only additional arguments to quickly get the download up and running.

Overwatch 2 fulfills the mission of breathing new life into what was once one of the best multiplayer shooter, before falling into great oblivion because of Blizzard's lack of attention. Switching to 5v5 matches ushers in a new era in Overwatch battles and abilities, where one-on-one duels take precedence over tactical team play. New heroes and maps freshen up the environment, while the Push mode could become a fan favorite. Overwatch has everything to shine again, as long as it gets the attenti

Ultimately, we can only judge the product by what it is now, which is a rock-solid online multiplayer shooter. And despite all of us, like Tracer, experiencing that strange sense of déjà vu, Overwatch 2 is more than a 1.5 version of the original. It feels like an evolution of the original that makes for even better gameplay through subtle changes and additions. The new revenue model does leave a bad taste behind and is at odds with the sense of togetherness that Overwatch should radiate.

While the game is called Overwatch 2, this all seems more like a transition to a full new game. The biggest changes are dropping the 6v6 gameplay in favour of a much more exciting 5v5 format. Three new heroes are also available, along with a new game mode, but for a final and real verdict, everyone should wait for the upcoming PvE segment.

We suspected it a little, but it is sure now : Overwatch 2 is more an update of the first installment than a real sequel. Fortunately, the game became a free-to-play game and the news business model helps to forget this lack of new features and the fact that Blizzard has recycled a lot of things, like all the characters and stages.

This is, really, a relaunch dressed up as a sequel, although the game remains as fun as ever. Some balancing issues and, of course, a questionable progression system hinder the potential of Overwatch 2.

Fans of the original game will find very little to be excited about, but ultimately Overwatch 2 is a solid free to play hero shooter with polished gameplay and enough content to be worth checking out.
Overwatch 2 revitalizes the stagnant hero shooter, but misunderstands what made it great.

Overwatch 2 is the awaited game that keeps on the legacy of the 2016 hero-shooter of Blizzard. With little news and a lot of problems with servers and bugs, the experience of the game has been a complete nightmare for a lot of users. But, it's not a secret that the game core is mainly the same as before: tons of fun playing with friends and alone, with an even bigger roaster of heroes and maps, and even new game modes, Overwatch 2 is the game that the fans of the franchise will love.

Overwatch 2 replaces its predecessor with a similar, yet different experience, but its future feels uncertain as the franchise moves toward a more aggressive monetization structure with little to distinguish itself from every other live-service game.

Overwatch 2 does a lot of good, but at the same time almost everything went wrong before, around and after launch. It's hard to see the wood for all those trees of mistakes, when somewhere in that mess is still hiding one of the best shooters of the moment. Part two makes some much-needed improvements that make it play better in theory than ever before. Unfortunately, in practice, the many bugs and server problems predominated last week. It is therefore 'better' that part two is free-to-play, be

Overwatch 2’s launch issues are still awful. Like most of you, dear readers, I didn’t get an advance copy of the game or any special connection privileges. I was with all of you in the trenches, dealing with the constant error messages and inexhaustible queues. The game’s new battle pass system remains questionable and concerning.But I am very pleased with the core gameplay changes and where the game is headed. Game development is a group effort, and although Kaplan is deeply missed, I see these
Overwatch 2 is as fun and fast-paced as the original game, but its free-to-play structure sullies the experience a bit.

As things stand, Overwatch 2 is not so much a sequel as it is an update to the first chapter. Net of the changes made, we cannot consider what we have seen so far as something fully satisfactory or capable of justifying the number "to the right" of the title. The changes introduced are appreciable and the business model adopted could give new life to the franchise, but unfortunately, between the absence of the campaign and the progression of the characters, the real potential of the experience r

Although it is still a very fun and enjoyable online FPS game to play, the fact that the in-game economy is so greedy and focused on exploiting the player, and there isn't anything in game that deserves that "2" in the name lowered the overall score for me. Unfortunately Battle Passes are now a part of these games, but Overwatch 2 has exploited that a lot. At least base game is free.
Given a new lease of life by those whip-smart changes, in the moment-to-moment Overwatch 2 sings. [Issue#378, p.108]

Overwatch 2 is a free to play game, and I’d definitely recommend trying it out if you haven’t to see how you feel about the gameplay and to see how well it runs on Steam Deck. Despite its flaws, it is still worth playing, but some of the decisions with how certain content has been handled in-game are weird. Overwatch 2 is yet to reach the highs of the original Overwatch, but it is still a great multiplayer game to play on Steam Deck. I enjoyed Overwatch 2 quite a bit on Switch despite the visual

While Overwatch 2 is still a good time, and an improvement over the original, it feels like less of a sequel and more of an update. There's some solid changes to the foundations of Overwatch, but a noticeable lack of new content, archaic game modes, and balancing issues leave something to be desired.
There is a good game hiding underneath Overwatch 2's predatory shell, one that is soon to evolve into an even more massive headache for players as the game evolves.
While Overwatch 2 adds a host of heroes and features to Blizzard’s iconic FPS game, its PvP feels more like a simple content update than a full blown sequel.

For long-term players, Overwatch 2 may feel more like a glorified update with problematic progression and roadblocks to enjoyment that didn't exist in the original game. In its current state, Overwatch 2 is more product than art, but there is still plenty of time to turn things around between now and whenever the game's 1.0 version launches.

Fans who have continued to play Overwatch over the years grew hungry for the new maps, modes, and heroes that Overwatch 2 delivers on. At this point, it feels like the bare minimum fans need to keep enjoying Overwatch. As more maps and heroes are introduced, Overwatch 2 may start to feel more like a full sequel rather than a hefty much-needed update. If Blizzard had charged sixty dollars for what the game currently is, fans would be disappointed. The ten-dollar Battle Pass gives fans lots of con

Overwatch 2 delivers high-octane multiplayer gameplay and a wholly improved version of the much-loved hero shooter. Now that the dust has settled, it’s becoming clear that despite a number of teething issues, the game manages to do what many sequels struggle to; build upon a successful game, and make it even better.

So much damage has already been done to the game simply by calling it Overwatch 2. From the second of its announcement, Blizzard were at the mercy of players' expectations for a full-fledged sequel. And when they compare those two side-by-side screenshots and see virtually no difference, that's a major disappointment and perhaps a turn-off for many players. For a content update, Overwatch 2 does an absolutely phenomenal job. For a sequel, it feels pretty underwhelming. I wonder, would it have be

Enjoying Overwatch 2 is an exercise in cautious optimism — not just in the future direction of its ever-changing lore and world, but in the idea that years of new content will ultimately deliver on the promise of a full sequel.
Overwatch 2 shines and pops with updated graphics and gorgeous new maps, but its deviation from the source material is impossible to ignore.

Overwatch 2 is a great game, but it is held back by its own identity crisis, lack of content, and questionable choices. Despite its blemishes, Overwatch 2 is a must-try experience, especially if you are playing with a dedicated squad of friends. Whether you click with it right away, grow to love it, or try it and bounce off, the core gameplay of Overwatch 2 is undeniably fun regardless of its external flaws.
An aimless-feeling revamp of 2016's best multiplayer game, slightly coarsened by free-to-play grinding.

Overwatch 2 has a branding problem. The “2” implies something new or different or at least fundamentally distinguishable from what came before. But as it is, it’s just not, particularly for a numbered sequel to one of the most popular shooters of all time. Playing rekindled all those old, happy feelings I hadn’t experienced since the early days of Overwatch, and part of that is because it just feels the same. But it’s too early to say if that’s enough for Overwatch to make its comeback.

Don't be fooled by Overwatch 2's title: it's nowhere near a sequel, but a relaunch instead. Blizzard clearly took this opportunity to update (not necessarily enhance) pretty much every aspect of the game and catch up with every other hot, current multiplayer game. Is that a bad thing? Hell naw. OW2 is wildly fun.

참치마요볶음밥 재료 (1인분) - 밥 1공기 - 참치캔 1/2개 (기름 제거) - 마요네즈 1큰술 - 달걀 1개 - 대파 1/2대 (잘게 썰기) - 간장 1작은술 - 소금, 후추 약간 - 식용유 1큰술 - 김가루 (선택) 1. 팬에 식용유를 두르고 달걀을 넣어 스크램블로 만든 뒤 따로 덜어둡니다. 2. 같은 팬에 대파를 넣고 살짝 볶아 향을 냅니다. 3. 기름 뺀 참치를 넣고 볶다가, 밥을 넣고 잘 풀어가며 함께 볶습니다. 4. 마요네즈와 간장을 넣고 골고루 섞으며 볶습니다. 5. 스크램블 에그를 다시 넣고 섞은 뒤, 소금과 후추로 간을 맞춥니다. 6. 그릇에 담고 김가루를 뿌리면 완성입니다. + 김치, 옥수수, 치즈 등을 추가하면 더 맛있게 즐길 수 있습니다. 마요네즈 양을 조절하면 느끼함을 조절할 수 있습니다.

이거 솔직하게 말하자면 게임성 자체는 재밌음 이건 팩트임. 하고 싶으면 솔직히 말리고 싶지는 않음. 근데 운영을 진짜 더럽게 못함 그것 때문에 접는 사람도 꽤 있음. 탱커는 계속 약하게 만들어서 점점 스펙이 하향 평준화가 되고 있음. 원래 구데기 였던 애들이 op였던 애들이 구데기가 되서 구데기였던 애들은 버프 하나 안받았는데 사람 수준 되는 꼴 보면 열 불 터짐. 반대로 힐러는 점점 상향 평준화가 되고 있음 힐러는 일반인 상식으로 보면 힐만 하고 공격력은 약한 게 정상 이지만 이겜은 힐러가 웬만한 딜러보다 더 쎄거나 비등비등함 물론 약한 애들도 있긴한데 힐러는 계속 버프를 받음. 이게 왜 그러냐면 게임 메타를 블리자드 애들이 모름. 또 북미 서버가 개빡침. 얘네 여론에 따라 버프 너프를 하는 데 한국 서버는 픽하기만 해고 트롤소리 듣는 캐릭터를 북미 얘네는 겁나 많이씀. 그래서 쓰레기가 너프를 받고 씹사기가 버프를 받음. 이게 뭔상황이냐면 간신히 산소호흡기 달고 있는 사람 팔다리
