title: Monster Hunter Rise
critic_response:
몬스터 헌터 라이징은 스위치 버전의 완벽한 PC 버전이라고 평가되며, 높은 프레임률과 개선된 성능을 제공한다. 그러나 한 리뷰는 PC 버전이 스위치 버전과 크게 다르지 않다고 지적했다. 또한, 게임이 더 어렵고 환경적으로 매력적이지 않다는 의견도 있다. 전반적으로 몬스터 헌터 라이징은 우수한 게임이며, 품질과 완성도가 높다.
장점: 높은 프레임률과 개선된 성능을 제공한다.
단점: PC 버전이 스위치 버전과 크게 다르지 않다. / 게임이 더 어렵고 환경적으로 매력적이지 않다.
critic_contexts: Monster Hunter Rise is a brilliant addition to the Monster Hunter lineup. It delivers incredible combat with newly added monsters and highly-desired mobility. However, it does suffer from messy systems, a mediocre story and can become quite grindy. Despite these flaws though, I have had countless hours of fun and will continue to do so. The co-op aspect just adds even more enjoyment to an already enjoyable game. I just wish the system UI had been redone for PC.
Monster Hunter Rise’s combat is excellent, and the PC version is virtually a perfect port of the Switch version. As someone who had never played a Monster Hunter game before, I’m hooked.

Monster Hunter Rise on PC is the definitive version of an excellent entry in this long running franchise and whether you are a veteran of the series or a rookie, looking to jump in for the first time, it’s the perfect opportunity to grab a weapon and start hunting some monsters.

The PC port for Monster Hunter Rise is the definitive way to experience this game. I am excited to see where the franchise goes from here, although I will find it hard to slide back into any Monster Hunter game without the new traversal and streamlined elements. Whether you want to hunt alone or play with your friends, play “Fashion Hunter” or experience the main quests, there is something for everyone in Monster Hunter Rise.

Monster Hunter is a series about systems—Capcom can’t escape that, no matter how much marketing and PR they throw at it. But it isn’t a series about getting it either, as much as its staunchest adherents would have you believe. It’s about feeling it. And Monster Hunter Rise feels good—simple as that.

When considering the PC version of Rise, it’s hard not to compare it directly to the as-yet unmatched juggernaut that is Monster Hunter World – a competition that I don’t think Rise wins. That said, Monster Hunter Rise is still an enjoyable, well-crafted game with some compelling innovations that make it a wonderful addition to the Monster Hunter franchise. Plus, you can make a fire-breathing T-Rex suplex a dragon. If that doesn’t persuade you, nothing will.

Monster Hunter Rise remains a stellar title on the PC, hardly losing any ground from the greatness it already achieved earlier this year on the Nintendo Switch. The game looks better than ever now that powerful hardware improves the graphics, giving everything from the monsters to the environments a nice polish. Unfortunately, however, the new format doesn’t always take the various kinds of players into account. Veteran players will likely grow tired of skipping through the tutorials again, and
Monster Hunter Rise stands toe-to-toe with World, especially on PC. Its end-game grind may not be as robust, but a serious focus on fun and community over narrative and fidelity pays off.

The PC version of Monster Hunter Rise is a solid port that in general does not differ much from what we saw on Switch. Therefore, praise and complaints about the Switch edition mostly still apply. It’s a bit disappointing that the year that has passed between the releases brought us little more than graphical improvements, but the game itself is still stellar.

With an accelerated combat system, some light grind and well-designed monsters, Monster Hunter Rise will provide you with many hours of adrenaline-filled fun. The experience that this game provides in multiplayer is unique, regardless of the complicated formation of the party. Definitely a version that every PC gamer should have in their Steam library.
Monster Hunter Rise blends the best elements from earlier series entries, and delivers a streamlined, action-packed, monster-slaying experience.

I strongly recommend Monster Hunter Rise, but this isn’t World. It’s harder, less environmentally appealing, and more focused on the rivalry between the hunters and the monsters. Gaining the pieces to build that armor or make that weapon is sometimes a bit more of a grind as the RNG isn’t quite as good but the satisfaction as always is there and strong. But amazing combat and great rewards keep the fun moving, albeit very differently from its most recent predecessor.

Monster Hunter Rise combines all of the quality of life features introduced in World while keeping the best parts of the earlier games in the series, resulting in the most refined Monster Hunter title to date.

Despite releasing nearly a year after its Switch counterpart, the PC version of Monster Hunter Rise feels like a minimal improvement. Sure, you can get frame rates in the hundreds and high resolutions, but frequent crashes can reset your settings and cutscenes are still locked at 30. The keyboard and mouse control options are a big boon for accessibility, but the visual improvements here feel so minimal I’ll stick to the portable edition.

Monster Hunter Rise has lost none of its appeal in moving from a Nintendo Switch screen to a PC one. In Ultrawide, with an unlocked frame-rate, it’s the best way to play what may be the franchise’s best entry yet. If you’re looking for deep RPG systems, a T-Rex that breathes fire, and a huge hammer to smack it in the head with, then Monster Hunter Rise is essential.
Indeed, although it is identical in terms of content to the Nintendo Switch version on PC, it is a total success and remains an excellent gateway for beginners.

Monster Hunter Rise is probably the best game in the series. It isn’t quite as accessible as World, but long term it offers much more varied combat and a richer experience. Its world and gameplay are insanely engaging and fun. Technically, the port to PC results in solid, smooth gameplay and marginally better visuals. If you have a choice, it’s definitely the way to play, the best and most complete version of one of 2021’s standout games.
Monster Hunter Rise isn’t as ambitious as its predecessor, but it still manages to build on the franchise in meaningful ways.

Monster Hunter Rise is a big step forward for the Monster Hunter series, both in terms of its design and the quality of its PC port. The port is nothing groundbreaking, but the MH team has done themselves proud with this, and I’m just glad to be able to play this game at a high framerate.

Beyond mounting monsters and using the wirebug to wall-run in spectacular fashion, not much else distinguishes Monster Hunter Rise from the installments that came before. It has enough endgame content to keep you occupied long after the credits roll (if you don’t mind copious amounts of grinding) and multiplayer is still the optimal way to play, but the excitement of my early hunts waned before long. Monster Hunter Rise is far from being the next definitive chapter in the series. Nevertheless, i
Improves on what was already a fantastic game in almost every way.

Monster Hunter Rise was an excellent chapter of the series on Switch, but the PC version plays in a completely different category. If you liked Monster Hunter World, you'll love Rise. Yes, even if the thought of starting all over again terrifies you.

It seems that it was a simple task to bring Monster Hunter Rise to PC because the result was pretty okay, especially when it comes to performance. The only bad thing is that its graphics don't look better, even if the resolution is 4K. Nevertheless, it's worth your time, you're going to have fun from first to the last hunt.
MH Rise refines the combat system with clever innovations that both veterans and newcomers will enjoy - if they can overlook the low-grade graphics and story.

It is an HD upgrade, but its graphic improvements are enough to amply enhance the monster hunting experience of Monster Hunter: Rise, and this makes it one of the best action games on PC. If you have not played this game on Nintendo Switch, I strongly recommend you buy this game and experience the new action system and Wirebugs in Monster Hunter: Rise. But if you are a seasoned hunter who has defeated the Thunder Serpent Narwa, you may choose to wait and make a decision when the DLC is released

Monster Hunter Rise is a great game with an even better PC port that I believe is the best way to enjoy the game especially if you're a hardcore fan of the series that prefers to hunt monsters in the most optimal way possible.

Monster Hunter Rise is a great game that’s even better on PC, but it’s hard to recommend if you’ve already played it on the Switch and don’t have unlimited free time. If you managed to get a Steam Deck preorder, though, this could well be the perfect game to christen it with for anyone looking to get into the series. It’s just unfortunate that Monster Hunter enthusiasts who already jumped on Rise will have to start from scratch. I hope Capcom releases future Monster Hunter games on PC at the sam

Monster Hunter Rise arrives on PC in dazzling form, complete with support for 4K resolution, unlocked frame-rate and ultrawide monitors. Too bad we’re not able to carry the old save into this new version that will force you to start all over again.
An excellent port that presents a solid, complete experience. Super fast load times, improved performance and a ton of content make Monster Hunter Rise on PC the definitive version.
Monster Hunter Rise is at its best on the PC. The extra power brings a smoother, prettier experience to an already cracking game.
Monster Hunter Rise looks better on PC, runs better on PC, has more control & online options on PC, and arrives with more content at its respective launch.

Overall, Monster Hunter Rise on PC is another perfect example of why the series belongs on computer. It offers all the excellent experiences found on the Nintendo Switch, albeit with improved visuals, better resolution, a myriad of performance options, and greater controller support. Hopefully there comes a day when PC players get to experience a Monster Hunter title at the same time as console players, because the series has shown that it thrives on PC, with a playerbase ready to dive into it w

Monster Hunter Rise on PC has all the updates and new content added to the Switch version right from the get go with vastly improved performance and graphics. While the visuals could've been improved further, this is still the best way to play Monster Hunter Rise.
Monster Hunter Rise on PC is the best place to play this incredible creature battler. If the series was looking for a permanent home, PC players would be more than happy to see it moving in.

With the streamlining of some of the most annoying aspects of the Monster Hunter experience and some excellent additions to the formula like Wirebugs and Switch Skills, Monster Hunter Rise reaches the heights of the best entries in the series with ease. While not all of the changes and new features have been for the best, like Rampage Quests, their general quality level, and the amazing PC port, do make the game one that's worth playing for both long-time fans of the series and newcomers.

For the most part, though, Monster Hunter Rise is another stonking addition to the series, and in my books a more than worthy successor to Monster Hunter: World. There's a generous and playful sense of freedom here that keeps combat and exploration feeling fresh, and the momentum of its hunting-led missions means you're rarely spinning your wheels as you seek out that last elusive armour part. It's kept me playing much longer than I ever did with World, and I can't wait to see how it develops wi

Overall, the PC port of Monster Hunter Rise is superior to the Nintendo Switch version, thanks to the PC enhancements. It has some of the best combat mechanics in the Monster Hunter series, the new monsters are an absolute delight to hunt, and the improved frame rate and resolutions enrich the hunting experience. It does have a couple of flaws, but they are minor issues in the grand scheme of things.

Monster Hunter Rise on PC is just a different way to play a fantastic game. And while that’s great news for PC players who’ve never bought into Nintendo’s ecosystem, its graphics fail to impress, even on the most impressive of hardware.
An impressive port, with a good amount of graphical options and great optimization. Indeed the best way to enjoy Rise's amazing combat system.

Almost a year after the debut of Monster Hunter Rise on Switch, the expectation of fans on PC has been rewarded with a valuable port, which not only preserves the merits of the original version but enhances them with a good collection of technical improvements. Net of some fluctuations in the overall yield of the proposal, the new edition of Rise therefore has all the credentials to offer the people of Steam dozens of hours of hunting fun and epic clashes, enhanced by an unprecedented fluidity f
This is a fantastic adaptation of a fantastic game, that plays and looks better than ever on PC.

Monster Hunter Rise is another entertaining entry in the venerated franchise that continues to scratch a unique itch. New players will be eased into the franchise and returning players will enjoy hunting with friends as well. Capcom has also delivered yet another good port that definitely makes this version the one to play.

A hundred hours in, Rise does feel a little rote. The lack of quest variety and endgame options besides grinding for talismans has begun to wear on me somewhat. But the journey of getting to this point has been nothing short of an incredible experience. From zipping through the air to bludgeon monsters in the face to setting out on gathering runs with my trusty palamute, the minute-to-minute gameplay of Rise is impeccable. The only real issue with Rise is that there isn’t more of it, but with pl

Thanks to a beautifully realised setting and some genuinely innovative combat additions, Monster Hunter Rise is fun from start to finish. If you haven’t played a Monster Hunter game yet, this is the best one to start with.

Monster Hunter Rise is a successful and worthy port, its fantastic gameplay and content more than compensate for the somewhat outdated graphics on PC. It's still clean and rather pleasant to look at during the fights, and especially very fluid. Those who enjoyed Monster Hunter World shouldn't hesitate to jump in, it's an equally valid game in the license, even if it's a bit different. For the others, it's also a gateway to the license, with improvements in comfort and accessibility, as well as a

This PC port fulfills its role very well because it cancels a lot of limitations of the Switch version. The framerate is no longer stuck at 30 frames per second, the resolution can go up to 4K, some textures are much more detailed, and loading times are noticeably reduced. However, we should not expect either that this former Nintendo exclusive will miraculously transform into graphic mastery worthy of the biggest productions of 2022.
Monster Hunter: Rise is arguably the greatest entry in Capcom's flagship series, and a game that simply never stops giving.
user_response:
몬스터 헌터 라이즈는 선브레이크부터 시작하는 것이 좋으며, 라이즈 본편은 건너뛰어도 돈값 한다는 의견이 있습니다. 한 리뷰는 월드 시리즈보다 라이트한 게임플레이와 조작감이 더 쾌적하다고 느꼈으며, 옛날 몬헌 조작감에 더 가깝다고 평가했습니다. 빠른 포텐, 속도감, 긴장감, 도파민을 즐기는 플레이어들에게 적합하며, 성장에 따른 밧줄벌레의 업그레이드로 플레이 스타일의 변화도 경험할 수 있습니다. 그러나 일본풍 배경이 과하게 느껴질 때가 있으며, 무게감 있는 타격감이 사라져 게임이 너무 가벼워진다는 의견도 있습니다.
장점: 빠른 포텐과 속도감을 즐기는 플레이어에게 적합합니다. / 성장하며 변화하는 플레이 스타일을 경험할 수 있습니다. / 다양한 무기를 사용할 수 있습니다.
단점: 일본풍 배경이 과하게 느껴질 때가 있습니다. / 무게감 있는 타격감이 사라져 게임이 너무 가벼워집니다. / 엔드 콘텐츠가 지루하고 짜치는 편입니다. / 조작감이 이상한 부분이 있습니다.
user_contexts: 일단 한마디 하자면, 리뷰 쓴 사람 중 플레이시간 10~20시간 되는 사람들 말은 싹 다 무시하는게 좋다. 이 게임은 라이즈는 껍질이고 선브레이크가 본체인데, 저 사람들은 싱싱한 수박의 당도높은 빨간 속살이 아닌 맛대가리없는 껍질만 갉아먹다 지 멋대로 포기한 족속들이니까. 게임의 역사에 대해 간략하게 알면 내가 왜 이런 말을 하는지 이해가 갈 것이다. 초기에, 야스노리란 무책임한 디렉터가 게임을 미완성인 채로 내놨다. 이 머저리는 뭐가 그렇게 맘에 안들었는지 비 가드무기들의 주 생존수단인 구르기와 스텝의 회피성능을 미친 수준으로 너프해서 아예 대놓고 쓰지 말라고 막아놨다. 그러면서 주 메타로 미는 카운터 및 슈퍼아머 기술들의 분배는 중구난방으로 해서 무기별 밸런스가 다 박살났다. 거기에 억지로 플레이타임을 늘리려는 의도가 뻔히 보이는 인혼조 시스템을 넣어놨고, 쪼한 재미도 없고 보상도 형편없고 무기에 따라 불합리한 난이도가 되는 백룡야행이란 희대의 똥을 쏴놨다. 거기에 메인보스 몹으

초반 완전 맨땅으로 7시간정도 해본 느낌이 아이스본 인도하는 땅을 3~4백시간 솔플한 느낌보다 의외로 더 피곤함. 물론 세트 장비 받아서 하니깐 당연히 할만해짐. 썬브레이크가 본편보다 훨씬 잼있다니 기대가 됨. 초반에 하는 백룡야행은 어차피 혼자 깨야함. 2026년 2월 현재 도와주는 사람 없음, 이거 못깨면 라이즈 접어야 함. 왜냐면 퀘스트 진행 불가. 초보들은 여기서 대부분 접을꺼라 예상됨 라이즈 보다 월드 아이스본 먼저 하길 추천. 월드 아이스본한 사람들한테는 백룡야행 별거아님. 라이즈 뉴비 절단기 백룡야행 (도와주는 사람 전혀 없음) 월드 아이스본 뉴비 절단기는 토비카가치 아종부터 벨리오로스 등등등 (구조요청하면 고인물들 항시 대기중) 백룡야행 솔플은 반격의징 타이밍 보면 무난. 근데 벌써 70시간 되는데 솔플이 강제되다보니 쫌 지루하고 피곤하다. ( 반면에 1년전에 몬헌 월드 처음 할때는 사람들이랑 같이 하니 엄청 잼있었는데 ) 라이즈는 대형 몬스터가 슈퍼아머 걸린 상태로

title: Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order
critic_response:
스타워즈 팬들에게 최고의 게임이라는 평가를 받으며, 높은 그래픽 퀄리티와 60프레임 퍼 초의 부드러운 게임플레이를 제공한다. 게임은 '진정한 스타워즈 게임'으로 평가되며, 적절한 긴장감과 탐험의 균형을 제공한다. 그러나 한 리뷰는 게임의 내비게이션과 불필요한 백앤포스가 게임의 재미를 감소시킨다고 지적했다. 또한, 기술적인 문제가 게임의 잠재력을 저해한다는 의견도 있다. 전반적으로, 스타워즈 제다이: 폴른 오더는 스타워즈 팬들에게 최고의 게임 중 하나로 평가된다.
장점: 게임은 '진정한 스타워즈 게임'으로 평가되며, 적절한 긴장감과 탐험의 균형을 제공한다.
단점: 한 리뷰는 게임의 내비게이션과 불필요한 백앤포스가 게임의 재미를 감소시킨다고 지적했다. / 기술적인 문제가 게임의 잠재력을 저해한다는 의견도 있다. / 과도한 적 리스폰과 불필요한 백트래킹으로 인해 게임의 재미가 감소할 수 있다.
critic_contexts: Fallen Order is a must have for Star Wars fans and the best Star Wars game in years. Both literally and figuratively: the Force is strong with this one. [01/2020, p.42]
Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order gives its best on PC, delivering an awesome experience in 4K and 60 frames per second with advanced visual effects.
An excellent entry into the new Star Wars canon.

Fallen Order is a decent alternative to Dark Souls if you’re looking for a less hardcore experience. It leaves a pleasant aftertaste thanks to a well-directed ending, which makes you wonder if this game could have been so much better if it fully embraced its inner Uncharted instead of trying to wear two hats.

As underwhelmed as I was by Fallen Order, I hope that its inevitable success will encourage EA to do more with this franchise, since it makes a powerful case for the resonance of Star Wars — if this mediocre casserole of concepts yanked from superior titles didn’t have the license attached, I doubt anyone would be talking about it.

With Jedi Fallen Order, Respawn have made a small miracle and have delivered a stellar single-player Star Wars game. I daresay that JFO resembles what a modern Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast would have if it combined the best elements of gameplay defining games, sensibly unified and supported by a story which sets the rules without trying to be ground-breaking.
Technical issues marr an otherwise slick adventure. A must for Star Wars fans.

With an excellent droid companion and entertaining lightsaber combat, Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order could have been a truly forceful addition to the franchise. However, navigation problems and unnatural level design takes it back down a darker path.

Being an EA game, albeit developed by Respawn Entertainment, I had some doubts, but they were quickly dispelled. It stands as proof that single-player games can stand on their own. The only problem is that if you rush through it, you could finish Fallen Order in about 12 hours, but that excludes finding the secrets and unlocking all the cool stuff. Lastly, I have to mention the name, Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order. To me, it sounds like Star Wars Jedi could be a new line of games, with Fallen Orde

Star Wars Jedi Fallen Order is yet another excellent game developed by Respawn. With its excellent story, charming characters, engaging platforming and combat mechanics, the game is a must-buy for any self-respecting Star Wars fan, despite a lack of innovation which doesn't truly harm the experience as a whole.
Although lacking in innovation, Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order is the best Star Wars game of all time.

Fallen Order is a game whose worst moments are serviceable, and best moments are memorable. It’s worth experiencing by Star Wars fans hungry for an original story that doesn’t settle for trite, and action game players looking for a decent – albeit flawed – combat.

Jedi: Fallen Order borrows liberally from other games, but a strong supporting cast, clever level design, and a cute little droid companion make Respawn's Star Wars story more than the sum of its parts.

Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order certainly tries, but as Yoda pointed out, you either do or do not. For the most part, it does -- but without the grace or precision of a full-fledged Jedi Knight. The apprentice is not yet the master.
Falling Order mixes acrobatics with exploration and challenging light saber battles. Excessive enemy respawns and unnecessary backtracking reduce the enjoyment, though.

Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order could have been a good, short, but intense action game, lasting about ten hours, but instead, it swallows the player in unnecessary back and forths, artificial obstacles, puzzles and chests hunts, which burden the rhythm and fun. We come out all the more frustrated by a predictable story that goes nowhere, as well as by its combat system very close to Sekiro's, but which is far from reaching its level of refinement and control, especially on the bosses.

Respawn did nothing to reinvent the wheel, but instead married a bunch of popular gameplay genres together into a cohesive whole, wrapped up in well-crafted Star Wars story. It may not be KOTOR levels of greatness, but it's good. And I think that's exactly what fans needed right about now.

I was astonished at how much I enjoyed Jedi Fallen Order. Not because it’s a great game, it inhabits a niche that we haven’t seen much of this generation. It’s merely a good game. I ran into a ton of glitches and collision issues. Still, the overall package was entertaining enough to ignore them for the most part.
Fallen Order wants you to feel like a fighty, telepathic space priest, and for about 20 hours or so, it does that. Stick with it, and you might also find a cool, yellow lightsaber.

Gameplay is solid from the start and gains depth, transforming you into a Jedi badass. Respawn has also nailed the Star Wars universe, for better (sights, sounds, and cinematic flair) and worse (cringey dialogue and vacuous plot).

It's good enough that its host of technical problems feels like an affront to what the game could have been, and to the hard work and talent--and there's a considerable amount of talent here--of the people who made it.

Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order hits the target by offering the right mix of adrenaline-pumping action and thoughtful exploration. The metroidvania mechanics are grafted to perfection within the game formula, and the same can be said for those borrowed from the latest productions of From Software. Without introducing anything really new, Respawn Entertainment manages to merge all these elements and give birth to a fascinating and satisfying experience, as well as being consistent with the Star Wars

I love Star Wars, I love video games, and Fallen Order is a glorious amalgamation of the two. Though I’ve experienced everything the game has to offer, I genuinely can’t wait to play through it again on the hardest difficulty. At this point, I can only hope Respawn follows it up with a sequel because the force is remarkably strong with this one.

Much like what Arkham Asylum did for Batman games, Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order sets the new standard for Star Wars titles. Not only is it a great Star Wars game, it is also the best game of the year.
Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order is the ideal example of a Star Wars game in 2019. It's an expert blend of multiple genres.
Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order is an absolute masterpiece. Well paced, written, and executed, the game is Respawn’s best thus far.

Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order is the Star Wars game that fans have been crying out for years now. While it doesn't do anything new, its clear inspirations from Dark Souls and Tomb Raider have helped to create an epic, sprawling adventure that'll have you ready for more the moments the credits roll. If only there had been more of it, because once I started, I didn't want to stop.

Thanks to a mix-and-match of different genres Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order is the best Star Wars-game is years. Combat and traversal is challenging and diverse, puzzles will require your brain and Force powers are cleverly used in every part of the game. Besides all that it even provides a more than decent story for fans of the franchise.
Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order makes up for a lot of lost time with a fantastic single-player action-adventure that marks the return of the playable Jedi.

Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order captured the magic of growing as a Jedi, overcoming insurmountable odds through fellowship, and the power of hope in the midst of despair. If Respawn’s delivery of Jedi: Fallen Order is the future of the Star Wars franchise out of EA, the franchise is in incredible hands. The Force is strong with these ones.

Despite its polish and best ideas, I wish I'd just reinstalled both Force Unleashed games, and I encourage anybody eager for a fun, Force-filled, third-person Star Wars experience this year to do the same.

Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order is a dream come true for many Star Wars fans. It perfectly combines great gameplay with exploration, puzzles and a personal story. Sadly a lackluster ending and some technical and graphical issues tarnish the overall positive experience somewhat.

Developer Respawn builds a Star Wars adventure that constantly reminds me why I fell in love with the Skywalker saga as a child. Fallen Order is a love letter to the classic trilogy, respects the clone wars, and shows me the dark era of the Empire like no other Star Wars story before.

I greatly enjoyed my time playing Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order. The combat feels great, even though it can get repetitive, and the lightsaber gameplay is some of the best there has ever been in a Star Wars game. There are so many aspects of Jedi: Fallen Order that I loved, and I hope we get to see more from Respawn in the future. For now, I think this is the Star Wars game we’ve been looking for.

스타워즈 제다이: 몰락한 오더는 전반적으로 좋은 퀄리티의 게임으로 평가됩니다. 다만, 일부 유저들은 길찾기에 어려움을 겪었고, 조작감에 대해서도 아쉬움을 표현했습니다. 게임의 진행 장벽은 마스터 모드가 적당하다는 평가도 있습니다. 게임의 콘텐츠는 풍부하며, BD-1이라는 캐릭터도 귀엽고 매력적입니다. 다만, 일부 유저들은 게임의 맵 디자인에 대해 아쉬움을 표현했습니다. 전반적으로, 스타워즈 제다이: 몰락한 오더는 스타워즈 팬들에게 좋은 평가를 받고 있습니다. 게임의 스토리와 세계관은 아름답고, 플레이어는 이 게임에 빠져들게 됩니다.
장점: 매력적인 스토리와 그래픽으로 몰입감이 좋습니다. / 풍부한 콘텐츠와 귀여운 BD-1 캐릭터로 재미있습니다. / 좋은 퀄리티의 게임으로 가성비가 좋습니다.
단점: 길찾기에 어려움을 겪을 수 있습니다. / 조작감에 아쉬움이 있을 수 있습니다.

제다이: 길찾기의 몰락 스토리: 노잼 그래픽: 5070ti로도 144방어가 안된다. 프레임 드랍 겁나 심함. 조작감: 이정도면 내 손이 문제인가? 라는 말이 절로 나옴. 맵디자인: 디지털 쓰레기. 액션: 전투훈련도 받은 제다이의 공격 모션이 단 4개뿐인게 말이 됨? 하다보면 나오는 말: 대체 이 길은 왜 여기로 감?
진심 길 개 ㅈ같고 조작감은 진심 패드립 나올뻔 했음 하다가 스트레스 받음 걍 할인안하면 절때 사지 마셈 물론 무조건 할인 구매 ㄱ 그나마 스킬 새로 해금될떄 마다 재미는 있어서 일단은 하는중 후,
이 게임 음 아주 조옷같은 점이 있는데 나는 광선검 액션을 즐기고싶은데 퍼즐만 ㅈ나게 풀다가 지쳐서 낙오했음 퍼즐 너무 싫다 진짜 재미도없고

스타워즈 팬이 아니었음에도 굉장히 재미있게 플레이했습니다 리뷰를 찾아봤을때 길찾기가 어렵고 복귀시 빠른이동이 없어 불편하다는 이야기가 있었는데 맵을 보면서 가면 길찾기 어렵지않고 복귀시 이동할때 안 가본길로도 가보고 전에는 못갔었던 곳을 이번에는 가면서 보상도 받고 하면서 굉장히 재미있게 했습니다 길찾기가 어렵다 하는 사람들은 아마 게임 1시간만 하고 내일하고 그런거같은데 쭉 이어서 하다보면 길이 자연스럽게 대충이라도 외워져서 스트레스 받지도 않습니다 단점이라고 할건 크게 없고 가끔씩 외형이나 모션이 이상하게 보이는게 있습니다만 그렇게 큰건 아닙니다 본인이 게임을 하고싶으면 사서 하시면 되고 1시간 깔딱하면서 도파민채우고 싶으면 다른거 하시면 됩니다 이상한 거짓음해에 속지마시고 믿고 하십쇼 19년도에 나온 게임이었음에도 세련되고 재미있었습니다 고유명사가 나오기때문에 사전에 영화나 자료 참조는 해보시면 좋습니다

생각 날 때 마다 조금씩 한다는 것이, 몇 년만에 엔딩을 보게 되었다. 스타워즈는 사실 1, 2, 3. 그것도 어릴 때 본게 전부라 설정이나 캐릭터 정도만 대충 아는 수준이었는데 평이 좋아서 충동적으로 구매, 초반에는 취향에 잘 안 맞는 것 같고, 스타워즈를 엄청 좋아하는 것도 아니라 첫 행성 도중에 접었었다. 이후 소울라이크 시리즈를 좀 하면서, 야숨 엔딩을 본 후에 패링이나 길찾기, 퍼즐에 대한 거부감이 좀 줄어서 다시 도전하게 되었고, 드디어 엔딩을 보았다. 스타워즈의 광선검 액션과, 포스를 활용한 액션은, 영화에서 보는 정도의 즐거움은 충분히 준 작품이다. 스토리 자체도 나름 몰입감있고, 루즈하지 않게 잘 짜여졌다고 생각돼서 플레이 자체의 즐거움은 꽤 있었다. 하지만 길찾기와, 동선이 첫 플레이부터 엔딩까지 계속해서 불편함을 줘서, 아쉬움이 남는다 엔딩을 보기도 전에 후속작 서바이버를 미리 샀는데, 폴른 오더 엔딩의 여운이 충분히 가신 후에 서바이버도 도전해보고자 한다. 스타
스타워즈를 모르고 해도 괜찮은 작품. 광선검 휘두르고 광선총 튕겨내는 것 만으로도 재밌다. 대신 길찾기에 스트레스 받을 각오 이빠이하고 하셈.

길 찾는 게 어려운 거 빼면 진짜 고트 갓겜 캐릭터, 스토리와 그래픽, 전투 모두 좋앗지만 가장 좋았던 건 연출력. 영화 그 이상의 연출이라 정말 좋았다. 스덕이라면 무조건해야함 제다이가 될 수 있음

title: God of War Ragnarök
critic_response:
God of War Ragnarök는 뛰어난 최적화와 인상적인 그래픽스를 제공하는 우수한 PC 포트입니다. 그러나 한 리뷰는 게임이 전작만큼 흥미롭지 않다고 느꼈습니다. 전반적으로 게임은 높은 시각적 품질과 뛰어난 성능을 제공합니다. 게임의 포트에 대한 평가도 긍정적입니다. Jetpack Interactive의 포트 작업은 산타 모니카의 타이틀을 더욱 향상시킵니다. PC 버전은 플레이스테이션 버전의 모든 것을 제공하며, 시각적으로도 뛰어납니다.
장점: 게임의 포트도 훌륭하며, PC 버전은 플레이스테이션 버전의 모든 것을 제공합니다. / 게임은 높은 시각적 품질과 뛰어난 성능을 제공합니다.
단점: 버튼 프롬프트 문제도 한 리뷰는에게 발생합니다.
critic_contexts: There isn’t much left to say about God of War Ragnarök that hasn’t already been said with its console release. The PC version is an excellent port with impressive optimization, allowing this masterpiece to be enjoyed by an even larger audience.
The God of War: Ragnarök PC version improves the quality of the PS5, with improvements in performance and shadows that generate a more polished and epic visual experience.

It isn't perfect, but God of War: Ragnarok remains a very good adventure game. The main story might meander for a good chunk of time, but the tale in the DLC makes up for that nicely. The action remains solid from beginning to end, and the usual additions made to the PC version and the disabling of hints almost makes up for the questionable decision to make a PSN login mandatory. If that last part isn't a deal-breaker, then you'll have a great time with Ragnarok on the PC.
Perfected craft distilled into a work of art.

Even if I don’t enjoy God of War Ragnarök’s cutscenes, having this second chance to play the game now that it’s out on PC has at least made me realize that the newer version of Kratos isn’t as much of a reboot as I previously thought. I wouldn’t say I love the new games as much as I did the originals, and I definitely would’ve enjoyed this game more if it felt a little more focused on action and less on dialogue and characters, but God of War Ragnarök is, at the end of the day, a pretty good con

God of War Ragnarok follows its predecessor’s path for its PC port, offering players the best version of the adventures of Kratos and Atreus. Of course the game takes advantage of the power of PCs and their exclusive technologies to push even further the visual quality, yet already impressive on PlayStation 5: native 4k, framerate unlocked, DLSS or FSR are particularly in the game to offer optimal performance for the vast majority of configurations.

God of War Ragnarok on PC brings everything that made the PlayStation version fantastic, and visually, it even surpasses it. Even if you've already played the game, it's worth experiencing it again in its full glory.

If only all PC game ports were this polished. Ragnarök runs smoothly on PC, with no crashes, offering all the post-launch content from the original game and introducing a few small but welcome updates. Definitely a thumbs up.

As far as the game itself is concerned, God of War Ragnarok could best be described as a safe sequel. Ragnarok focuses primarily on storytelling and big cinematic moments while refusing to take any chances when it comes to combat, exploration or other parts of the gameplay. This refusal to innovate sometimes backfires as there are plenty of moments when the narrative isn't strong enough to carry the rest of the game. God of War Ragnarok is mostly an enjoyable experience, especially when you incl

The port is beautiful and it’s hard to complain about its technical aspects. Unfortunately, the game itself, while still good, is not as fascinating as its predecessors. One simply expects more from a series with such reputation, especially when it comes to the joy of smashing things up.
I can say without a doubt that Ragnarok takes advantage of the PC’s power better than any of the other games from the Playstation family.

Even though it doesn't bring significant graphical improvements over the PS5 version and takes up a colossal amount of space on your hard drive, God of War Ragnarok on PC is a good port that has great image quality and decent performance.

God of War Ragnarok for PC establishes itself as a more than worthy version of Santa Monica Studio's acclaimed title. With solid performance, a good range of options and a few minor improvements, Kratos can now tackle fatherhood in style.
God of War Ragnarok is just as stunning as it was on PS5, and those wanting to enjoy such an incredible game now have the chance on PC.

The base of the PC port is good, there is good performance, although there are more various minor problems in various areas. Basically, if you enjoyed the first game with Kratos and want more from this world, don't miss the second one.

God of War: Ragnarök is quite a piece for its high visual quality and exceptional performance. It is not necessary to use DLSS or FSR to enjoy the game. Although playing with a mouse and keyboard can be difficult but it is a successful PlayStation port. The graphics, combined with the immersion provided by the Dualsense, make PC players feel comfortable experiencing this acclaimed franchise.

God of War: Ragnarök's PC port is certainly packed with features, and while most players won’t encounter some of the issues outlined above, the button prompts problem can remain an annoyance for those who like the Classic control scheme. It’s also possible some of the bugs we ran into were just strange accidents, as we cannot replicate them again aside from Atreus’ green hair on Deck. With that in mind, while PS5 may still be the ideal way to play, the PC version will allow players to experience

God of War: Ragnarok lands on PC with a porting that is perfect, prudent to the right point and wise beyond measure. Thanks to scalability and optimization, practically anyone can enjoy the exploits of Kratos and Atreus. If you also have a recent gaming PC, then your journey into the myth will be absolutely unforgettable.

With all the video game movies being made these days (some good some bad)… it is not surprising that God Of War is joining their ranks. The best thing is whether the movie is a success or not… you can play God Of War Ragnarok and feel like you are playing a movie right now! If you’ve enjoyed the previous games or want to see what it’s all about… grab a copy. It’s a brilliant game on many levels and there’s plenty of lore to get caught up in.

God of War Ragnarök comes to PC with a very polished and well-executed version if we talk about performance level, performing fantastically well on a high-end PC and also doing a good job on a more modest computer such as one of the "Steam Deck" pocket computers that are now present on the market.

God of War Ragnarök for PC is the ultimate way to experience this epic Norse adventure without the need for a monster PC. In terms of graphics and performance, the game rarely drops a stitch, but the PC port manages to elevate the already sky-high level of the PlayStation version to an even higher standard.
God of War Ragnarök isn't the cleanest sequel, but it leaves Sony's angriest franchise in a great place.
A fabulous port of a fabulous game, God of War Ragnarok combines great story telling, great action, and great graphics to deliver a top notch experience.

God of War Ragnarok for PC does not boast at any time, and implements a number of restrictions difficult to justify, but ultimately we have a functional port that allows us to enjoy in all its glory of this jewel of the PlayStation catalog.

God of War Ragnarok is one of Sony's best games in recent years and it is on PC very quickly after only 2 years of PS jail, it running amazingly with big spec support, however a little too restrictive in gpu options and graphical settings.

God of War: Ragnarök from Santa Monica Studio and Sony Interactive Entertainment is a beast on the PC. It brings the same big adventure while sporting some of the most gorgeous graphics the game has shown to date.

If you've never experienced God of War Ragnarok, then there's a strong chance this port will satisfy you after the extended wait. It's just a pity it's not flawless as the extra time might have you expecting.

God of War: Ragnarok on PC is yet another confirmation that expertly crafted ports are still possible, despite everything. Stunning graphics (though you’ll need a good PC to handle all that visual splendor), gameplay as sharp as Kratos’ blades, and a gripping narrative that won’t let you go. Sure, there was the risk of relying on past success and resting on its laurels. It doesn’t revolutionize, nor does it need to: it perfects what already worked on the PlayStation platform, with a confidence t

Jetpack Interactive returns to the scene with a porting of God of War Ragnarok that can enhance Santa Monica's title even more, if possible. The epic of Kratos and Atreus in desktop version improves the quality of the original production and, without overturning the technical layout, boosts the game's solidity, delivering an even more mature and complete product. If you have never experienced Fimbulwinter or are planning to resume the adventures in the nine realms, God of War Ragnarok for PC is

God of War: Ragnarök is, for many, one of the best games available on PS5 and now comes to PC in exquisite quality. It's an almost perfect port, except for some minor details that will surely be corrected with successive patches. If you haven't tried it and you have a half-decent PC, do it as soon as possible, you'll love it and you'll also enjoy the best version available.

God of War Ragnarok will give PC gamers their money's worth and then some. It's a lengthy adventure and one of Kratos' greatest outings to date. Still, there are some pacing issues and the PSN account requirement is a disappointment. It's unlikely that Sony is going to let-up on the latter, so PC gamers that want to enjoy the company's games without buying a PS5 should keep that in mind.
A 9/10 game let down by a shoddy port to PC.

God of War Ragnarok shines on PC. Packed with all the graphical bells and whistles you’d hope for in 2024, alongside the game’s brilliant Valhalla mode, there’s no doubt this is the definitive way to experience Kratos’ final Norse outing.

The cracks in its facade are stark because it’s otherwise such an incredibly vivid work, and the life breathed into it by its animators, artists, and actors is potent enough to survive some deeply odd writing and tonal choices. There’s a wonderful story, I’m sure, to be told about Kratos’s journey from destroyer to conciliator - the glimpses at mythological wonders that are allowed to exist in his presence without getting suplexed into paste here are stunning - this one just feels like it skippe

Keeping with PlayStation Studios' tradition, the PC version of Ragnarok is the definitive way to play and enjoy the epic adventure, regardless of whether you have a beefy high-end gaming rig, or your primary PC is your Steam Deck.

God of War Ragnarök is an amazing achievement and it remains a must-play game for fans of action-RPGs. The port to PC is a model of technical polish, smart changes, and generous content. If you haven’t played God of War Ragnarök, and have a moderately powerful PC, it’s time. If you’ve played it on PS5 and have a good to high-end rig, playing it on PC is not only worth your time, but an even more impressive experience than on the console.
Even on PC and with mouse and keyboard, Ragnarök is an action-packed thriller with an insanely captivating story.
Even if you’ve already played the game to 100% on PS5, you’ll likely get something out of witnessing God of War Ragnarok again in all its glory on a PC.

Aside from a couple of hitches that don’t significantly alter the quality of the production, the PC version of God of War Ragnarok shows up to the gamers’ appointment in great shape, ready to drag the masses through the snows of Fimbulwinter. From a performance point of view, we’re talking about a product capable of adapting very well to a wide range of configurations, thanks to the precautions taken by Santa Monica already at the time of the first release. Although the gap between the PS5 and P

One of the best PlayStation games of recent years gets an equally good port: God of War Ragnarök is also a hit on the PC and plays more smoothly than ever - countless settings make it possible. An almost perfect port.
God of War Ragnarök continues Jetpack Interactives' excellent porting efforts to provide PC players with the definitive version of the game.

God of War Ragnarök – A Story About Letting Go, Love, and Growth Sometimes you play a game at the wrong moment. That was the case for me with God of War Ragnarök. A year and a half ago, I started playing it, full of expectations after the fantastic first game. But somehow, it did not click. The combat did not feel quite right, the story did not grab me as much as I had hoped, and halfway through I stopped playing. 확실한건 전작 갓오브워 했던 사람들은 실망이 컸을 것. 전작은 정말 완성도 때문에 더 즐거움이 컸는데 그쪽은 내다버리고 출시한 느낌. 스토리 라인이나 이런건 전작 만든 사람들이 다 완성한 느낌이고 그 사람들 다 나가고 역량 없는 사람들이 살 채워서 내보낸 거 같은 느낌. 추가) 버그는 또 얼마나 많은지 클리어까지 거의 30번은 튕긴듯. AAA게임 중에 이정도로 대충 만들고 낸건 첨봄.

전작보다 많이 아쉬움. 몹들의 패턴이 더 악랄해져서 전작의 그 시원시원한 전투는 사라졌음. 스토리도 연출과 내용은 좋지만 개연성은 별로. 풀어가는 방식이 너무 가족 중심적으로 바뀜. GOW 스토리만의 장점을 죽이고 차기작만을 위해서 짜 맞추는 느낌. 판만 벌려 놓고 정작 개연성을 챙기지 못했음. pc판 오류인지 모르겠는데 특정 부분에서 버벅이거나 깜빡거리는 현상이 자주 있음. 잔버그가 좀 많음. 퍼즐 자체는 그렇게 안 어려운데 상호작용 가능 물체를 알아보기 어렵게 만듦. 틀린 그림 찾기 하는 기분임. 솔직히 평작보다 조금 나은 정도임.
"넌 잔혹하고, 오만하며, 이기적이다. 하지만 넌 그 이상의 존재다. 너는 늘 다른 이들이 생각하던 것 이상의 존재였다. 너는 그 이상의 존재다" 우린 더 나아져야 합니다. 의무가 아닙니다. 크레토스의 이명같은 거죠. 희망입니다. 우린 더 나아질 수 있을 것입니다. [b] 우린 더 나아질 겁니다. [/b]
갓오브워 북유럽 신화 시리즈의 아름다운 마무리 가족이라는 키워드로 전작과 함께 뛰어난 스토리 진행을 보여준다. 각각 매력있는 캐릭터들의 서사가 쌓일 수록 경험의 가치는 높아졌다. 크래토스의 여러 의미로써의 성장이 울림을 준다
타격감 높은 전투는 재미있고, 그래픽, 듀얼센스 모션센스 등의 조작감 등은 너무 좋았습니다. 다만, 길찾기는 너무 짜증이나고, 반복적으로 같은 길을 어려번 왔다리갔다리하는 것이 짜증 났고 퍼즐이 매인이요, 전투와 스토리는 후순위인 게임이라 이런 걸 감안하고 하세요. 바로, 스토리만 밀고 지웠습니다. 참, 스토리도 그닥.

훌륭한 게임이지만 전작에 비해 아쉬운 점이 많음 단점을 꼽자면 1. 스토리 진행이 좀 엉성함. 특히 후반부로 갈수록 얼렁뚱땅 넘기는 느낌 2. 퍼즐이 너무 과함. 퍼즐들이 전작 대비 직관성이 많이 떨어지고, 너무 덕지덕지 있어서 흐름을 끊는 느낌이었음. 3. 길이 너무 복잡함. 후반에 기존 맵을 새로 파밍 할 수단이 생기는데 길을 하도 꼬아놔서 재방문 시 길 찾기가 고역이었음. 거의 미로수준 4. 전작 대비 줄어든 타격감. 특히 혼돈의 블레이드 타격감이 심각함 5. PC 묻음. 앙그르보다 파트에서 좀 몰입하기가 힘들었음 하지만 전작에 비해 커진 맵과 늘어난 사이드 퀘스트 등 컨텐츠가 풍부해졌다는 장점이 있고, 여전히 연출도 탁월해서 특정 파트를 제외하면 몰입해서 아주 재밌게 플레이 할 수 있었음 스토리도 단점은 있었지만, 크레토스와 아트레우스 부자간의 서사가 훌륭하고 엔딩도 여운이 있어서 좋았음 맵 퀄리티도 뛰어나서 돌아다닐 때 풍경보는 재미도 있음

전작 갓오브워에서 아쉬웠던 적들의 종류도 늘고 맵탐방과 수집물 수집 현황을 확인하기 쉬워진 느낌이여서 수집물 수집하는 과정에 담긴 북유럽 신화와 크레토스의 그리스 썰과 미미르의 썰을 듣는 소소한 재미가 더욱 커져서 좋았다. 무기도 다양하고 무엇보다도 맵 배경과 그에 어울리는 웅장한 음악도 훌륭한게 괜히 메타크리틱 90이 아니구나 라는 생각이 컸다. 유일하게 아쉬운점이라면은 9개의 영역중에서 아스가르드나 요툰헤임이 중점으로 스토리가 흐를 줄 알았는데 생각보다 좀 많이 탐방을 하지 못해서 아쉽다. 그래도 dlc 발할라를 무료로 얹어줘서 크레토스의 그리스 시절 무기와 순수 파밍이아닌 전투만 담백하게 만든 로그라이트인데 분량도 짧지 않아서 즐기기 좋았다. 시원한 액션과 적당히 매콤한 전투난이도를 원하면 적극 추천하는 갓겜이다

The game felt like a powerful, emotional journey from start to finish. The story stayed intense the whole way through, especially watching Kratos and Atreus deal with fate, family, and tough decisions. Every fight felt smooth, brutal, and really satisfying. Thor stood out a lot, he first comes across as a terrifying god, but later shows a broken side under Odin’s control. Overall, it’s a gripping, emotional, and unforgettable experience that really stays with you.

title: Hitman World of Assassination
critic_response:
Hitman World of Assassination은 시리즈의 의 콘텐츠를 편리한 패키지로 제공합니다. 이 게임은 도전과 접근성의 혼합을 제공하는 Freelancer 모드를 특징으로 합니다. 그러나 한 리뷰는 콘텐츠가 제한되어 있고 온라인 요구 사항이 필요하다고 비판합니다. 또한 Freelancer 모드는 반복의 문제를 가지고 있지만, 핵심 게임 루프와 레벨 디자인은 흥미롭습니다.
장점: Hitman World of Assassination은 시리즈의 의 콘텐츠를 편리한 패키지로 제공합니다. / Freelancer 모드는 도전과 접근성의 혼합을 제공하는 흥미로운 모드입니다. / 핵심 게임 루프와 레벨 디자인은 흥미롭습니다.
단점: 콘텐츠가 제한되어 있고 온라인 요구 사항이 필요합니다.
critic_contexts: Hitman: World of Assassination brings together most of the trilogy’s content in a convenient package. While what’s excluded and the always online requirements are baffling, the game itself is still a ton of fun with the new Freelancer mode bringing you to locations from all three games. This is a great way to dive in for newcomers, though veterans will be confused as to what’s not included and why.

Hitman’s Freelancer mode is something rare: an intoxicating blend of challenge and approachability. It plays on the hubris of longtime players, but also guides newcomers with thematic objectives and a more explicit overall structure. It may not allow for the micro-repetition that makes the base trilogy tick. But it does maintain a rapid momentum from the beginning of each run to its bitter, comical end. After so many hours spent with this trilogy, combing each of its locations for something, any

With any roguelike game, the biggest problem is repetition. Hitman: Freelancer is in a strange place when it comes to this; the core gameplay loop and these levels have, in some cases, been around since 2016, and repetition is built into the game's framework. If someone has yet to play the recent entries, and with all three previous games wrapped up in one package, Hitman: World of Assassination is an incredible deal. As a free update for players who already own Hitman 3, it's absolutely worth j

While Freelancer Mode introduces a lot of new elements to the table, it also brings a myriad of bugs and issues that can take away from the overall experience. The freelancer mode is by no means bad, but it definitely has issues executing its concept due to its flawed RNG and mechanics.
user_response:
Hitman World of Assassination은 잠입 암살 게임으로, 공식 한글 패치가 없어 아쉽다는 의견이 있습니다. 다만, 구매 과정이 복잡하고 정리가 안 돼 있어 구매 가이드가 필요하다는 의견도 있습니다. 하지만, 한 리뷰는 게임의 진행 장벽이 어려울 수 있으며, CQB가 되어버리는 경우도 있습니다. Hitman World of Assassination은 잠입 액션 게임으로, 에이전트47이 되어 전 세계를 무대로 비밀 결사 프로비던스를 추적하고 타겟을 암살하는 과정을 다룹니다. Hitman World of Assassination은 탐험 콘텐츠를 제공하며, 플레이어는 자신의 취향에 맞게 게임을 즐길 수 있습니다.
장점: 다양한 루트가 존재해 루트대로 깨는 것이 더 재미있다는 의견도 있습니다. / 핵심 게임플레이가 매우 만족스럽습니다.
단점: 구매 과정이 복잡하고 정리가 안 돼 있어 구매 가이드가 필요하다는 의견도 있습니다. / CQB가 되어버리는 경우도 있습니다.

[h1]돈에 환장한 IOI의 상술에 가려진 훌륭한 잠입 액션[/h1] HITMAN WoA는 전설적인 암살자 에이전트47이 되어 전 세계를 무대로 비밀 결사 프로비던스를 추적하고 타겟을 암살하는 과정을 다룹니다. 기존 3부작을 통합한 본작을 플레이한 후 지극히 주관적인 관점에서 장단점 리뷰를 작성해 보려 합니다. [*] 초심자를 위한 배려: 다소 복잡할 수 있는 잠입과 암살 시스템을 튜토리얼을 통해 초심자들도 쉽게 적응할 수 있도록 보다 잘 다듬었다. [*] 압도적인 볼륨과 리플레이성: 변장, 사고 위장 등 다양한 루트로 타겟을 암살하는 자유도가 매우 우수하다. 로그라이크 요소를 결합한 프리랜서 모드 등 지속적인 라이브 콘텐츠 업데이트로 파고들 요소가 넘

히트맨 이거 정말 요물입니다. 게임 구매 자체가 뭘 사야 하는지 개씹잣같고 정리가 안돼 있어서 그렇지 일단 구매(아니 구매에 왜 가이드가 필요한 건지 아직도 모르겠지만.) 설치와 한글화만 제대로 하고 나면 잠입 암살류에 이만한 게임이 없습니다. 게임 진행 자체가 한 판 씩 해도 전혀 문제되지 않아서 직장인이 긴 호흡 스토리 부시는 게임에 심신이 지칠 때 이런거 하면 아주 심플하고 수면장애가 생기지 않아서 좋습니다. 그리고 트릴로지로 개편되면서 ui가 확실히 바뀐건지 아니면 단순 느낌인지는 모르겠으나 일전 그냥 히트맨1 했을때 보다 게임 내에서는 조금 더 친절해진 느낌을 받았습니다. (구매 통일화랑 한글화도 제발 좀!) 그리고 정말 존귀한 분들이 친절하게 딸깍 갓글화 해주셔서 매우 쾌적하게 플레이 중이라 다시 한 번 감사 드립니다.

title: Dragon Age: Inquisition
critic_response:
드래곤 에이지: 인퀴지션은 비오웨어의 대표작 중 하나로 평가되며, 높은 수준의 스토리텔링, 그래픽스, 사운드 디자인으로 호평을 받았습니다. 또한, 이 게임은 비오웨어의 가장 완성도 높은 작품 중 하나로 평가되며, 2014년 최고의 RPG 중 하나로 꼽혔습니다. 그러나 한 리뷰는 게임의 일부 요소가 과도하게 사용되었다고 비판했습니다.
장점: 또한, 이 게임은 비오웨어의 가장 완성도 높은 작품 중 하나로 평가되며, 2014년 최고의 RPG 중 하나로 꼽혔습니다.
단점: 그러나 한 리뷰는 게임의 일부 요소가 과도하게 사용되었다고 비판했습니다. / 또한, 한 리뷰는 게임의 콘텐츠가 MMO와 유사하다고 비판했습니다.
critic_contexts: Best Dragon Age so far, hands down. Pity about the naff writing. [Issue#257, p.51]
The masters over at Bioware have done it again. This epic journey into the world of Thedas will steal more than 100 hours of your time, hours so full of superb gaming that you'll never forget them.
Despite numerous shortcomings, the new BioWare project is undoubtedly worth your attention. One of the best RPGs of 2014.
RPG of the year and a must-have for everyone who values good story delivered in a cinematic fashion and supported by outstanding sound and graphics. [13/2014, p.58]
The most ambitious game BioWare has ever made. [Jan 2014, p.104]
A game to spend the whole winter with. The breathtaking and complex story is set in a huge open world, and despite its partial mistakes the game works pretty well. [Issue#248]

As 2014 draws to a close, Inquisition is a contender not only for game of the year, but for the best title to come out of BioWare’s impressive stable - either way, it’s an essential purchase for any RPG fan.
At the very least BioWare managed to present a workable concept of an RPG which is completely detached from the legacy of pen-and-paper RPG. [Jan 2015, p.52]
The best of the Dragon Age series by far and one of BioWare's most accomplished games to boot. [Jan 2015, p.50]

Dragon Age: Inquisition is a weird amalgamation of two different games. One is almost like those classic RPGs from BioWare’s early years; it makes you sit on the edge of your chair, waiting for shocking twists, deliberate over moral dilemmas, laugh and cry. The other is a MMO with generic quests and soulless grinding, having slithered where it should not be. And, alas, there is no way to separate them.

Huge amount of content, many possible choices with distinct consequences, reappearance of beloved characters. Despite some obvious flaws and various shortcomings, Dragon Age: Inquisition certainly feels like a step in the right direction for the series.
Rubbish controls, dull combat, and a general sense that it's not quite sure what it wants to be, let down an otherwise entertaining and regularly amusing world-saving romp.
Inquisition is an excellent return to form after the claustrophobic, rushed Dragon Age 2, an exciting and sprawling colossus of a game that rewards the player at every turn.

Excellent RPG beats both of its predecessors and offers the unspoiled entertainment for hundreds of hours. The sophisticated world of Dragon Age finally comes alive in all aspects and incredible character and story detail fully compensates a bit overused plot about saving the world.
Inquisition is a good game that suffers from a lack of editorial judgment and polish.

Dragon Age: Inquisition is a frustrating game. I often found myself scratching and clawing through boring, MMO-like content for enough power points to progress to the next major plot event. However, the fact that I was willing to slog through it at all shows just how good this game can be when it hits its stride.
Despite some minor hiccups, Inquisition is absolutely the realest of deals, and should be missed at your own peril.

Dragon Age Inquisition is not a common RPG: it's the furious scream of Bioware, strong enough to make windows and walls rattle. It is a demonstration of skill and power that proves that the Canadian developer still knows how to put heart and brain into their projects.

For people looking to get lost in a detailed world brimming with political intrigue, warring factions, tough choices that affect the story and gobs of replayability that will last you well beyond Fall and into the new year, Dragon Age Inquisition is a must buy.

Dragon Age: Inquisition makes up for the way too short Dragon Age 2. The gameworld is huge and tremendously diverse. The franchise seems to go a bit less dark and intense, but it introduces a new blockbuster RPG standard that many developers will struggle to meet.
It’s a welcome return to form for a franchise that felt adrift after Dragon Age 2, and is easily recommendable to RPG fans who have a spare few weeks to dedicate to a single game.
From a content point of view is simply astonishing but the game has several problems with the combat system starting from the tactical pause (and view) to the action aspect of the fights.

I found it to be a jack-of-all-trades, but a master of none. Because of this, you’re left having your attention continually and equally divided, forcing you to pour in tons of hours just to start feeling invested in the world.
It's the only RPG you'll need this winter and finally a worthy heir to Origins, last generation's best role-playing game.

More playground than living breathing world. BioWare opens a gigantic fantasy theme park. But unfortunately the only thing that is epic is the amount of content. Story, character behavior, quests and minigames are a mere shadow of the studio's former glory.
BioWare has created a role-playing game which feels like a massive monument to our culture. Inquisition is an absolute blast to experience for one hour or 150.
Graphics, gameplay, scale, storytelling, depth, content and the finish: Dragon Age: Inquisition proves that it can bring each of these components together without making any concessions.

Dragon Age: Inquisition is the best installment in the series and one of the best games in BioWare's history. There's a lot to praise: an interesting story, cool characters, big world, Skyhold. Sure, there are some minor issues and the combat system isn't perfect but the scale and attention to detail make all of that seem unimportant. An excellent game indeed.

Inquisition offers tons of rpg-gameplay-elements. The classic high-fantasy-setting combines action- and tactical combat, rich characters with lots of dialogue-options. On top you’ll find yourself as a leader of the inquisition-faction who has to build up a majestic stronghold. Graphics and sound are great, but technical issues like glitches and bugs with animations are also part of the game. Menus are not optimized for PC and the skill-system is highly focused on combat.
Inquisition gets under your skull like red lyrium.

It’s a great game from start to finish, one that lets you do just about whatever you want, and then rewards you for just about all of it. While it does have some minor problems, they don’t detract from the quality of the whole. The way the world responds to, and builds itself around, your character makes Inquisition a must-play for any RPG enthusiast.

Dragon Age: Inquisition is still a brilliant if flawed gem from one of the best storytellers in the genre. The journey is long. The path is fraught with dangers and more than a few missteps, but it’s an epic adventure well worth the undertaking.

It’s one of the most overwhelming experiences on the market, containing not only the best character development found in any RPG, but an open world that’s actually polished. The artistic and visual fidelity help with the immersion, but it’s the branching, player driven storyline and exquisitely layered combat system that create something special.

Can only be described as Bioware’s return to glory and as the preeminent force in the RPG genre. Dragon Age Inquisition is a must play for anyone who calls themselves a fan of role playing games. Dragon Age Inquisition, when or if it is finally completed will leave players with the overall sense that this game will replace a favorite movie and a beloved book as a source of contemplation for a long, long time.

Not only one of the most expansive RPGs I’ve ever played, but one of the few that successfully fills its gorgeous, massive world with meaningful things to do and see. A frustratingly vague plot and typical BioWare bugginess drag it down a bit, but both in combat and out, Inquisition marks a welcome return to the RPG depth that made Dragon Age: Origins and Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic so magnetic.

How much you enjoy Inquisition will likely depend on what it is you enjoy about RPGs. If you want complex systems and hardcore challenge, it could potentially disappoint. I don’t. I want a rich world, interesting characters, and a dramatic and memorable plot. Judged on those criteria, Dragon Age: Inquisition sits happily alongside BioWare’s best.

Its not-so-fatal flaw is that in offering so much, both in terms of player choice and in going for peak-BioWare in every aspect of the game, those individual moments, characters, activities and plot beats often don't benefit from the focus and importance needed to unlock their full potential. Still, that's hardly a crime, and one more than made up for by the many high points

The biggest criticism that can be leveled at the title is that it is a little too conservative in its approach to the role-playing and action genres and fails to introduce something entirely new.At the same time, the developers have managed to carefully polish the core tenets of Dragon Age and fans of the series will be happy to discover how easy it is to tailor Inquisition to their own play style.

An epic experience that haunts you between play sessions, enticing you to come back for more. It may not hit the lofty heights of Skyrim, but it comes bloody close and, unlike its most obvious comparison, boasts consistently fantastic characters, a memorable and compelling storyline, and breathtaking visual fidelity out of the box.

The brilliant part of this concept is how it imbues every activity with purpose. The world of Inquisition is immense, and a freeform structure means everything you accomplish, no matter how small, feeds your larger aspirations.

BioWare studio wanted to redeem itself for the not-so-convincing Dragon Age II. This episode is one of its finest productions. Amazing environments that take hours to get explored, greats quests with some hard decisions to make, fantastic characters and a great combat system: Dragon Age :Inquisition will probably become a masterpiece for anyone who's been in love with Dungeons & Dragons or Tolkien's books for a long time.
Yes, Inquisition is an all around excellent game.A beautiful fantastic world, a great roleplaying game - but not as good as Dragon Age: Origins
The first Dragon Age still is the best game of the franchise, but we've really enjoyed this one. Fans of the genre won't be disappointed.

Dragon Age: Inquisition is BioWare's new masterpiece and they weren't bluffing. The RPG has everything a fan of the genre could wish for: an amazing amount of gameplay, a great story and extreme beauty. A must-have for everyone that calls himself a gamer.

Bioware is back with sheer passion and love for its work. It demonstrates so offering a new way for players to enjoy strategic RPGs, with a battle system that merges action and tactics, and it plays smoothly whether on PC or consoles. Add to this an incredible amount of gameplay, free roaming and an interesting intrigue, and what you get is one of the best games of this year.
user_response:
드래곤 에이지: 인퀴지션은 방대한 콘텐츠와 다양한 캐릭터를 제공하여 플레이어의 몰입감을 높였다는 평가를 받았다. 65시간을 플레이한 한 유저는 게임의 방대한 세계를 탐험하며 즐거운 경험을 했다고 말했다. 게임의 조작감과 UI는 일부 유저들에게 비판을 받았다. 컨트롤이 불편하고 메뉴가 복잡하다는 의견이 있었다. 버그 문제는 게임의 진행을 방해하는 요소로 작용했다. 한 유저는 버그로 인해 게임의 진행이 끊기고 답답함을 느꼈다고 말했다. 패드 지원을 통해 게임의 접근성이 높아졌다는 평가도 있었다. 드래곤 에이지 시리즈 중 오리진 이후 두 번째로 플레이한 유저는 패드 지원을 통해 더 재미있게 플레이할 수 있었다고 말했다. 게임의 그래픽은 평균 수준이라는 평가를 받았다.
장점: 다양한 캐릭터와 방대한 콘텐츠로 몰입감이 높은 게임이다. / 패드 지원을 통해 접근성이 높아졌다.
단점: 버그로 인해 게임 진행이 끊길 수 있다. / 반복적인 플레이로 인해 지루할 수 있다.

드래곤 에이지 오리진 부터 최근 출시된 베일가드까지 모든 시리즈의 엔딩을 본 유저입니다. 드에 유저 대부분 오리진을 최고의 작품으로 여깁니다만 전 인퀴지션을 오리진 보다 더 높게 평가합니다. 이렇게 평가 할수 있는데엔 한가지 전제가 붙습니다. <모든 시리즈를 즐길 수 있는가> 또는 <즐겼는가> 입니다. 드에의 세계관은 매우 촘촘합니다. 최근 RPG게임에 익숙한 유저분들이 이 작품에 입문하신다면 클래스 선택 부분에서 부터 의문 부호를 가지시게 될겁니다. 기상천외한 클래스가 난무하는 현세대 게임에 전 도 마법사 단 세가지 라니. 이 시리즈에 장점은 클래스가 아니에요. 굳이 비유를 하자면 클래스는 음식이 내 앞에 펼쳐졌는데 이걸 수저로 먹을지 젓가락으로 먹을지 포크로 먹을지를 선택하는 정도로 보시면 됩니다. 그보다 중요한 건 여러분의 선택과 탐험입니다. 게임에서 조차 알아보고 공부해야 하나 하시는 분들에겐 정말 맞지 않을 정도로 당신이 세계관에 빠져드는 만큼 혹은 빠져들수 있을 만큼 인퀴

It's rare for me to find a game with a gameplay loop so soul-sucking that I have to tap out, but Bioware managed it. The gameplay is so bad I had to reevaluate my opinions on a number of other cRPGs I had previously disliked.

뭐랄까 너무 작위적이고 죽어있는채로 보여주기만 하는 세계 마치 놀이공원에 입장했는데 눈에 빛이 없고 무표정한 직원들이 손을 흔들면서 환영해주는걸 본 기분 같음 공포게임이라면 그런 기괴함을 높게 평가하겠지만 rpg라면 그렇게 하지 못하겠음 일단 대폭발의 유일한 생존자고 교황시해의 범인으로 몰리던 pc가 왜 프롤로그 이후 갑자기 인퀴지션 내부의 대단한 인물로 대우받는지 부터가 뭐지..? 인 상황. 프롤로그 막바지의 환영을 다 믿는다고 치면 교황시해의 범인은 아닐 수 있었다는게 드러날수는 있어도 왼손의 표식이 균열과 반응을 한다는점에서 대균열을 일으킨 장본인이거나 관계자 용의선상에서 내려올수가 없는 인물 같은데 왜 갑자기 새로 발족한 인퀴지션 내부의 지휘관이나 참모들이 우린 널 믿어요 ㅎㅎ 하며 그들과 비견될정도의 중요인물 취급받는건지 솔직히 납득이 가지 않음 pc가 그래도 중요한 표식을 가졌으니 인퀴지션 내부에서 일해라 하면서 나름 자율성을 받고 조그만한 분대의 분대장으로 임명받은것도

재미있음 근데 1이나 2를 안해서.스토리 이해하는데 좀 오래걸려요. 나무위키로 공부하고 오는것도 괜찮을거같음. 액션은 좋음.스킬도 다채롭고. 세일할때 사서 70시간 매우 알차게 했으요 동료들도 다들 매력있고 선택지 다양한것도 재미있어요 다만 길찾기는 좀 짜증나요.방향이나 이런거 알려주면 좋으련만 지도도 보기 어렵고 키보드 마우스로 전투하기도 좀 빡십니다ㅎㅎㅎㅎㅎㅎ 그래도 보통따리로 하면 별 제한사항은 없을듯! 이번에 1회차 했고 2회차 할일이 있을거같은데 그때는 전사말고 법사나 궁수 키워보려구요 성기사 루트말고 법사 루트로 여튼 추천함!

오리진이나 2에 비해 스토리가 조금 아쉽긴 하지만 그래도 만족스러웠다. 오리진과 2의 캐릭터와 이어지던 스토리가 유의미하게 영향을 주고 신캐릭터가 나오면 기존의 캐릭터를 너프 시키는 경우가 많은데 그렇지 않고 존중하는 모습이 보여서 뭔가 본인의 팬을 무시하지 않는 게임이라는 생각이 들었다. 게임을 계속 하면서도 아 조금 아쉬운데 씁 좀 아쉽네를 반복했지만 그래도 재미있게 했다. 연동을 이 정도 시키는 것도 고맙게 느껴짐. 그리고 좆에이는 드래곤 에이지 ip 팔고 좀 뒤져라

긴 대장정이 끝나고 마침내 엔딩을 봤습니다. 게임을 약 2시간 정도 플레이한 후 저는 이 게임이 어떻게 GOTY를 받았는지 의문을 갖지 않을 수 없었습니다. 그만큼 불편한 점이 산더미처럼 쌓여있었으며 혁신적인 요소도 찾아볼 수 없었습니다. 보통 GOTY를 받았다면 불편함을 감수하고도 게임을 즐겨야만 하는 강점을 가지고 있었으나 이 게임은 그렇지 않습니다. 오히려 이도저도 아닌 어중간한 포지션에 끼어 있는 게임입니다. 게임의 엔딩을 보고 난 후 여전히 GOTY급 게임인지에 대해선 의문이 가득하지만 게임에 내세우는 재미 정도는 확실하게 있다는 생각으로 바뀌었습니다. 세미 오픈월드는 짧고 멍청한 서브퀘스트와 잡일, 반복 임무로 채워져 있고 조작감은 구리며 어색한 모션에 전투는 미묘합니다. 그럼에도 스토리는 꽤 재밌는 편에 속합니다. 명작이라고 칭송할 수준의 것은 아니지만 상황에 몰입하며 이 세계를 알아가고 싶다는 열망이 생길 정도로 괜찮은 이야기를 담고 있습니다. 물론 많은 고유명사와 캐

모드 매니져에 한글패치 적용해서 모드로 플레이 실행해서 하는중. 게임 패드 인식이 안되서 키마로 하다가, 게임 설정에서 게임 패드로 바꿔줘야 되더라. 게임패드로 하는게 더 재밌음. 1.첫 번째 보스 잡을 때 그냥 체력 돼지라 좋같았음 2.다른 캐릭터들도 조종할 수 있는데, 인물들이랑 대화할 때 주인공 목소리가 나와서 몰입 안되서 걍 주인공만 쓰게 됌. 전술 화면에 들어가서 애들 명령내리는것도 걍 하기 귀찮음, 게임 소개에서도 하기 귀찮으면 걍 하나만 조종하라니까 대충 캐릭터 전술 설정만 함. 3.와우같은 붕쯔붕쯔한 액션이지만 나름 레트로한 매력이랑 여럿이서 다니니까 안외롭고 분위기 괜찮음. 4.오픈월드는 꽤 잘만들었음 B급 반지의제왕 느낌 5.초반 테이젠의 기록물인가 뭔가 찾으려면 나침반 울릴 때 몇 번 눌러야함. 6.옛날게임 티 많이나고, 온갖 고유명사가 판치는 판타지세계긴 한데 그냥 하이판타지 느낌으로 이해하면될듯. 아이템 장착하는 것도 서툴고, 이렇게 하는게 맞나 하면서 계속

[h1] 버그가 망쳐버린 대작 [/h1] 이라는 한 마디로 표현하고 싶습니다. 제가 왠만해서는 버그 가지고 심각할 정도로 뭐라고 하진 않습니다. 보통 한 번 정도 일어날 해프닝이기도 하고, 게임 전반으로 영향을 주는 버그가 아닌 이상 그렇게 자주 터지는 편도 아니니까요. 근데 얘는 선을 좀 많이 넘었습니다. 게임 진행하면서 치명적인 버그가 2개씩이나 있었고, 결국 침입자 DLC 하다가 하차했습니다. 제 억울함을 한 번 들어주시죠. 하나는 게임을 할 때마다 로딩 후 짧으면 5분, 길면 2시간 단위로 게임과 그래픽카드가 충돌이 나며 게임이 멈춥니다. 아니 사실 말이 글카와 충돌이 난다는 거지 아직까지도 원인이 뭔지를 모르겠습니다. 글카나 드라이버도 꿀릴 정도의 사양이나 버전은 아니고,(9070XT) 멈출 때마다 DirectX나 모드매니저 둘 중 하나가 계속 튕기고 심하면 게임때문에 글카가 멈추는 일도 있었습니다. 찾아봤더니 버그를 겪은 사람이 적지 않고 해결 방법도 없으니, 너무 자주

DLC 깨면 주는 무기들이 워낙 좋아서 굳이 개조할 필요성을 못느낌. 드래곤 에이지 시리즈의 신기한 점은, 몰입감이 매스 이펙트 시리즈처럼 높은 것도, 또 극도의 재미가 있는 것도 아닌데 계속 붙잡고 있게 만든다는 점. 개인적으로 중세 판타지를 그리 좋아하지 않는데, 그래도 100시간 넘게

드래곤 에이지 오리진에서부터 이어진 드래곤 에이지 킵 연동 시스템을 통해 전작 속 내가 골랐던 선택지들이 서로 유기적으로 이어져 도래한 결과를 인퀴지션에서 후세의 주인공의 시선에서 바라볼 수 있어서 즐거웠던 게임입니다. 전작에 등장했던 동료들이 언급되는게 얼마나 반갑던지. 이번작에서 주인공인 심문관을 영웅으로 쌓아올리는 서사, 그리고 그 서사를 몰입감있게 전달하는 연출이 플레이하면서 정말 벅차올랐어요. 그리고 제가 스토리 중심에 로맨스 한숟갈 곁다리 식으로 들어간 게임 진짜 없어서 못 먹는데, 아 진짜 여기서 인생캐 만날 줄은 누가 알았겠어요. 솔라스 동료로던, 로맨스 상대로던 진짜 한숟갈만 서사 드셔보세요 진짜 얘 생각하면 미칠 거 같음. 솔라스 진짜 미친놈임(긍정적)

✅ Yes — I'd recommend Dragon Age: Inquisition. I've sunk about 10 hours into it so far and I'm genuinely hooked. Combat feels really good (I'm playing as a two-handed warrior on Normal). The a

The debates around Dragon Age: Inquisition still haven’t died down. Some people see it as an empty game that lost the spirit of old-school Dragon Age, while for others it’s the best entry in the series. Personally, I’m somewhere in the middle. My introduction to the BioWare franchise was actually pretty unusual — I started with Dragon Age 2. I loved it so much that I immediately went on to play Origins, and a couple of years later Inquisition came out. Unfortunately, I couldn’t play it at launch
